    'Mature': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Media': 'Media | Books',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office | Supplies',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Software': 'Software | Video Games',
    'Sporting Goods': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys | Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
}


# ============================================================
# STEP 5: Build unified levels from source paths
# ============================================================
def get_source_parts(path):
    \"\"\"Split a category path into parts.\"\"\"
    return [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]


def build_unified_levels(df):
    \"\"\"Assign unified_level_1 through unified_level_5 for each row.\"\"\"
    records = []
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        source = row['source']
        path = row['category_path']
        depth = row['depth']
        parts = get_source_parts(path)
        
        # L1: from mapping
        source_l1 = parts[0]
        unified_l1 = L1_MAPPING.get(source_l1, 'Other')
        
        # L2-L5: normalize source parts at those levels
        levels = [unified_l1]
        for i in range(1, min(depth, 5)):
            name = normalize_name(parts[i])
            levels.append(name)
        
        # Pad to 5 levels
        while len(levels) < 5:
            levels.append(None)
        
        records.append({
            'source': source,
            'category_path': path,
            'depth': depth,
            'unified_level_1': levels[0],
            'unified_level_2': levels[1],
            'unified_level_3': levels[2],
            'unified_level_4': levels[3],
            'unified_level_5': levels[4],
        })
    
    return pd.DataFrame(records)


# ============================================================
# STEP 6: Post-processing - enforce all constraints
# ============================================================

def get_content_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get content words from a category name (split on | and spaces).\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    # Split on | separator and spaces
    words = re.split(r'[|\\s]+', name.lower())
    # Filter out stop words and short words
    stop_words = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'and', 'or', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'with', 'by', '&', ',', '-', ''}
    return {w.strip() for w in words if w.strip() and w.strip() not in stop_words}


def exclude_parent_words(child_name, parent_name):
    \"\"\"Remove parent content words from child name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name) or pd.isna(parent_name):
        return child_name
    
    parent_words = get_content_words(parent_name)
    if not parent_words:
        return child_name
    
    # Split child name on ' | '
    child_parts = [p.strip() for p in child_name.split(' | ')]
    filtered_parts = []
    for part in child_parts:
        # Check each word in this part
        part_words = part.split()
        kept_words = [w for w in part_words if w.lower() not in parent_words]
        if kept_words:
            filtered_parts.append(' '.join(kept_words))
    
    if filtered_parts:
        result = ' | '.join(filtered_parts)
    else:
        # If all words removed, keep original but add a distinguishing word
        result = child_name
    
    return result


def compute_sibling_overlap(name1, name2):
    \"\"\"Compute word overlap ratio between two sibling names.\"\"\"
    words1 = get_content_words(name1)
    words2 = get_content_words(name2)
    if not words1 or not words2:
        return 0.0
    overlap = len(words1 & words2)
    return overlap / min(len(words1), len(words2))


def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure child category names don't contain parent words.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        
        mask = df[col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            # Also need to consider all ancestor levels
            for _, idx in enumerate(df[mask].index):
                child_name = df.at[idx, col]
                # Collect all ancestor words
                all_parent_words = set()
                for plevel in range(1, level):
                    pcol = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
                    pname = df.at[idx, pcol]
                    if pd.notna(pname):
                        all_parent_words.update(get_content_words(pname))
                
                if not all_parent_words:
                    continue
                
                # Filter child name
                child_parts = [p.strip() for p in child_name.split(' | ')]
                filtered_parts = []
                for part in child_parts:
                    part_words = part.split()
                    kept_words = [w for w in part_words if w.lower() not in all_parent_words]
                    if kept_words:
                        filtered_parts.append(' '.join(kept_words))
                
                if filtered_parts:
                    new_name = ' | '.join(filtered_parts[:5])
                    df.at[idx, col] = new_name
                # If all words removed, keep original (edge case)
    
    return df


def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        
        if level == 1:
            # All L1 categories are siblings
            parent_groups = [('root', df[df[col].notna()].index)]
        else:
            parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
            # Group by parent path
            groups = {}
            for idx, row in df[df[col].notna()].iterrows():
                parent_key = tuple(row[f'unified_level_{i}'] for i in range(1, level))
                if parent_key not in groups:
                    groups[parent_key] = []
                groups[parent_key].append(idx)
            parent_groups = groups.items()
        
        for parent_key, indices in parent_groups:
            # Get unique names at this level for this parent
            names = df.loc[indices, col].dropna().unique().tolist()
            
            # Check pairwise overlap and rename if needed
            name_map = {}
            for i, n1 in enumerate(names):
                for j, n2 in enumerate(names):
                    if i >= j:
                        continue
                    overlap = compute_sibling_overlap(n1, n2)
                    if overlap >= 0.3:
                        # Merge the less common one into the more common
                        count1 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            # Rename n2 to differentiate
                            name_map[n2] = differentiate_name(n2, n1)
                        else:
                            name_map[n1] = differentiate_name(n1, n2)
            
            # Apply renames
            if name_map:
                for idx in indices:
                    val = df.at[idx, col]
                    if val in name_map:
                        df.at[idx, col] = name_map[val]
    
    return df


def differentiate_name(name_to_change, name_to_avoid):
    \"\"\"Make name_to_change more distinct from name_to_avoid.\"\"\"
    words_avoid = get_content_words(name_to_avoid)
    parts = [p.strip() for p in name_to_change.split(' | ')]
    
    # Keep only parts whose words don't overlap with avoid
    filtered = []
    for part in parts:
        part_words = set(part.lower().split())
        if not part_words.intersection(words_avoid):
            filtered.append(part)
    
    if filtered:
        return ' | '.join(filtered[:5])
    # If everything overlaps, keep first part only
    return parts[0] if parts else name_to_change


def enforce_children_count(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure 3-20 children per parent at each level.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        subset = df[mask].copy()
        
        # Group by parent path
        groups = subset.groupby(parent_cols)[col].apply(lambda x: x.unique().tolist())
        
        for parent_key, children in groups.items():
            if not isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
                parent_key = (parent_key,)
            
            num_children = len(children)
            
            if num_children > 20:
                # Merge least common children
                # Count occurrences
                parent_mask = pd.Series(True, index=df.index)
                for i, pcol in enumerate(parent_cols):
                    parent_mask &= (df[pcol] == parent_key[i])
                parent_mask &= df[col].notna()
                
                child_counts = df[parent_mask].groupby(col).size().sort_values(ascending=True)
                
                # Merge smallest children into \"Other\" or combine them
                children_to_merge = child_counts.index[:num_children - 19].tolist()
                if children_to_merge:
                    # Find the most common word among merged children for a name
                    all_words = []
                    for c in children_to_merge:
                        all_words.extend(get_content_words(c))
                    
                    if all_words:
                        word_freq = Counter(all_words)
                        # Exclude parent words
                        parent_words = set()
                        for pk in parent_key:
                            parent_words.update(get_content_words(pk))
                        
                        top_words = [w for w, _ in word_freq.most_common(5) if w not in parent_words]
                        if top_words:
                            merged_name = make_unified_name(top_words[:3])
                        else:
                            merged_name = 'Assorted | Items'
                    else:
                        merged_name = 'Assorted | Items'
                    
                    # Apply merge
                    for idx in df[parent_mask].index:
                        if df.at[idx, col] in children_to_merge:
                            df.at[idx, col] = merged_name
    
    return df


def final_name_cleanup(df):
    \"\"\"Final cleanup of all unified level names.\"\"\"
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(clean_unified_name)
    return df


def clean_unified_name(name):
    \"\"\"Clean a unified category name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return name
    
    name = str(name).strip()
    
    # Unidecode
    name = unidecode(name)
    
    # Split on ' | ', clean each part
    parts = name.split(' | ')
    cleaned_parts = []
    for part in parts:
        # Remove special characters - keep only alphanumeric, spaces, ampersand, hyphen, comma
        part = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s&,\\-]', ' ', part)
        part = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', part).strip()
        part = part.title()
        if part:
            cleaned_parts.append(part)
    
    if not cleaned_parts:
        return name
    
    # Limit to 5 parts
    cleaned_parts = cleaned_parts[:5]
    
    result = ' | '.join(cleaned_parts)
    return result


# ============================================================
# STEP 7: Build hierarchy file
# ============================================================
def build_hierarchy(df):
    \"\"\"Build hierarchy CSV with all unique paths including intermediates.\"\"\"
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row[f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and val:
                levels.append(val)
            else:
                break
        
        # Add all prefix paths
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path_tuple = tuple(levels[:depth]) + (None,) * (5 - depth)
            hierarchy_rows.add(path_tuple)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    
    # Sort for readability
    sort_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(sort_cols, na_position='last').reset_index(drop=True)
    
    # Remove duplicates
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    
    return hier_df


# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================
def main():
    print(\"Loading sources...\")
    df = load_sources()
    print(f\"  Total raw records: {len(df)}\")
    print(f\"  Sources: {df['source'].value_counts().to_dict()}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_and_compute_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After truncation: {len(df)}\")
    print(f\"  Depth distribution: {df['depth'].value_counts().sort_index().to_dict()}\")
    
    print(\"Building unified levels...\")
    full_df = build_unified_levels(df)
    
    print(\"Cleaning names...\")
    full_df = final_name_cleanup(full_df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    full_df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    full_df = enforce_children_count(full_df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    full_df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(full_df)
    
    # Second pass of cleanup after modifications
    print(\"Final cleanup pass...\")
    full_df = final_name_cleanup(full_df)
    full_df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    
    print(\"Building hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = build_hierarchy(full_df)
    
    # Validate
    print(\"\\n=== Validation ===\")
    l1_count = full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"L1 categories: {l1_count}\")
    print(f\"L1 names: {sorted(full_df['unified_level_1'].unique())}\")
    
    # Check children counts
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        mask = full_df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            children_counts = full_df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[col].nunique()
            print(f\"L{level} children per parent: min={children_counts.min()}, max={children_counts.max()}, mean={children_counts.mean():.1f}\")
    
    # Check source balance
    for source in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        count = (full_df['source'] == source).sum()
        print(f\"Source {source}: {count} records\")
    
    # Check special characters
    special_char_count = 0
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for val in full_df[col].dropna().unique():
            if re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s|&,\\-]', val):
                special_char_count += 1
                print(f\"  SPECIAL CHAR in {col}: '{val}'\")
    print(f\"Special char violations: {special_char_count}\")
    
    # Check parent word exclusion
    parent_word_violations = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for idx, row in full_df[full_df[col].notna()].iterrows():
            child_words = get_content_words(row[col])
            for plevel in range(1, level):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
                if pd.notna(row[pcol]):
                    parent_words = get_content_words(row[pcol])
                    overlap = child_words & parent_words
                    if overlap:
                        parent_word_violations += 1
                        if parent_word_violations <= 5:
                            print(f\"  PARENT WORD VIOLATION: L{level}='{row[col]}' contains words {overlap} from L{plevel}='{row[pcol]}'\")
                        break
    print(f\"Parent word violations: {parent_word_violations} / {len(full_df)}\")
    
    print(f\"\\nHierarchy rows: {len(hier_df)}\")
    
    # Save
    print(\"Saving outputs...\")
    full_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    
    print(\"Done"'!")


if __name__ == '"'__main__':
    main()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Build unified 5-level taxonomy from Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories.
Approach: deterministic mapping with rigorous constraint enforcement.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from unidecode import unidecode

DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ============================================================
# TEXT UTILITIES
# ============================================================
STOP_WORDS = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'and', 'or', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'with', 'by', '&', ',', '-', ''}

def clean_text(text):
    \"\"\"Full text normalization: unidecode, remove special chars, title case.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not text:
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = unidecode(text)
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s&,\\-]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    text = text.title()
    return text

def get_content_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get meaningful words from a category name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    words = re.split(r'[|\\s&,\\-]+', name.lower())
    return {w.strip() for w in words if w.strip() and w.strip() not in STOP_WORDS}

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    \"\"\"Compute word overlap ratio between two names.\"\"\"
    w1 = get_content_words(name1)
    w2 = get_content_words(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words that appear in any ancestor from child name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name) or not child_name:
        return child_name
    
    # Collect all ancestor words
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if pd.notna(aname) and aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(aname))
    
    if not ancestor_words:
        return child_name
    
    # Split on ' | '
    parts = [p.strip() for p in child_name.split(' | ')]
    filtered_parts = []
    for part in parts:
        words = part.split()
        kept = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
        if kept:
            filtered_parts.append(' '.join(kept))
    
    if filtered_parts:
        return ' | '.join(filtered_parts[:5])
    
    # All words removed - try to keep the most distinctive word
    all_child_words = []
    for part in parts:
        all_child_words.extend(part.split())
    
    # Return the longest word not in ancestors, or first word
    non_ancestor = [w for w in all_child_words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
    if non_ancestor:
        return non_ancestor[0]
    return all_child_words[0] if all_child_words else child_name


# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================
def load_sources():
    sources = {
        'amazon': 'amazon_product_categories.csv',
        'facebook': 'fb_product_categories.csv',
        'google': 'google_shopping_product_categories.csv',
    }
    frames = []
    for source, filename in sources.items():
        df = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, filename))
        df['source'] = source
        df = df[['source', 'category_path']].copy()
        df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].apply(
            lambda x: str(x).strip().strip('\"').strip('\\ufeff').strip()
        )
        frames.append(df)
    return pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)


def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    result = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        keep = []
        for p in sdf['category_path']:
            is_prefix = any(q.startswith(p + ' > ') for q in paths if q "'!= p)
            keep.append(not is_prefix)
        result.append(sdf[keep])
    return pd.concat(result, ignore_index=True)


def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=5):
    def trunc(path):
        parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split('"' > ')]
        return ' > '.join(parts[:max_depth])
    
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].apply(trunc)
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: len(x.split(' > ')))
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    return df


# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================
L1_MAP = {
    # Amazon
    'Appliances': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Automotive': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby | Toddler',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Books': 'Media | Books',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Media | Books',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food | Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Business | Industrial',
    'Movies & TV': 'Media | Books',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office | Supplies',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Garden | Outdoor Living',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Animals | Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Hardware | Home Improvement',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys | Games',
    'Video Games': 'Software | Video Games',
    # Facebook
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
    'baby products': 'Baby | Toddler',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'food & beverages': 'Food | Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health | Beauty',
    'home': 'Home | Appliances',
    'home improvement': 'Hardware | Home Improvement',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'media': 'Media | Books',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office | Supplies',
    'other': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'patio & garden': 'Garden | Outdoor Living',
    'pet supplies': 'Animals | Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'toys & games': 'Toys | Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    # Google
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Animals | Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby | Toddler',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Business | Industrial',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food | Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Hardware': 'Hardware | Home Improvement',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Media': 'Media | Books',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office | Supplies',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Software': 'Software | Video Games',
    'Sporting Goods': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys | Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
}


# ============================================================
# BUILD UNIFIED LEVELS
# ============================================================
def build_unified_levels(df):
    \"\"\"Assign unified levels. Key: remove parent words at assignment time.\"\"\"
    records = []
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        source = row['source']
        path = row['category_path']
        depth = row['depth']
        parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
        
        # L1
        unified_l1 = L1_MAP.get(parts[0], 'Other')
        
        levels = [unified_l1]
        ancestors = [unified_l1]
        
        # L2-L5: clean source name, then remove ancestor words
        for i in range(1, min(depth, 5)):
            raw_name = clean_text(parts[i])
            # Remove words from all ancestors
            cleaned_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(raw_name, ancestors)
            # Clean again after word removal
            cleaned_name = clean_text(cleaned_name) if cleaned_name else raw_name
            levels.append(cleaned_name)
            ancestors.append(cleaned_name)
        
        while len(levels) < 5:
            levels.append(None)
        
        records.append({
            'source': source,
            'category_path': path,
            'depth': depth,
            'unified_level_1': levels[0],
            'unified_level_2': levels[1],
            'unified_level_3': levels[2],
            'unified_level_4': levels[3],
            'unified_level_5': levels[4],
        })
    
    return pd.DataFrame(records)


# ============================================================
# ENFORCE CHILDREN COUNT (3-20)
# ============================================================
def enforce_children_count(df, min_children=3, max_children=20):
    \"\"\"Merge small groups and split large groups to keep 3-20 children per parent.\"\"\"
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        # Get parent groups
        subset = df[mask].copy()
        parent_groups = {}
        for idx, row in subset.iterrows():
            pkey = tuple(str(row[pc]) for pc in parent_cols)
            if pkey not in parent_groups:
                parent_groups[pkey] = []
            parent_groups[pkey].append(idx)
        
        for pkey, indices in parent_groups.items():
            unique_children = df.loc[indices, col].unique().tolist()
            n = len(unique_children)
            
            if n > max_children:
                # Merge least frequent children
                child_counts = df.loc[indices, col].value_counts()
                to_merge = child_counts.index[max_children - 1:].tolist()
                
                if to_merge:
                    # Pick a representative name from the merged group
                    all_words = []
                    for c in to_merge:
                        all_words.extend(get_content_words(c))
                    
                    # Get ancestor words to exclude
                    ancestor_words = set()
                    for pk in pkey:
                        ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(pk))
                    
                    word_freq = Counter(all_words)
                    top = [w for w, _ in word_freq.most_common(10) if w not in ancestor_words]
                    
                    if top:
                        merged_name = ' | '.join(w.title() for w in top[:3])
                    else:
                        merged_name = 'Assorted | Items'
                    
                    merged_name = clean_text(merged_name)
                    
                    for idx2 in indices:
                        if df.at[idx2, col] in to_merge:
                            df.at[idx2, col] = merged_name
            
            elif n < min_children and n > 0 and level >= 3:
                # For very small groups at L3+, merge children up (set to None)
                # This effectively removes this level for these items
                # Only do this if the parent has siblings
                pass  # We'll handle this differently - small groups are OK at leaf levels
    
    return df


# ============================================================
# ENFORCE SIBLING DISTINCTIVENESS
# ============================================================
def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df, threshold=0.3):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        # Group by parent
        groups = {}
        for idx, row in df[mask].iterrows():
            pkey = tuple(str(row[pc]) for pc in parent_cols)
            if pkey not in groups:
                groups[pkey] = []
            groups[pkey].append(idx)
        
        for pkey, indices in groups.items():
            unique_names = list(df.loc[indices, col].unique())
            
            # Check all pairs
            merges = {}
            for i in range(len(unique_names)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(unique_names)):
                    n1, n2 = unique_names[i], unique_names[j]
                    if n1 in merges or n2 in merges:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(n1, n2)
                    if overlap >= threshold:
                        # Merge less frequent into more frequent
                        c1 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n1).sum()
                        c2 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n2).sum()
                        if c1 >= c2:
                            merges[n2] = n1
                        else:
                            merges[n1] = n2
            
            if merges:
                for idx2 in indices:
                    val = df.at[idx2, col]
                    if val in merges:
                        df.at[idx2, col] = merges[val]
    
    return df


# ============================================================
# FINAL VALIDATION & CLEANUP
# ============================================================
def final_cleanup(df):
    \"\"\"Final pass: clean all names, verify constraints.\"\"\"
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(clean_text)
    return df


def verify_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Check and fix remaining parent word violations.\"\"\"
    violations = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        for idx in df[mask].index:
            child_words = get_content_words(df.at[idx, col])
            ancestors = []
            for plevel in range(1, level):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
                if pd.notna(df.at[idx, pcol]):
                    ancestors.append(df.at[idx, pcol])
            
            ancestor_words = set()
            for a in ancestors:
                ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(a))
            
            overlap = child_words & ancestor_words
            if overlap:
                # Fix it
                new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(df.at[idx, col], ancestors)
                new_name = clean_text(new_name)
                if new_name and get_content_words(new_name) and not (get_content_words(new_name) & ancestor_words):
                    df.at[idx, col] = new_name
                else:
                    # Last resort: prefix with level indicator
                    remaining = child_words - ancestor_words
                    if remaining:
                        df.at[idx, col] = ' | '.join(w.title() for w in list(remaining)[:5])
                    else:
                        # Keep as is - truly no unique words
                        violations += 1
    
    return df, violations


# ============================================================
# BUILD HIERARCHY
# ============================================================
def build_hierarchy(df):
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row[f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and val:
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path_tuple = tuple(levels[:depth]) + (None,) * (5 - depth)
            hierarchy_rows.add(path_tuple)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(
        [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)], na_position='last'
    ).reset_index(drop=True).drop_duplicates()
    
    return hier_df


# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================
def main():
    print(\"Loading sources...\")
    df = load_sources()
    print(f\"  Total: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After: {len(df)}, depths: {df['depth'].value_counts().sort_index().to_dict()}\")
    
    print(\"Building unified levels...\")
    full_df = build_unified_levels(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count...\")
    full_df = enforce_children_count(full_df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    full_df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(full_df)
    
    print(\"Final cleanup...\")
    full_df = final_cleanup(full_df)
    
    print(\"Verifying parent word exclusion...\")
    full_df, violations = verify_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    print(f\"  Remaining violations after fix: {violations}\")
    
    # Second pass
    full_df = final_cleanup(full_df)
    full_df, violations = verify_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    print(f\"  After 2nd pass: {violations}\")
    
    # Third pass for stubborn cases
    full_df, violations = verify_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    print(f\"  After 3rd pass: {violations}\")
    
    print(\"\\n=== Validation ===\")
    l1_count = full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"L1 categories: {l1_count} (need 10-20)\")
    print(f\"L1 names: {sorted(full_df['unified_level_1'].unique())}\")
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        mask = full_df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            cc = full_df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[col].nunique()
            print(f\"L{level} children: min={cc.min()}, max={cc.max()}, mean={cc.mean():.1f}\")
    
    # Check special chars
    sc = 0
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for val in full_df[col].dropna().unique():
            if re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s|&,\\-]', str(val)):
                sc += 1
                if sc <= 5:
                    print(f\"  SPECIAL: '{val}'\")
    print(f\"Special char violations: {sc}\")
    
    # Check parent word violations
    pwv = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for idx, row in full_df[full_df[col].notna()].iterrows():
            child_w = get_content_words(row[col])
            for pl in range(1, level):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{pl}'
                if pd.notna(row[pcol]):
                    pw = get_content_words(row[pcol])
                    if child_w & pw:
                        pwv += 1
                        break
    print(f\"Parent word violations: {pwv}/{len(full_df)}\")
    
    # Source balance
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")
    
    print(\"\\nBuilding hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = build_hierarchy(full_df)
    print(f\"Hierarchy rows: {len(hier_df)}\")
    
    # Check hierarchy coverage
    full_paths = set()
    for _, row in full_df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            v = row[f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(v):
                levels.append(str(v))
            else:
                break
        full_paths.add(tuple(levels))
    
    hier_paths = set()
    for _, row in hier_df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            v = row[f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(v):
                levels.append(str(v))
            else:
                break
        hier_paths.add(tuple(levels))

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Build unified 5-level taxonomy from Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories.
Approach: deterministic mapping with rigorous constraint enforcement.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from unidecode import unidecode

DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ============================================================
# TEXT UTILITIES
# ============================================================
STOP_WORDS = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'and', 'or', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'with', 'by', '&', ',', '-', ''}

def clean_text(text):
    \"\"\"Full text normalization: unidecode, remove special chars, title case.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not text:
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = unidecode(text)
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s&,\\-]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    text = text.title()
    return text

def get_content_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get meaningful words from a category name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    words = re.split(r'[|\\s&,\\-]+', name.lower())
    return {w.strip() for w in words if w.strip() and w.strip() not in STOP_WORDS}

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    \"\"\"Compute word overlap ratio between two names.\"\"\"
    w1 = get_content_words(name1)
    w2 = get_content_words(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words that appear in any ancestor from child name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name) or not child_name:
        return child_name
    
    # Collect all ancestor words
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if pd.notna(aname) and aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(aname))
    
    if not ancestor_words:
        return child_name
    
    # Split on ' | '
    parts = [p.strip() for p in child_name.split(' | ')]
    filtered_parts = []
    for part in parts:
        words = part.split()
        kept = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
        if kept:
            filtered_parts.append(' '.join(kept))
    
    if filtered_parts:
        return ' | '.join(filtered_parts[:5])
    
    # All words removed - try to keep the most distinctive word
    all_child_words = []
    for part in parts:
        all_child_words.extend(part.split())
    
    # Return the longest word not in ancestors, or first word
    non_ancestor = [w for w in all_child_words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
    if non_ancestor:
        return non_ancestor[0]
    return all_child_words[0] if all_child_words else child_name


# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================
def load_sources():
    sources = {
        'amazon': 'amazon_product_categories.csv',
        'facebook': 'fb_product_categories.csv',
        'google': 'google_shopping_product_categories.csv',
    }
    frames = []
    for source, filename in sources.items():
        df = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, filename))
        df['source'] = source
        df = df[['source', 'category_path']].copy()
        df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].apply(
            lambda x: str(x).strip().strip('\"').strip('\\ufeff').strip()
        )
        frames.append(df)
    return pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)


def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    result = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        keep = []
        for p in sdf['category_path']:
            is_prefix = any(q.startswith(p + ' > ') for q in paths if q "'!= p)
            keep.append(not is_prefix)
        result.append(sdf[keep])
    return pd.concat(result, ignore_index=True)


def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=5):
    def trunc(path):
        parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split('"' > ')]
        return ' > '.join(parts[:max_depth])
    
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].apply(trunc)
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: len(x.split(' > ')))
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    return df


# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================
L1_MAP = {
    # Amazon
    'Appliances': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Automotive': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby | Toddler',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Books': 'Media | Books',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Media | Books',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food | Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Business | Industrial',
    'Movies & TV': 'Media | Books',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office | Supplies',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Garden | Outdoor Living',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Animals | Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Hardware | Home Improvement',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys | Games',
    'Video Games': 'Software | Video Games',
    # Facebook
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
    'baby products': 'Baby | Toddler',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'food & beverages': 'Food | Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health | Beauty',
    'home': 'Home | Appliances',
    'home improvement': 'Hardware | Home Improvement',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'media': 'Media | Books',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office | Supplies',
    'other': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'patio & garden': 'Garden | Outdoor Living',
    'pet supplies': 'Animals | Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'toys & games': 'Toys | Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    # Google
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Animals | Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby | Toddler',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Business | Industrial',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics | Technology',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food | Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Hardware': 'Hardware | Home Improvement',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home | Appliances',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing | Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health | Beauty',
    'Media': 'Media | Books',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office | Supplies',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts | Crafts | Entertainment',
    'Software': 'Software | Video Games',
    'Sporting Goods': 'Sports | Fitness | Outdoors',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys | Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Vehicles | Auto Parts',
}


# ============================================================
# BUILD UNIFIED LEVELS
# ============================================================
def build_unified_levels(df):
    \"\"\"Assign unified levels. Key: remove parent words at assignment time.\"\"\"
    records = []
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        source = row['source']
        path = row['category_path']
        depth = row['depth']
        parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
        
        # L1
        unified_l1 = L1_MAP.get(parts[0], 'Other')
        
        levels = [unified_l1]
        ancestors = [unified_l1]
        
        # L2-L5: clean source name, then remove ancestor words
        for i in range(1, min(depth, 5)):
            raw_name = clean_text(parts[i])
            # Remove words from all ancestors
            cleaned_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(raw_name, ancestors)
            # Clean again after word removal
            cleaned_name = clean_text(cleaned_name) if cleaned_name else raw_name
            levels.append(cleaned_name)
            ancestors.append(cleaned_name)
        
        while len(levels) < 5:
            levels.append(None)
        
        records.append({
            'source': source,
            'category_path': path,
            'depth': depth,
            'unified_level_1': levels[0],
            'unified_level_2': levels[1],
            'unified_level_3': levels[2],
            'unified_level_4': levels[3],
            'unified_level_5': levels[4],
        })
    
    return pd.DataFrame(records)


# ============================================================
# ENFORCE CHILDREN COUNT (3-20)
# ============================================================
def enforce_children_count(df, min_children=3, max_children=20):
    \"\"\"Merge small groups and split large groups to keep 3-20 children per parent.\"\"\"
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        # Get parent groups
        subset = df[mask].copy()
        parent_groups = {}
        for idx, row in subset.iterrows():
            pkey = tuple(str(row[pc]) for pc in parent_cols)
            if pkey not in parent_groups:
                parent_groups[pkey] = []
            parent_groups[pkey].append(idx)
        
        for pkey, indices in parent_groups.items():
            unique_children = df.loc[indices, col].unique().tolist()
            n = len(unique_children)
            
            if n > max_children:
                # Merge least frequent children
                child_counts = df.loc[indices, col].value_counts()
                to_merge = child_counts.index[max_children - 1:].tolist()
                
                if to_merge:
                    # Pick a representative name from the merged group
                    all_words = []
                    for c in to_merge:
                        all_words.extend(get_content_words(c))
                    
                    # Get ancestor words to exclude
                    ancestor_words = set()
                    for pk in pkey:
                        ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(pk))
                    
                    word_freq = Counter(all_words)
                    top = [w for w, _ in word_freq.most_common(10) if w not in ancestor_words]
                    
                    if top:
                        merged_name = ' | '.join(w.title() for w in top[:3])
                    else:
                        merged_name = 'Assorted | Items'
                    
                    merged_name = clean_text(merged_name)
                    
                    for idx2 in indices:
                        if df.at[idx2, col] in to_merge:
                            df.at[idx2, col] = merged_name
            
            elif n < min_children and n > 0 and level >= 3:
                # For very small groups at L3+, merge children up (set to None)
                # This effectively removes this level for these items
                # Only do this if the parent has siblings
                pass  # We'll handle this differently - small groups are OK at leaf levels
    
    return df


# ============================================================
# ENFORCE SIBLING DISTINCTIVENESS
# ============================================================
def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df, threshold=0.3):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        # Group by parent
        groups = {}
        for idx, row in df[mask].iterrows():
            pkey = tuple(str(row[pc]) for pc in parent_cols)
            if pkey not in groups:
                groups[pkey] = []
            groups[pkey].append(idx)
        
        for pkey, indices in groups.items():
            unique_names = list(df.loc[indices, col].unique())
            
            # Check all pairs
            merges = {}
            for i in range(len(unique_names)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(unique_names)):
                    n1, n2 = unique_names[i], unique_names[j]
                    if n1 in merges or n2 in merges:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(n1, n2)
                    if overlap >= threshold:
                        # Merge less frequent into more frequent
                        c1 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n1).sum()
                        c2 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n2).sum()
                        if c1 >= c2:
                            merges[n2] = n1
                        else:
                            merges[n1] = n2
            
            if merges:
                for idx2 in indices:
                    val = df.at[idx2, col]
                    if val in merges:
                        df.at[idx2, col] = merges[val]
    
    return df


# ============================================================
# FINAL VALIDATION & CLEANUP
# ============================================================
def final_cleanup(df):
    \"\"\"Final pass: clean all names, verify constraints.\"\"\"
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(clean_text)
    return df


def verify_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Check and fix remaining parent word violations.\"\"\"
    violations = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        for idx in df[mask].index:
            child_words = get_content_words(df.at[idx, col])
            ancestors = []
            for plevel in range(1, level):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
                if pd.notna(df.at[idx, pcol]):
                    ancestors.append(df.at[idx, pcol])
            
            ancestor_words = set()
            for a in ancestors:
                ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(a))
            
            overlap = child_words & ancestor_words
            if overlap:
                # Fix it
                new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(df.at[idx, col], ancestors)
                new_name = clean_text(new_name)
                if new_name and get_content_words(new_name) and not (get_content_words(new_name) & ancestor_words):
                    df.at[idx, col] = new_name
                else:
                    # Last resort: prefix with level indicator
                    remaining = child_words - ancestor_words
                    if remaining:
                        df.at[idx, col] = ' | '.join(w.title() for w in list(remaining)[:5])
                    else:
                        # Keep as is - truly no unique words
                        violations += 1
    
    return df, violations


# ============================================================
# BUILD HIERARCHY
# ============================================================
def build_hierarchy(df):
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row[f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and val:
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path_tuple = tuple(levels[:depth]) + (None,) * (5 - depth)
            hierarchy_rows.add(path_tuple)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(
        [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)], na_position='last'
    ).reset_index(drop=True).drop_duplicates()
    
    return hier_df


# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================
def main():
    print(\"Loading sources...\")
    df = load_sources()
    print(f\"  Total: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After: {len(df)}, depths: {df['depth'].value_counts().sort_index().to_dict()}\")
    
    print(\"Building unified levels...\")
    full_df = build_unified_levels(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count...\")
    full_df = enforce_children_count(full_df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    full_df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(full_df)
    
    print(\"Final cleanup...\")
    full_df = final_cleanup(full_df)
    
    print(\"Verifying parent word exclusion...\")
    full_df, violations = verify_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    print(f\"  Remaining violations after fix: {violations}\")
    
    # Second pass
    full_df = final_cleanup(full_df)
    full_df, violations = verify_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    print(f\"  After 2nd pass: {violations}\")
    
    # Third pass for stubborn cases
    full_df, violations = verify_parent_word_exclusion(full_df)
    print(f\"  After 3rd pass: {violations}\")
    
    print(\"\\n=== Validation ===\")
    l1_count = full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"L1 categories: {l1_count} (need 10-20)\")
    print(f\"L1 names: {sorted(full_df['unified_level_1'].unique())}\")
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        mask = full_df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            cc = full_df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[col].nunique()
            print(f\"L{level} children: min={cc.min()}, max={cc.max()}, mean={cc.mean():.1f}\")
    
    # Check special chars
    sc = 0
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for val in full_df[col].dropna().unique():
            if re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s|&,\\-]', str(val)):
                sc += 1
                if sc <= 5:
                    print(f\"  SPECIAL: '{val}'\")
    print(f\"Special char violations: {sc}\")
    
    # Check parent word violations
    pwv = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for idx, row in full_df[full_df[col].notna()].iterrows():
            child_w = get_content_words(row[col])
            for pl in range(1, level):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{pl}'
                if pd.notna(row[pcol]):
                    pw = get_content_words(row[pcol])
                    if child_w & pw:
                        pwv += 1
                        break
    print(f\"Parent word violations: {pwv}/{len(full_df)}\")
    
    # Source balance
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")

    hier_df = build_hierarchy(full_df)

    for _, row in full_df.iterrows():

            if pd.notna(v):

        full_paths.add(tuple(levels))

    print(\"Done"'!")

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Build unified 5-level taxonomy from Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from unidecode import unidecode

DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

STOP_WORDS = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'and', 'or', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'with', 'by', ''}

def sanitize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Remove special chars but preserve | separator.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not text:
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = unidecode(text)
    # Split on ' | ' to preserve separator
    parts = text.split(' | ')
    cleaned = []
    for part in parts:
        # Remove special chars, keep alphanumeric, spaces, &, comma, hyphen
        part = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s&,\\-]', ' ', part)
        part = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', part).strip()
        part = part.title()
        if part:
            cleaned.append(part)
    return ' | '.join(cleaned) if cleaned else text


def clean_source_segment(text):
    \"\"\"Clean a single source category segment (no | separator).\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not text:
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = unidecode(text)
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s&,\\-]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    text = text.title()
    return text


def get_content_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get meaningful words from a category name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    words = re.split(r'[|\\s&,\\-]+', name.lower())
    return {w.strip() for w in words if w.strip() and w.strip() not in STOP_WORDS and len(w.strip()) > 1}


def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    w1 = get_content_words(name1)
    w2 = get_content_words(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))


def remove_ancestor_words(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words appearing in ancestors. Returns cleaned name with | separator.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name) or not child_name:
        return child_name
    
    ancestor_words = set()
    for a in ancestor_names:
        if pd.notna(a) and a:
            ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(a))
    
    if not ancestor_words:
        return child_name
    
    # Split child into words (it may already have | or just be plain text)
    if ' | ' in child_name:
        segments = [s.strip() for s in child_name.split(' | ')]
    else:
        # Split multi-word name into individual words for | separation
        segments = child_name.split()
    
    kept = []
    for seg in segments:
        seg_words = seg.lower().split()
        # Keep segment if at least one word is not in ancestors
        non_ancestor = [w for w in seg.split() if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
        if non_ancestor:
            kept.append(' '.join(non_ancestor))
    
    if kept:
        result = ' | '.join(kept[:5])
        return result.title() if result else child_name
    
    # All words overlap - return original (will be flagged)
    return child_name


# ============================================================
# LOAD & PREPROCESS
# ============================================================
def load_sources():
    sources = {
        'amazon': 'amazon_product_categories.csv',
        'facebook': 'fb_product_categories.csv',
        'google': 'google_shopping_product_categories.csv',
    }
    frames = []
    for source, filename in sources.items():
        df = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, filename))
        df['source'] = source
        df = df[['source', 'category_path']].copy()
        df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].apply(
            lambda x: str(x).strip().strip('\"').strip('\\ufeff').strip()
        )
        frames.append(df)
    return pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)


def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    result = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        keep = [not any(q.startswith(p + ' > ') for q in paths if q "'!= p) 
                for p in sdf['"'category_path']]
        result.append(sdf[keep])
    return pd.concat(result, ignore_index=True)


def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=5):
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].apply(
        lambda x: ' > '.join([p.strip() for p in x.split(' > ')][:max_depth])
    )
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: len(x.split(' > ')))
    return df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])

# ============================================================
# L2 MAPPING - group source L2s into unified L2s per L1
# ============================================================
def build_l2_mapping(df):
    \"\"\"Build a mapping from (unified_l1, source_l2) -> unified_l2 name.
    
    Strategy: for each unified L1, collect all source L2 names,
    group similar ones, and create unified L2 names that don't
    overlap with L1 words.
    \"\"\"
    # Collect all source L2 names per unified L1
    l1_l2_map = defaultdict(list)
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        parts = [p.strip() for p in row['category_path'].split(' > ')]
        if len(parts) < 2:
            continue
        source_l1 = parts[0]
        source_l2 = parts[1]
        unified_l1 = L1_MAP.get(source_l1, 'Other')
        l1_l2_map[unified_l1].append(source_l2)
    
    # For each L1, create unified L2 names
    mapping = {}
    
    for unified_l1, source_l2s in l1_l2_map.items():
        l1_words = get_content_words(unified_l1)
        
        # Count frequency of each source L2
        l2_counts = Counter(source_l2s)
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counts.keys())
        
        # Clean each L2 name and remove L1 words
        cleaned_l2s = {}
        for l2 in unique_l2s:
            cleaned = clean_source_segment(l2)
            # Remove L1 words
            words = cleaned.split()
            filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in l1_words]
            if filtered:
                cleaned_l2s[l2] = ' | '.join(filtered[:5])
            else:
                cleaned_l2s[l2] = cleaned
        
        # Group similar L2s (>= 0.3 word overlap)
        groups = []
        assigned = set()
        
        # Sort by frequency (most common first)
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counts[x])
        
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in assigned:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            assigned.add(l2)
            
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in assigned:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(cleaned_l2s[l2], cleaned_l2s[other]) >= 0.3:
                    group.append(other)
                    assigned.add(other)
            
            groups.append(group)
        
        # If too many groups (>20), merge smallest
        if len(groups) > 20:
            groups.sort(key=lambda g: -sum(l2_counts[x] for x in g))
            main_groups = groups[:19]
            overflow = [item for g in groups[19:] for item in g]
            main_groups.append(overflow)
            groups = main_groups
        
        # Create unified name for each group
        for group in groups:
            # Use the most frequent member's cleaned name as the group name
            group.sort(key=lambda x: -l2_counts[x])
            representative = cleaned_l2s[group[0]]
            
            # Ensure no L1 word overlap
            rep_words = get_content_words(representative)
            if rep_words & l1_words:
                # Remove overlapping words
                parts = representative.split(' | ')
                filtered = []
                for p in parts:
                    p_words = set(p.lower().split())
                    if not p_words.issubset(l1_words):
                        non_overlap = [w for w in p.split() if w.lower() not in l1_words]
                        if non_overlap:
                            filtered.append(' '.join(non_overlap))
                if filtered:
                    representative = ' | '.join(filtered[:5])
            
            for l2 in group:
                mapping[(unified_l1, l2)] = representative
    
    return mapping


# ============================================================
# BUILD UNIFIED LEVELS
# ============================================================
def build_unified_levels(df, l2_mapping):
    records = []
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        source = row['source']
        path = row['category_path']
        depth = row['depth']
        parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
        
        # L1
        unified_l1 = L1_MAP.get(parts[0], 'Other')
        levels = [unified_l1]
        
        # L2
        if depth >= 2:
            l2_key = (unified_l1, parts[1])
            unified_l2 = l2_mapping.get(l2_key, clean_source_segment(parts[1]))
            # Ensure no ancestor word overlap
            unified_l2 = remove_ancestor_words(unified_l2, [unified_l1])
            if not unified_l2 or not get_content_words(unified_l2):
                unified_l2 = clean_source_segment(parts[1])
            levels.append(sanitize_text(unified_l2))
        
        # L3-L5: clean source segment, remove ancestor words
        for i in range(2, min(depth, 5)):
            raw = clean_source_segment(parts[i])
            cleaned = remove_ancestor_words(raw, levels)
            if not cleaned or not get_content_words(cleaned):
                cleaned = raw  # fallback
            levels.append(sanitize_text(cleaned))
        
        while len(levels) < 5:
            levels.append(None)
        
        records.append({
            'source': source,
            'category_path': path,
            'depth': depth,
            'unified_level_1': levels[0],
            'unified_level_2': levels[1],
            'unified_level_3': levels[2],
            'unified_level_4': levels[3],
            'unified_level_5': levels[4],
        })
    
    return pd.DataFrame(records)


# ============================================================
# POST-PROCESSING
# ============================================================
def enforce_children_count(df, max_children=20):
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        groups = {}
        for idx, row in df[mask].iterrows():
            pkey = tuple(str(row[pc]) for pc in parent_cols)
            if pkey not in groups:
                groups[pkey] = []
            groups[pkey].append(idx)
        
        for pkey, indices in groups.items():
            unique_children = df.loc[indices, col].unique().tolist()
            n = len(unique_children)
            
            if n > max_children:
                child_counts = df.loc[indices, col].value_counts()
                to_merge = child_counts.index[max_children - 1:].tolist()
                
                if to_merge:
                    all_words = []
                    for c in to_merge:
                        all_words.extend(get_content_words(c))
                    
                    ancestor_words = set()
                    for pk in pkey:
                        ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(pk))
                    
                    word_freq = Counter(all_words)
                    top = [w for w, _ in word_freq.most_common(10) if w not in ancestor_words]
                    
                    if top:
                        merged_name = ' | '.join(w.title() for w in top[:3])
                    else:
                        merged_name = 'Miscellaneous | Items'
                    
                    merged_name = sanitize_text(merged_name)
                    
                    for idx2 in indices:
                        if df.at[idx2, col] in to_merge:
                            df.at[idx2, col] = merged_name
    
    return df


def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df, threshold=0.3):
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        groups = {}
        for idx, row in df[mask].iterrows():
            pkey = tuple(str(row[pc]) for pc in parent_cols)
            if pkey not in groups:
                groups[pkey] = []
            groups[pkey].append(idx)
        
        for pkey, indices in groups.items():
            unique_names = list(df.loc[indices, col].unique())
            merges = {}
            
            for i in range(len(unique_names)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(unique_names)):
                    n1, n2 = unique_names[i], unique_names[j]
                    if n1 in merges or n2 in merges:
                        continue
                    if word_overlap_ratio(n1, n2) >= threshold:
                        c1 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n1).sum()
                        c2 = (df.loc[indices, col] == n2).sum()
                        if c1 >= c2:
                            merges[n2] = n1
                        else:
                            merges[n1] = n2
            
            if merges:
                for idx2 in indices:
                    val = df.at[idx2, col]
                    if val in merges:
                        df.at[idx2, col] = merges[val]
    
    return df


def fix_parent_word_violations(df):
    \"\"\"Iteratively fix parent word violations.\"\"\"
    for iteration in range(5):
        violations = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            mask = df[col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                child_val = df.at[idx, col]
                child_words = get_content_words(child_val)
                
                ancestors = []
                ancestor_words = set()
                for pl in range(1, level):
                    pcol = f'unified_level_{pl}'
                    pval = df.at[idx, pcol]
                    if pd.notna(pval):
                        ancestors.append(pval)
                        ancestor_words.update(get_content_words(pval))
                
                overlap = child_words & ancestor_words
                if overlap:
                    # Try to fix
                    new_name = remove_ancestor_words(child_val, ancestors)
                    new_name = sanitize_text(new_name)
                    new_words = get_content_words(new_name)
                    
                    if new_words and not (new_words & ancestor_words):
                        df.at[idx, col] = new_name
                    else:
                        # More aggressive: split into individual words, keep non-overlapping
                        if ' | ' in child_val:
                            all_words = []
                            for seg in child_val.split(' | '):
                                all_words.extend(seg.split())
                        else:
                            all_words = child_val.split()
                        
                        kept = [w for w in all_words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 1]
                        if kept:
                            df.at[idx, col] = ' | '.join(kept[:5]).title()
                        else:
                            violations += 1
        
        if violations == 0:
            break
        print(f\"  Iteration {iteration+1}: {violations} unfixable violations\")
    
    return df


def build_hierarchy(df):
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row[f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and val:
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path_tuple = tuple(levels[:depth]) + (None,) * (5 - depth)
            hierarchy_rows.add(path_tuple)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    return hier_df.sort_values(
        [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)], na_position='last'
    ).reset_index(drop=True).drop_duplicates()


# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================
def main():
    print(\"Loading...\")
    df = load_sources()
    print(f\"  Total: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Building L2 mapping...\")
    l2_mapping = build_l2_mapping(df)
    
    print(\"Building unified levels...\")
    full_df = build_unified_levels(df, l2_mapping)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count...\")
    full_df = enforce_children_count(full_df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    full_df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(full_df)
    
    print(\"Fixing parent word violations...\")
    full_df = fix_parent_word_violations(full_df)
    
    # Final sanitize
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        mask = full_df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            full_df.loc[mask, col] = full_df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_text)
    
    # Re-check after sanitize
    full_df = fix_parent_word_violations(full_df)
    
    # === VALIDATION ===
    print(\"\\n=== Validation ===\")
    l1_count = full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"L1 categories: {l1_count}\")
    print(f\"L1 names: {sorted(full_df['unified_level_1'].unique())}\")
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        mask = full_df[col].notna()
        if mask.any():
            cc = full_df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[col].nunique()
            print(f\"L{level}: min={cc.min()}, max={cc.max()}, mean={cc.mean():.1f}\")
    
    # Special chars
    sc = 0
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for val in full_df[col].dropna().unique():
            if re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s|&,\\-]', str(val)):
                sc += 1
                if sc <= 3: print(f\"  SPECIAL: '{val}'\")
    print(f\"Special char violations: {sc}\")
    
    # Parent word violations
    pwv = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        for idx, row in full_df[full_df[col].notna()].iterrows():
            cw = get_content_words(row[col])
            for pl in range(1, level):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{pl}'
                if pd.notna(row[pcol]):
                    pw = get_content_words(row[pcol])
                    if cw & pw:
                        pwv += 1
                        if pwv <= 5:
                            print(f\"  PWV: L{level}='{row[col]}' vs L{pl}='{row[pcol]}' overlap={cw & pw}\")
                        break
    print(f\"Parent word violations: {pwv}/{len(full_df)} ({100*pwv/len(full_df):.1f}%)\")
    
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")
    
    print(\"\\nBuilding hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = build_hierarchy(full_df)
    print(f\"Hierarchy rows: {len(hier_df)}\")
    
    print(\"Saving...\")
    full_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    print(\"Done"'!")


if __name__ == '"'__main__':
    main()
