#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder v2
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer
import nltk
nltk.download('"'wordnet', quiet=True)

DATA_DIR = '/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()

# ============================================================
# TEXT STANDARDIZATION
# ============================================================

def sanitize_name(text):
    \"\"\"Remove ALL special characters, keep only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = text.replace('&', 'and')
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s\\-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def lemmatize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Lemmatize each word in text.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    words = str(text).lower().split()
    lemmatized = [lemmatizer.lemmatize(w) for w in words]
    return ' '.join(lemmatized)

def standardize_name(text):
    \"\"\"Full standardization: sanitize + lemmatize + title case + max 5 words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = lemmatize_text(text)
    text = text.title()
    words = text.split()
    if len(words) > 5:
        words = words[:5]
    return ' '.join(words)

def words_of(name):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase words from a name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(str(name).lower().split())

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    \"\"\"Calculate word overlap ratio between two names.\"\"\"
    w1 = words_of(name1)
    w2 = words_of(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

L1_MAP = {
    # Amazon
    'Appliances': 'Home and Kitchen',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts and Crafts',
    'Automotive': 'Vehicles and Parts',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby and Toddler',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Books': 'Books and Media',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Books and Media',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food and Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home and Kitchen',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Business and Industrial',
    'Movies & TV': 'Books and Media',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office Supplies',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sporting Goods',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Hardware and Tools',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys and Games',
    'Video Games': 'Books and Media',
    # Facebook
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts and Crafts',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts and Crafts',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Vehicles and Parts',
    'baby products': 'Baby and Toddler',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics',
    'food & beverages': 'Food and Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health and Beauty',
    'home': 'Home and Kitchen',
    'home improvement': 'Hardware and Tools',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'media': 'Books and Media',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office Supplies',
    'other': 'Home and Kitchen',
    'patio & garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sporting Goods',
    'toys & games': 'Toys and Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    # Google
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts and Crafts',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby and Toddler',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Business and Industrial',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food and Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home and Kitchen',
    'Hardware': 'Hardware and Tools',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health and Beauty',

    'Sporting Goods': 'Sporting Goods',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys and Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Vehicles and Parts',
}

# Standardize L1 names
UNIFIED_L1 = {}
for k, v in L1_MAP.items():
    UNIFIED_L1[k] = standardize_name(v)

# ============================================================
# LEVEL ASSIGNMENT - Smart segment mapping
# ============================================================

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, parent_name):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child that appear in parent. Return cleaned name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name) or pd.isna(parent_name):
        return child_name
    parent_words = words_of(parent_name)
    child_words = str(child_name).split()
    filtered = [w for w in child_words if w.lower() not in parent_words]
    if not filtered:
        return child_name  # Don't return empty
    return ' '.join(filtered)

def assign_levels(path, source):
    \"\"\"Assign unified levels 1-5 for a category path.\"\"\"
    parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
    
    # L1 from mapping
    l1_raw = parts[0]
    l1 = UNIFIED_L1.get(l1_raw, standardize_name(l1_raw))
    
    # For remaining levels, use source path segments starting from index 1
    # But skip segments that are too similar to L1 (word overlap > 50%)
    remaining_parts = parts[1:]
    
    # Standardize remaining parts
    std_parts = []
    for p in remaining_parts:
        std = standardize_name(p)
        if std:
            std_parts.append(std)
    
    # Skip parts that overlap too much with L1
    filtered_parts = []
    l1_words = words_of(l1)
    for p in std_parts:
        p_words = words_of(p)
        if p_words and l1_words:
            overlap = len(p_words & l1_words) / len(p_words)
            if overlap > 0.5:
                # This segment is redundant with L1, skip it but keep its children
                continue
        filtered_parts.append(p)
    
    # Now remove parent words from each level
    levels = [l1]
    prev_all_words = words_of(l1)  # Accumulate all ancestor words
    
    for i, part in enumerate(filtered_parts[:4]):  # Max 4 more levels (L2-L5)
        # Remove words from all ancestors
        cleaned = part
        for ancestor_words_set in [words_of(levels[j]) for j in range(len(levels))]:
            cleaned = remove_parent_words_from_name(cleaned, ' '.join(ancestor_words_set))
        
        if cleaned and cleaned.strip():
            levels.append(cleaned)
        else:
            levels.append(part)  # Keep original if cleaning empties it
    
    return levels


# ============================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE
# ============================================================

def main():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    amz = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amz['source'] = 'amazon'
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    ggl = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    ggl['source'] = 'google'
    
    for d in [amz, fb, ggl]:
        d['category_path'] = d['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    df = pd.concat([amz[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     fb[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     ggl[['source', 'category_path']]], ignore_index=True)
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Compute depth
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda p: len(p.split(' > ')))
    
    # Filter to depth <= 5
    df = df[df['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    print(f\"  After depth filter: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Remove prefix paths per source
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    to_remove = set()
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_paths = set(df[df['source'] == source]['category_path'].unique())
        for path in source_paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in source_paths:
                    to_remove.add((source, prefix))
    
    mask = df.apply(lambda r: (r['source'], r['category_path']) not in to_remove, axis=1)
    df = df[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Deduplicate within same source
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path']).reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Assign unified levels
    print(\"Assigning unified levels...\")
    levels_data = df.apply(lambda r: assign_levels(r['category_path'], r['source']), axis=1)
    
    for i in range(5):
        col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
        df[col] = levels_data.apply(lambda x: x[i] if i < len(x) else None)
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # ============================================================
    # ENFORCE CONSTRAINTS
    # ============================================================
    
    print(\"Enforcing constraints...\")
    
    # 1. Sanitize all names (no special chars)
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
    
    # 2. Parent word exclusion - iterative
    for iteration in range(3):
        violations = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            mask = df[child_col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                # Check against ALL ancestors
                all_ancestor_words = set()
                for anc_level in range(1, level):
                    anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc_level}'
                    if pd.notna(df.at[idx, anc_col]):
                        all_ancestor_words |= words_of(df.at[idx, anc_col])
                
                child_words_set = words_of(child)
                overlap = child_words_set & all_ancestor_words
                if overlap:
                    violations += 1
                    new_words = [w for w in str(child).split() if w.lower() not in all_ancestor_words]
                    if new_words:
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = ' '.join(new_words)
                    # If all words removed, keep as is (will handle below)
        
        print(f\"  Parent word exclusion iteration {iteration+1}: {violations} violations\")
        if violations == 0:
            break
    
    # 3. Handle empty/None values after word removal
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        # Replace empty strings with None
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # For rows where a middle level became None, shift down
    for idx in df.index:
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val).strip())
        # Reassign compacted
        for i in range(5):
            col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
            df.at[idx, col] = levels[i] if i < len(levels) else None
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # 4. Enforce sibling distinctiveness (<30% word overlap)
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[child_col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        # Build rename map: merge similar siblings
        subset = df[mask][parent_cols + [child_col]].drop_duplicates()
        groups = subset.groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        
        rename_map = {}
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():
            siblings = list(row[child_col])
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Sort by frequency (most common first)
            freq = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols + [child_col]).size().reset_index(name='cnt')
            
            # Iteratively merge similar siblings
            merged = True
            while merged:
                merged = False
                for i in range(len(siblings)):
                    for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                        if word_overlap_ratio(siblings[i], siblings[j]) >= 0.30:
                            # Keep the longer/more descriptive one
                            keep = siblings[i] if len(str(siblings[i])) >= len(str(siblings[j])) else siblings[j]
                            remove = siblings[j] if keep == siblings[i] else siblings[i]
                            parent_key = tuple(row[c] for c in parent_cols)
                            rename_map[(parent_key, remove)] = keep
                            siblings.remove(remove)
                            merged = True
                            break
                    if merged:
                        break
        
        # Apply renames
        if rename_map:
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                parent_key = tuple(df.at[idx, c] for c in parent_cols)
                child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                key = (parent_key, child)
                if key in rename_map:
                    df.at[idx, child_col] = rename_map[key]
    
    # 5. Enforce children count (3-20 per parent)
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[child_col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        child_counts = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique().reset_index()
        child_counts.columns = parent_cols + ['count']
        
        # Handle >20 children: merge least frequent into \"Other\" bucket
        for _, row in child_counts[child_counts['count'] > 20].iterrows():
            parent_filter = mask.copy()
            for c in parent_cols:
                parent_filter = parent_filter & (df[c] == row[c])
            
            children_freq = df[parent_filter][child_col].value_counts()
            if len(children_freq) > 20:
                keep = set(children_freq.index[:18])
                # Group the rest by first letter or merge
                for idx in df[parent_filter].index:
                    child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                    if child not in keep:
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = 'General Item'
    
    # 6. Final sanitization pass
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
        # Remove empty
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # ============================================================
    # OUTPUT
    # ============================================================
    
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                   'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                   'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[output_cols].copy()
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Full mapping: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    # Generate hierarchy with ALL intermediate paths
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    for _, row in full_df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row.get(f'unified_level_{i}')
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path = tuple(levels[:depth] + [None] * (5 - depth))
            hierarchy_rows.add(path)
    
    hierarchy_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    hierarchy_df = hierarchy_df.sort_values(
        [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)],
        na_position='last'
    ).reset_index(drop=True)
    hierarchy_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hierarchy_df)} rows\")
    
    # ============================================================
    # DIAGNOSTICS
    # ============================================================
    
    print(\"\\n=== DIAGNOSTICS ===\")
    print(f\"Sources: {full_df['source'].value_counts().to_dict()}\")
    l1_count = full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"L1 categories ({l1_count}): {sorted(full_df['unified_level_1'].unique())}\")
    
    # Special chars check
    special_count = 0
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        m = full_df[col].notna()
        has_special = full_df.loc[m, col].apply(lambda x: bool(re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s\\-]', str(x))))
        special_count += has_special.sum()
    print(f\"Special char violations: {special_count}\")
    
    # Parent word exclusion check
    parent_violations = 0
    total_pairs = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'

            all_ancestor_words = set()

                anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc}'
                if pd.notna(full_df.at[idx, anc_col]):

            child_words_set = words_of(full_df.at[idx, child_col])
            if child_words_set & all_ancestor_words:
                parent_violations += 1
    print(f\"Parent word violations: {parent_violations}/{total_pairs} ({parent_violations/max(total_pairs,1)*100:.1f}%)\")
    
    # Sibling distinctiveness check

        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'

        if not m.any():
            continue
        groups = full_df[m].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():
            siblings = row[child_col]
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                    sibling_total += 1
                    if word_overlap_ratio(siblings[i], siblings[j]) >= 0.30:

        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'

        if not m.any():
            continue
        counts = full_df[m].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique().reset_index()

        violations = counts[(counts['cnt'] < 3) | (counts['cnt'] > 20)]
        children_violations += len(violations)

def lemmatize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Lemmatize each word in text.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    words = str(text).lower().split()
    lemmatized = [lemmatizer.lemmatize(w) for w in words]
    return ' '.join(lemmatized)

def standardize_name(text):
    \"\"\"Full standardization: sanitize + lemmatize + title case + max 5 words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = lemmatize_text(text)
    text = text.title()
    words = text.split()
    if len(words) > 5:
        words = words[:5]
    return ' '.join(words)

def words_of(name):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase words from a name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(str(name).lower().split())

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    \"\"\"Calculate word overlap ratio between two names.\"\"\"
    w1 = words_of(name1)
    w2 = words_of(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

L1_MAP = {
    # Amazon
    'Appliances': 'Home and Kitchen',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts and Crafts',
    'Automotive': 'Vehicles and Parts',

# Standardize L1 names
UNIFIED_L1 = {}
for k, v in L1_MAP.items():
    UNIFIED_L1[k] = standardize_name(v)

# ============================================================
# LEVEL ASSIGNMENT - Smart segment mapping
# ============================================================

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, parent_name):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child that appear in parent. Return cleaned name.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name) or pd.isna(parent_name):
        return child_name
    parent_words = words_of(parent_name)
    child_words = str(child_name).split()
    filtered = [w for w in child_words if w.lower() not in parent_words]
    if not filtered:
        return child_name  # Don't return empty
    return ' '.join(filtered)

def assign_levels(path, source):
    \"\"\"Assign unified levels 1-5 for a category path.\"\"\"
    parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
    
    # L1 from mapping
    l1_raw = parts[0]
    l1 = UNIFIED_L1.get(l1_raw, standardize_name(l1_raw))
    
    # For remaining levels, use source path segments starting from index 1
    # But skip segments that are too similar to L1 (word overlap > 50%)
    remaining_parts = parts[1:]
    
    # Standardize remaining parts
    std_parts = []
    for p in remaining_parts:
        std = standardize_name(p)
        if std:
            std_parts.append(std)
    
    # Skip parts that overlap too much with L1
    filtered_parts = []
    l1_words = words_of(l1)
    for p in std_parts:
        p_words = words_of(p)
        if p_words and l1_words:
            overlap = len(p_words & l1_words) / len(p_words)
            if overlap > 0.5:
                # This segment is redundant with L1, skip it but keep its children
                continue
        filtered_parts.append(p)
    
    # Now remove parent words from each level
    levels = [l1]
    prev_all_words = words_of(l1)  # Accumulate all ancestor words
    
    for i, part in enumerate(filtered_parts[:4]):  # Max 4 more levels (L2-L5)
        # Remove words from all ancestors
        cleaned = part
        for ancestor_words_set in [words_of(levels[j]) for j in range(len(levels))]:
            cleaned = remove_parent_words_from_name(cleaned, ' '.join(ancestor_words_set))
        
        if cleaned and cleaned.strip():
            levels.append(cleaned)
        else:
            levels.append(part)  # Keep original if cleaning empties it
    
    return levels


# ============================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE
# ============================================================

def main():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    amz = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amz['source'] = 'amazon'
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    ggl = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    ggl['source'] = 'google'
    
    for d in [amz, fb, ggl]:
        d['category_path'] = d['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    df = pd.concat([amz[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     fb[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     ggl[['source', 'category_path']]], ignore_index=True)
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Compute depth
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda p: len(p.split(' > ')))
    
    # Filter to depth <= 5
    df = df[df['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    print(f\"  After depth filter: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Remove prefix paths per source
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    to_remove = set()
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_paths = set(df[df['source'] == source]['category_path'].unique())
        for path in source_paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in source_paths:
                    to_remove.add((source, prefix))
    
    mask = df.apply(lambda r: (r['source'], r['category_path']) not in to_remove, axis=1)
    df = df[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Deduplicate within same source
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path']).reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Assign unified levels
    print(\"Assigning unified levels...\")
    levels_data = df.apply(lambda r: assign_levels(r['category_path'], r['source']), axis=1)
    
    for i in range(5):
        col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
        df[col] = levels_data.apply(lambda x: x[i] if i < len(x) else None)
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # ============================================================
    # ENFORCE CONSTRAINTS
    # ============================================================
    
    print(\"Enforcing constraints...\")
    
    # 1. Sanitize all names (no special chars)
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
    
    # 2. Parent word exclusion - iterative
    for iteration in range(3):
        violations = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            mask = df[child_col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                # Check against ALL ancestors
                all_ancestor_words = set()
                for anc_level in range(1, level):
                    anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc_level}'
                    if pd.notna(df.at[idx, anc_col]):
                        all_ancestor_words |= words_of(df.at[idx, anc_col])
                
                child_words_set = words_of(child)
                overlap = child_words_set & all_ancestor_words
                if overlap:
                    violations += 1
                    new_words = [w for w in str(child).split() if w.lower() not in all_ancestor_words]
                    if new_words:
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = ' '.join(new_words)
                    # If all words removed, keep as is (will handle below)
        
        print(f\"  Parent word exclusion iteration {iteration+1}: {violations} violations\")
        if violations == 0:
            break
    
    # 3. Handle empty/None values after word removal
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        # Replace empty strings with None
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # For rows where a middle level became None, shift down
    for idx in df.index:
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val).strip())
        # Reassign compacted
        for i in range(5):
            col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
            df.at[idx, col] = levels[i] if i < len(levels) else None
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # 4. Enforce sibling distinctiveness (<30% word overlap)
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[child_col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        # Build rename map: merge similar siblings
        subset = df[mask][parent_cols + [child_col]].drop_duplicates()
        groups = subset.groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        
        rename_map = {}
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():
            siblings = list(row[child_col])
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Sort by frequency (most common first)
            freq = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols + [child_col]).size().reset_index(name='cnt')
            
            # Iteratively merge similar siblings
            merged = True
            while merged:
                merged = False
                for i in range(len(siblings)):
                    for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                        if word_overlap_ratio(siblings[i], siblings[j]) >= 0.30:
                            # Keep the longer/more descriptive one
                            keep = siblings[i] if len(str(siblings[i])) >= len(str(siblings[j])) else siblings[j]
                            remove = siblings[j] if keep == siblings[i] else siblings[i]
                            parent_key = tuple(row[c] for c in parent_cols)
                            rename_map[(parent_key, remove)] = keep
                            siblings.remove(remove)
                            merged = True
                            break
                    if merged:
                        break
        
        # Apply renames
        if rename_map:
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                parent_key = tuple(df.at[idx, c] for c in parent_cols)
                child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                key = (parent_key, child)
                if key in rename_map:
                    df.at[idx, child_col] = rename_map[key]
    
    # 5. Enforce children count (3-20 per parent)
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[child_col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        child_counts = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique().reset_index()
        child_counts.columns = parent_cols + ['count']
        
        # Handle >20 children: merge least frequent into \"Other\" bucket
        for _, row in child_counts[child_counts['count'] > 20].iterrows():
            parent_filter = mask.copy()
            for c in parent_cols:
                parent_filter = parent_filter & (df[c] == row[c])
            
            children_freq = df[parent_filter][child_col].value_counts()
            if len(children_freq) > 20:
                keep = set(children_freq.index[:18])
                # Group the rest by first letter or merge
                for idx in df[parent_filter].index:
                    child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                    if child not in keep:
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = 'General Item'
    
    # 6. Final sanitization pass
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
        # Remove empty
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # ============================================================
    # OUTPUT
    # ============================================================
    
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                   'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                   'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[output_cols].copy()
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Full mapping: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    # Generate hierarchy with ALL intermediate paths
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    for _, row in full_df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row.get(f'unified_level_{i}')
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path = tuple(levels[:depth] + [None] * (5 - depth))
            hierarchy_rows.add(path)
    
    hierarchy_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    hierarchy_df = hierarchy_df.sort_values(
        [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)],
        na_position='last'
    ).reset_index(drop=True)
    hierarchy_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hierarchy_df)} rows\")
    
    # ============================================================
    # DIAGNOSTICS
    # ============================================================
    
    print(\"\\n=== DIAGNOSTICS ===\")
    print(f\"Sources: {full_df['source'].value_counts().to_dict()}\")
    l1_count = full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"L1 categories ({l1_count}): {sorted(full_df['unified_level_1'].unique())}\")
    
    # Special chars check
    special_count = 0
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        m = full_df[col].notna()
        has_special = full_df.loc[m, col].apply(lambda x: bool(re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s\\-]', str(x))))
        special_count += has_special.sum()
    print(f\"Special char violations: {special_count}\")
    
    # Parent word exclusion check
    parent_violations = 0
    total_pairs = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        m = full_df[child_col].notna()
        for idx in full_df[m].index:
            total_pairs += 1
            all_ancestor_words = set()
            for anc in range(1, level):
                anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc}'
                if pd.notna(full_df.at[idx, anc_col]):
                    all_ancestor_words |= words_of(full_df.at[idx, anc_col])
            child_words_set = words_of(full_df.at[idx, child_col])
            if child_words_set & all_ancestor_words:
                parent_violations += 1
    print(f\"Parent word violations: {parent_violations}/{total_pairs} ({parent_violations/max(total_pairs,1)*100:.1f}%)\")
    
    # Sibling distinctiveness check
    sibling_violations = 0
    sibling_total = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        m = full_df[child_col].notna()
        if not m.any():
            continue
        groups = full_df[m].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():
            siblings = row[child_col]
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                    sibling_total += 1
                    if word_overlap_ratio(siblings[i], siblings[j]) >= 0.30:
                        sibling_violations += 1
    print(f\"Sibling distinctiveness violations: {sibling_violations}/{sibling_total}\")
    
    # Children count check
    children_violations = 0
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        m = full_df[child_col].notna()
        if not m.any():
            continue
        counts = full_df[m].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique().reset_index()
        counts.columns = parent_cols + ['cnt']
        violations = counts[(counts['cnt'] < 3) | (counts['cnt'] > 20)]
        children_violations += len(violations)
        if len(violations) > 0:
            print(f\"  L{level} children count violations:\")
            for _, v in violations.head(10).iterrows():
                parent = ' > '.join(str(v[c]) for c in parent_cols)
                print(f\"    {parent}: {v['cnt']} children\")
    print(f\"Children count violations total: {children_violations}\")
    
    # Hierarchy coverage check
    full_paths = set()
    for _, row in full_df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row.get(f'unified_level_{i}')
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path = tuple(levels[:depth] + [None] * (5 - depth))
            full_paths.add(path)
    
    hier_paths = set()
    for _, row in hierarchy_df.iterrows():
        path = tuple(row[f'unified_level_{i}'] if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}']) else None for i in range(1, 6))
        hier_paths.add(path)
    
    missing = full_paths - hier_paths
    print(f\"Hierarchy coverage: {len(missing)} missing paths\")
    if missing:
        for p in list(missing)[:5]:
            print(f\"  Missing: {p}\")
    
    print(\"\\nDone"'!")


if __name__ == '"'__main__':
    main()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder v3
Key fixes: stop words in overlap, better L2 naming, children count enforcement
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer
import nltk
nltk.download('"'wordnet', quiet=True)

DATA_DIR = '/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()

STOP_WORDS = {'and', 'or', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'with', 'by', 'from'}

def sanitize_name(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = text.replace('&', 'and')
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s\\-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def lemmatize_text(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    words = str(text).lower().split()
    lemmatized = [lemmatizer.lemmatize(w) for w in words]
    return ' '.join(lemmatized)

def standardize_name(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = lemmatize_text(text)
    text = text.title()
    words = text.split()
    if len(words) > 5:
        words = words[:5]
    return ' '.join(words)

def content_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase content words (excluding stop words).\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(w for w in str(name).lower().split() if w not in STOP_WORDS)

def all_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get all lowercase words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(str(name).lower().split())

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    w1 = content_words(name1)
    w2 = content_words(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

L1_MAP = {
    'Appliances': 'Home And Kitchen',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts And Crafts',
    'Automotive': 'Vehicles And Parts',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby And Toddler',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health And Beauty',
    'Books': 'Books And Media',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Books And Media',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing And Accessories',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food And Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health And Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home And Kitchen',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Business And Industrial',
    'Movies & TV': 'Books And Media',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office Supplies',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Home And Garden',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sporting Goods',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Hardware And Tools',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys And Games',
    'Video Games': 'Books And Media',
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts And Crafts',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts And Crafts',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Vehicles And Parts',
    'baby products': 'Baby And Toddler',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing And Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics',
    'food & beverages': 'Food And Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health And Beauty',
    'home': 'Home And Kitchen',
    'home improvement': 'Hardware And Tools',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Clothing And Accessories',
    'media': 'Books And Media',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office Supplies',
    'other': 'Home And Kitchen',
    'patio & garden': 'Home And Garden',
    'pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sporting Goods',
    'toys & games': 'Toys And Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing And Accessories',
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing And Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts And Crafts',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby And Toddler',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Business And Industrial',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food And Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home And Kitchen',
    'Hardware': 'Hardware And Tools',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health And Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home And Garden',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing And Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health And Beauty',
    'Media': 'Books And Media',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office Supplies',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts And Crafts',
    'Software': 'Electronics',
    'Sporting Goods': 'Sporting Goods',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys And Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Vehicles And Parts',
}

def remove_overlap_words(child_name, ancestor_content_words):
    \"\"\"Remove content words from child that appear in ancestors. Keep stop words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name):
        return child_name
    words = str(child_name).split()
    filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() in STOP_WORDS or w.lower() not in ancestor_content_words]
    if not filtered:
        return child_name
    result = ' '.join(filtered)
    # If only stop words remain, keep original
    if not content_words(result):
        return child_name
    return result

def assign_levels(path, source):
    \"\"\"Assign unified levels 1-5 for a category path.\"\"\"
    parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
    
    l1 = L1_MAP.get(parts[0], standardize_name(parts[0]))
    l1_content = content_words(l1)
    
    # Collect remaining segments, skip those redundant with L1
    remaining = []
    for p in parts[1:]:
        std = standardize_name(p)
        if not std:
            continue
        std_content = content_words(std)
        if std_content and l1_content:
            overlap = len(std_content & l1_content) / len(std_content) if std_content else 0
            if overlap > 0.5:
                continue
        remaining.append(std)
    
    # Build levels: L1 + up to 4 more from remaining
    levels = [l1]
    ancestor_content = set(l1_content)
    
    for seg in remaining[:4]:
        cleaned = remove_overlap_words(seg, ancestor_content)
        if cleaned and cleaned.strip() and content_words(cleaned):
            levels.append(cleaned)
            ancestor_content |= content_words(cleaned)
        else:
            levels.append(seg)
            ancestor_content |= content_words(seg)
    
    return levels

def main():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    amz = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amz['source'] = 'amazon'
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    ggl = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    ggl['source'] = 'google'
    
    for d in [amz, fb, ggl]:
        d['category_path'] = d['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    df = pd.concat([amz[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     fb[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     ggl[['source', 'category_path']]], ignore_index=True)
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda p: len(p.split(' > ')))
    df = df[df['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    print(f\"  After depth filter: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Remove prefix paths per source
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    to_remove = set()
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_paths = set(df[df['source'] == source]['category_path'].unique())
        for path in source_paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in source_paths:
                    to_remove.add((source, prefix))
    
    mask = df.apply(lambda r: (r['source'], r['category_path']) not in to_remove, axis=1)
    df = df[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path']).reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Assign unified levels
    print(\"Assigning unified levels...\")
    levels_data = df.apply(lambda r: assign_levels(r['category_path'], r['source']), axis=1)
    
    for i in range(5):
        col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
        df[col] = levels_data.apply(lambda x: x[i] if i < len(x) else None)
    
    # Sanitize all names
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
    
    # ============================================================
    # PARENT WORD EXCLUSION (content words only, skip stop words)
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    for iteration in range(5):
        violations = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            mask = df[child_col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                ancestor_cw = set()
                for anc in range(1, level):
                    anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc}'
                    if pd.notna(df.at[idx, anc_col]):
                        ancestor_cw |= content_words(df.at[idx, anc_col])
                
                child_cw = content_words(df.at[idx, child_col])
                overlap = child_cw & ancestor_cw
                if overlap:
                    violations += 1
                    cleaned = remove_overlap_words(df.at[idx, child_col], ancestor_cw)
                    if cleaned and cleaned.strip() and content_words(cleaned):
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = cleaned
        
        print(f\"  Iteration {iteration+1}: {violations} violations\")
        if violations == 0:
            break
    
    # Clean up empty values
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # Compact levels (remove gaps)
    for idx in df.index:
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val).strip())
        for i in range(5):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{i+1}'] = levels[i] if i < len(levels) else None
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # ============================================================
    # SIBLING DISTINCTIVENESS
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[child_col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        subset = df[mask][parent_cols + [child_col]].drop_duplicates()
        groups = subset.groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        
        rename_map = {}
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():
            siblings = list(row[child_col])
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            merged = True
            while merged:
                merged = False
                for i in range(len(siblings)):
                    for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                        if word_overlap_ratio(siblings[i], siblings[j]) >= 0.30:
                            keep = siblings[i] if len(str(siblings[i])) >= len(str(siblings[j])) else siblings[j]
                            remove = siblings[j] if keep == siblings[i] else siblings[i]
                            parent_key = tuple(row[c] for c in parent_cols)
                            rename_map[(parent_key, remove)] = keep
                            siblings.remove(remove)
                            merged = True
                            break
                    if merged:
                        break
        
        if rename_map:
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                parent_key = tuple(df.at[idx, c] for c in parent_cols)
                child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                key = (parent_key, child)
                if key in rename_map:
                    df.at[idx, child_col] = rename_map[key]
    
    # ============================================================
    # CHILDREN COUNT ENFORCEMENT (3-20)
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    
    # Multiple passes: merge small groups, split large groups
    for pass_num in range(3):
        changes = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
            
            mask = df[child_col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            
            child_counts = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique().reset_index()
            child_counts.columns = parent_cols + ['count']
            
            # Handle >20: merge least frequent into most similar existing
            for _, row in child_counts[child_counts['count'] > 20].iterrows():
                parent_filter = mask.copy()
                for c in parent_cols:
                    parent_filter = parent_filter & (df[c] == row[c])
                
                children_freq = df[parent_filter][child_col].value_counts()
                if len(children_freq) > 20:
                    keep_set = set(children_freq.index[:19])
                    keep_list = list(keep_set)
                    
                    for idx in df[parent_filter].index:
                        child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                        if child not in keep_set:
                            # Find most similar kept sibling
                            best_match = keep_list[0]
                            best_sim = -1
                            for k in keep_list:
                                sim = word_overlap_ratio(child, k)
                                if sim > best_sim:
                                    best_sim = sim
                                    best_match = k
                            df.at[idx, child_col] = best_match
                            changes += 1
            
            # Handle <3: merge into parent (remove this level, shift children up)
            for _, row in child_counts[child_counts['count'] < 3].iterrows():
                parent_filter = mask.copy()
                for c in parent_cols:
                    parent_filter = parent_filter & (df[c] == row[c])
                
                # Get the children
                children = df[parent_filter][child_col].unique()
                if len(children) < 3:
                    # Merge children into parent by combining parent + child name
                    parent_name = row[parent_cols[-1]]
                    for idx in df[parent_filter].index:
                        child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                        # Shift deeper levels up
                        for deeper in range(level, 5):
                            next_col = f'unified_level_{deeper+1}'
                            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{deeper}'] = df.at[idx, next_col] if pd.notna(df.at[idx, next_col]) else None
                        df.at[idx, 'unified_level_5'] = None
                        changes += 1
        
        print(f\"  Pass {pass_num+1}: {changes} changes\")
        if changes == 0:
            break
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # Final sanitization
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # ============================================================
    # OUTPUT
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                   'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                   'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[output_cols].copy()
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Full mapping: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    # Generate hierarchy with ALL intermediate paths
    hierarchy_rows = set()
    for _, row in full_df.iterrows():
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = row.get(f'unified_level_{i}')
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val))
            else:
                break
        for depth in range(1, len(levels) + 1):
            path = tuple(levels[:depth] + [None] * (5 - depth))
            hierarchy_rows.add(path)
    
    hierarchy_df = pd.DataFrame(
        list(hierarchy_rows),
        columns=[f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    )
    hierarchy_df = hierarchy_df.sort_values(
        [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)],
        na_position='last'

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder v3
Key fixes: stop words in overlap, better L2 naming, children count enforcement
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer
import nltk
nltk.download('"'wordnet', quiet=True)

DATA_DIR = '/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()

STOP_WORDS = {'and', 'or', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'with', 'by', 'from'}

def sanitize_name(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = text.replace('&', 'and')
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s\\-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def lemmatize_text(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    words = str(text).lower().split()
    lemmatized = [lemmatizer.lemmatize(w) for w in words]
    return ' '.join(lemmatized)

def standardize_name(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = lemmatize_text(text)
    text = text.title()
    words = text.split()
    if len(words) > 5:
        words = words[:5]
    return ' '.join(words)

def content_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase content words (excluding stop words).\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(w for w in str(name).lower().split() if w not in STOP_WORDS)

def all_words(name):
    \"\"\"Get all lowercase words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(str(name).lower().split())

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    w1 = content_words(name1)
    w2 = content_words(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

L1_MAP = {

def remove_overlap_words(child_name, ancestor_content_words):
    \"\"\"Remove content words from child that appear in ancestors. Keep stop words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name):
        return child_name
    words = str(child_name).split()
    filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() in STOP_WORDS or w.lower() not in ancestor_content_words]
    if not filtered:
        return child_name
    result = ' '.join(filtered)
    # If only stop words remain, keep original
    if not content_words(result):
        return child_name
    return result

def assign_levels(path, source):
    \"\"\"Assign unified levels 1-5 for a category path.\"\"\"
    parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
    
    l1 = L1_MAP.get(parts[0], standardize_name(parts[0]))
    l1_content = content_words(l1)
    
    # Collect remaining segments, skip those redundant with L1
    remaining = []
    for p in parts[1:]:
        std = standardize_name(p)
        if not std:
            continue
        std_content = content_words(std)
        if std_content and l1_content:
            overlap = len(std_content & l1_content) / len(std_content) if std_content else 0
            if overlap > 0.5:
                continue
        remaining.append(std)
    
    # Build levels: L1 + up to 4 more from remaining
    levels = [l1]
    ancestor_content = set(l1_content)
    
    for seg in remaining[:4]:
        cleaned = remove_overlap_words(seg, ancestor_content)
        if cleaned and cleaned.strip() and content_words(cleaned):
            levels.append(cleaned)
            ancestor_content |= content_words(cleaned)
        else:
            levels.append(seg)
            ancestor_content |= content_words(seg)
    
    return levels

def main():

    df = pd.concat([amz[['source', 'category_path']],

    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda p: len(p.split(' > ')))
    df = df[df['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    print(f\"  After depth filter: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Remove prefix paths per source
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    to_remove = set()
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_paths = set(df[df['source'] == source]['category_path'].unique())
        for path in source_paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in source_paths:
                    to_remove.add((source, prefix))
    
    mask = df.apply(lambda r: (r['source'], r['category_path']) not in to_remove, axis=1)
    df = df[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path']).reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Assign unified levels
    print(\"Assigning unified levels...\")
    levels_data = df.apply(lambda r: assign_levels(r['category_path'], r['source']), axis=1)
    
    for i in range(5):
        col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
        df[col] = levels_data.apply(lambda x: x[i] if i < len(x) else None)
    
    # Sanitize all names
    for i in range(1, 6):

            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'

                continue
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                ancestor_cw = set()
                for anc in range(1, level):
                    anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc}'
                    if pd.notna(df.at[idx, anc_col]):
                        ancestor_cw |= content_words(df.at[idx, anc_col])
                
                child_cw = content_words(df.at[idx, child_col])
                overlap = child_cw & ancestor_cw
                if overlap:
                    violations += 1
                    cleaned = remove_overlap_words(df.at[idx, child_col], ancestor_cw)
                    if cleaned and cleaned.strip() and content_words(cleaned):
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = cleaned
        
        print(f\"  Iteration {iteration+1}: {violations} violations\")
        if violations == 0:
            break
    
    # Clean up empty values
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # Compact levels (remove gaps)
    for idx in df.index:
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):

    for level in range(2, 6):

        mask = df[child_col].notna()

        subset = df[mask][parent_cols + [child_col]].drop_duplicates()
        groups = subset.groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        
        rename_map = {}
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():

                            parent_key = tuple(row[c] for c in parent_cols)

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder v4
Key fix: Remove '"'and' from all category names to avoid parent-child word overlap
\"\"\"

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer
import nltk
nltk.download('wordnet', quiet=True)

DATA_DIR = '/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()

# Words to strip from ALL category names to avoid parent-child overlap
STRIP_WORDS = {'and', 'or', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'with', 'by', 'from'}

def sanitize_name(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = str(text).strip()
    text = text.replace('&', ' ')
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s\\-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def lemmatize_text(text):
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    words = str(text).lower().split()
    lemmatized = [lemmatizer.lemmatize(w) for w in words]
    return ' '.join(lemmatized)

def standardize_name(text):
    \"\"\"Full standardization: sanitize + lemmatize + remove stop words + title case + max 5 words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text):
        return text
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = lemmatize_text(text)
    # Remove stop/filler words
    words = [w for w in text.split() if w.lower() not in STRIP_WORDS]
    if not words:
        # If all words stripped, keep original minus special chars
        words = text.split()
    # Title case, max 5 words
    words = [w.title() for w in words[:5]]
    return ' '.join(words)

def words_of(name):
    if pd.isna(name) or not name:
        return set()
    return set(str(name).lower().split())

def word_overlap_ratio(name1, name2):
    w1 = words_of(name1)
    w2 = words_of(name2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# ============================================================
# L1 MAPPING - No \"And\" in names"'!
# ============================================================

L1_MAP = {
    '"'Appliances': 'Home Kitchen',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts Crafts',
    'Automotive': 'Vehicles Parts',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby Toddler',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health Beauty',
    'Books': 'Books Media',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Books Media',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home Kitchen',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Business Industrial',
    'Movies & TV': 'Books Media',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office Supplies',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Home Garden',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sporting Goods',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Hardware Tools',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys Games',
    'Video Games': 'Books Media',
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts Crafts',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts Crafts',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Vehicles Parts',
    'baby products': 'Baby Toddler',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics',
    'food & beverages': 'Food Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health Beauty',
    'home': 'Home Kitchen',
    'home improvement': 'Hardware Tools',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'media': 'Books Media',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office Supplies',
    'other': 'Home Kitchen',
    'patio & garden': 'Home Garden',
    'pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sporting Goods',
    'toys & games': 'Toys Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts Crafts',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby Toddler',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Business Industrial',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home Kitchen',
    'Hardware': 'Hardware Tools',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home Garden',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health Beauty',
    'Media': 'Books Media',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office Supplies',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts Crafts',
    'Software': 'Electronics',
    'Sporting Goods': 'Sporting Goods',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Vehicles Parts',
}

def remove_ancestor_words(child_name, ancestor_words_set):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child that appear in any ancestor.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(child_name):
        return child_name
    words = str(child_name).split()
    filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words_set]
    if not filtered:
        return child_name
    return ' '.join(filtered)

def assign_levels(path, source):
    parts = [p.strip() for p in path.split(' > ')]
    
    l1 = L1_MAP.get(parts[0], standardize_name(parts[0]))
    l1_words = words_of(l1)
    
    # Collect remaining segments, skip those redundant with L1
    remaining = []
    for p in parts[1:]:
        std = standardize_name(p)
        if not std:
            continue
        std_words = words_of(std)
        if std_words and l1_words:
            overlap = len(std_words & l1_words) / len(std_words) if std_words else 0
            if overlap > 0.5:
                continue
        remaining.append(std)
    
    # Build levels with ancestor word removal
    levels = [l1]
    ancestor_words = set(l1_words)
    
    for seg in remaining[:4]:
        cleaned = remove_ancestor_words(seg, ancestor_words)
        if cleaned and cleaned.strip() and words_of(cleaned):
            levels.append(cleaned)
            ancestor_words |= words_of(cleaned)
        else:
            levels.append(seg)
            ancestor_words |= words_of(seg)
    
    return levels

def main():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    amz = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amz['source'] = 'amazon'
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    ggl = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    ggl['source'] = 'google'
    
    for d in [amz, fb, ggl]:
        d['category_path'] = d['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    df = pd.concat([amz[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     fb[['source', 'category_path']], 
                     ggl[['source', 'category_path']]], ignore_index=True)
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    df['depth'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda p: len(p.split(' > ')))
    df = df[df['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    print(f\"  After depth filter: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Remove prefix paths per source
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    to_remove = set()
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_paths = set(df[df['source'] == source]['category_path'].unique())
        for path in source_paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in source_paths:
                    to_remove.add((source, prefix))
    
    mask = df.apply(lambda r: (r['source'], r['category_path']) not in to_remove, axis=1)
    df = df[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path']).reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # Assign unified levels
    print(\"Assigning unified levels...\")
    levels_data = df.apply(lambda r: assign_levels(r['category_path'], r['source']), axis=1)
    
    for i in range(5):
        col = f'unified_level_{i+1}'
        df[col] = levels_data.apply(lambda x: x[i] if i < len(x) else None)
    
    # Sanitize all names
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
    
    # ============================================================
    # PARENT WORD EXCLUSION - ALL words
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    for iteration in range(5):
        violations = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            mask = df[child_col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                ancestor_words = set()
                for anc in range(1, level):
                    anc_col = f'unified_level_{anc}'
                    if pd.notna(df.at[idx, anc_col]):
                        ancestor_words |= words_of(df.at[idx, anc_col])
                
                child_words = words_of(df.at[idx, child_col])
                overlap = child_words & ancestor_words
                if overlap:
                    violations += 1
                    cleaned = remove_ancestor_words(df.at[idx, child_col], ancestor_words)
                    if cleaned and cleaned.strip() and words_of(cleaned):
                        df.at[idx, child_col] = cleaned
        
        print(f\"  Iteration {iteration+1}: {violations} violations\")
        if violations == 0:
            break
    
    # Clean up empty values and compact
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    for idx in df.index:
        levels = []
        for i in range(1, 6):
            val = df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{i}']
            if pd.notna(val) and str(val).strip():
                levels.append(str(val).strip())
        for i in range(5):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{i+1}'] = levels[i] if i < len(levels) else None
    
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # ============================================================
    # SIBLING DISTINCTIVENESS
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[child_col].notna()
        if not mask.any():
            continue
        
        subset = df[mask][parent_cols + [child_col]].drop_duplicates()
        groups = subset.groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].apply(lambda x: list(set(x))).reset_index()
        
        rename_map = {}
        for _, row in groups.iterrows():
            siblings = list(row[child_col])
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            merged = True
            while merged:
                merged = False
                for i in range(len(siblings)):
                    for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                        if word_overlap_ratio(siblings[i], siblings[j]) >= 0.30:
                            keep = siblings[i] if len(str(siblings[i])) >= len(str(siblings[j])) else siblings[j]
                            remove = siblings[j] if keep == siblings[i] else siblings[i]
                            parent_key = tuple(row[c] for c in parent_cols)
                            rename_map[(parent_key, remove)] = keep
                            siblings.remove(remove)
                            merged = True
                            break
                    if merged:
                        break
        
        if rename_map:
            for idx in df[mask].index:
                parent_key = tuple(df.at[idx, c] for c in parent_cols)
                child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                key = (parent_key, child)
                if key in rename_map:
                    df.at[idx, child_col] = rename_map[key]
    
    # ============================================================
    # CHILDREN COUNT ENFORCEMENT (3-20)
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    for pass_num in range(3):
        changes = 0
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
            
            mask = df[child_col].notna()
            if not mask.any():
                continue
            
            child_counts = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique().reset_index()
            child_counts.columns = parent_cols + ['count']
            
            # Handle >20: merge least frequent into most similar
            for _, row in child_counts[child_counts['count'] > 20].iterrows():
                parent_filter = mask.copy()
                for c in parent_cols:
                    parent_filter = parent_filter & (df[c] == row[c])
                
                children_freq = df[parent_filter][child_col].value_counts()
                if len(children_freq) > 20:
                    keep_set = set(children_freq.index[:19])
                    keep_list = list(keep_set)
                    
                    for idx in df[parent_filter].index:
                        child = df.at[idx, child_col]
                        if child not in keep_set:
                            best_match = keep_list[0]
                            best_sim = -1
                            for k in keep_list:
                                sim = word_overlap_ratio(child, k)
                                if sim > best_sim:
                                    best_sim = sim
                                    best_match = k
                            df.at[idx, child_col] = best_match
                            changes += 1
            
            # Handle <3: collapse this level into parent
            for _, row in child_counts[child_counts['count'] < 3].iterrows():
                parent_filter = mask.copy()
                for c in parent_cols:
                    parent_filter = parent_filter & (df[c] == row[c])
                
                children = df[parent_filter][child_col].unique()
                if len(children) < 3:
                    for idx in df[parent_filter].index:
                        # Shift deeper levels up
                        for deeper in range(level, 5):
                            next_col = f'unified_level_{deeper+1}'
                            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{deeper}'] = df.at[idx, next_col] if pd.notna(df.at[idx, next_col]) else None
                        df.at[idx, 'unified_level_5'] = None
                        changes += 1
        
        print(f\"  Pass {pass_num+1}: {changes} changes\")
        if changes == 0:
            break
    
    # Recalculate depth
    df['depth'] = df.apply(
        lambda row: sum(1 for i in range(1, 6) if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{i}'])),
        axis=1
    ).astype(int)
    
    # Final sanitization
    for i in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{i}'
        mask = df[col].notna()
        df.loc[mask, col] = df.loc[mask, col].apply(sanitize_name)
        df.loc[mask & (df[col].str.strip() == ''), col] = None
    
    # ============================================================
    # OUTPUT
    # ============================================================
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
