# =============================================================================
# Text utilities
# =============================================================================

def sanitize(text):
    if pd.isna(text) or text is None:
        return None
    text = str(text)
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', '', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def lem(word):
    return lemmatizer.lemmatize(word.lower())

def make_name(raw, ancestor_words=None):
    \"\"\"Create sanitized, lemmatized, pipe-separated name excluding ancestor words.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(raw) or raw is None:
        return None
    s = sanitize(raw)
    if not s:
        return None
    words = s.split()
    words = [lem(w).title() for w in words if w.strip()]
    if ancestor_words:
        filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
        if filtered:
            words = filtered
    words = words[:5]
    return ' | '.join(words) if words else None

def words_of(name):
    if pd.isna(name) or name is None:
        return set()
    return set(w.strip().lower() for w in str(name).split('|'))

def overlap_ratio(n1, n2):
    w1, w2 = words_of(n1), words_of(n2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# =============================================================================
# Load & prepare
# =============================================================================

def load_data():
    amz = pd.read_csv('/root/data/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amz['source'] = 'amazon'
    
    fb = pd.read_csv('/root/data/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    fb = fb[['category_path', 'source']]
    
    ggl = pd.read_csv('/root/data/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    ggl['source'] = 'google'
    
    combined = pd.concat([amz, fb, ggl], ignore_index=True)
    combined['category_path'] = combined['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    # Normalize to title case for cross-source matching
    def title_case_path(p):
        parts = str(p).split(' > ')
        return ' > '.join(part.strip().title() for part in parts)
    
    combined['category_path'] = combined['category_path'].apply(title_case_path)
    
    combined['parts'] = combined['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    combined['depth'] = combined['parts'].str.len()
    combined = combined[combined['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    
    for i in range(5):
        combined[f'src_l{i+1}'] = combined['parts'].apply(lambda x: x[i].strip() if i < len(x) else None)
    
    return combined

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove prefix paths globally. Keep all source rows for leaf paths.\"\"\"
    all_paths = set(df['category_path'].unique())
    to_remove = set()
    
    for path in all_paths:
        parts = path.split(' > ')
        for i in range(1, len(parts)):
            prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
            if prefix in all_paths:
                to_remove.add(prefix)
    
    return df[~df['category_path'].isin(to_remove)].copy()

# =============================================================================
# L1 mapping
# =============================================================================

L1_MAP = {}

_l1_groups = {
    'Pet | Supply': ['Pet Supplies', 'Animals & Pet Supplies'],
    'Vehicle | Part': ['Automotive', 'Auto Parts & Accessories', 'Vehicles & Parts'],
    'Baby | Product': ['Baby Products', 'Baby & Toddler'],
    'Apparel | Accessory': ['Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry', 'Clothing & Accessories',
                            'Apparel & Accessories', 'Jewelry & Watches',
                            'Luggage & Bags', 'Travel & Luggage'],
    'Electronic | Computing': ['Electronics', 'Cell Phones & Accessories',
                               'Cameras & Optics', 'Video Games', 'Software'],
    'Food | Beverage': ['Grocery & Gourmet Food', 'Food & Beverages',
                        'Food, Beverages & Tobacco'],
    'Health | Wellness': ['Health & Household', 'Beauty & Personal Care',
                          'Health & Beauty', 'Mature'],
    'Home | Furnishing': ['Home & Kitchen', 'Home', 'Furniture', 'Appliances'],
    'Hardware | Improvement': ['Tools & Home Improvement', 'Home Improvement', 'Hardware'],
    'Garden | Patio': ['Patio, Lawn & Garden', 'Patio & Garden', 'Home & Garden'],
    'Sporting | Recreation': ['Sports & Outdoors', 'Sporting Goods'],
    'Toy | Game': ['Toys & Games'],
    'Art | Craft': ['Arts, Crafts & Sewing', 'Arts & Crafts', 'Arts & Entertainment',
                    'Antiques & Collectibles', 'Religious & Ceremonial'],
    'Book | Media': ['Books', 'Cds & Vinyl', 'Movies & Tv', 'Media'],
    'Office | Supply': ['Office Products', 'Office Supplies'],
    'Industrial | Commercial': ['Industrial & Scientific', 'Business & Industrial'],
    'Musical | Instrument': ['Musical Instruments'],
    'General | Merchandise': ['Other'],
}

for unified, sources in _l1_groups.items():
    for s in sources:
        L1_MAP[s.title()] = unified

# =============================================================================
# Grouping logic for L2-L5
# =============================================================================

def group_and_name(raw_values, value_counts, ancestor_words, min_children=3, max_children=20, fallback='Misc'):
    \"\"\"
    Given a list of raw category names, create unified names.
    Groups small categories together, enforces min/max children.
    Returns dict: raw_value -> unified_name
    \"\"\"
    if not raw_values:
        return {}
    
    # Create initial name mapping
    name_map = {}
    for raw in raw_values:
        name = make_name(raw, ancestor_words)
        if name:
            name_map[raw] = name
        else:
            name_map[raw] = fallback.title()
    
    # If too many unique names, merge least frequent
    unique_names = set(name_map.values())
    if len(unique_names) > max_children:
        # Sort by frequency
        freq = {}
        for raw, name in name_map.items():
            cnt = value_counts.get(raw, 0)
            freq[raw] = cnt
        
        sorted_raws = sorted(name_map.keys(), key=lambda r: freq.get(r, 0), reverse=True)
        
        # Keep top (max_children - 1) unique names by frequency
        kept_names = set()
        kept_raws = set()
        for raw in sorted_raws:
            name = name_map[raw]
            if name not in kept_names:
                if len(kept_names) < max_children - 1:
                    kept_names.add(name)
                    kept_raws.add(raw)
                else:
                    name_map[raw] = f'Other | {fallback}'
            else:
                kept_raws.add(raw)
    
    # Fix sibling overlap
    name_map = fix_siblings(name_map)
    
    return name_map

def fix_siblings(name_map):
    \"\"\"Ensure < 30% word overlap between sibling unified names.\"\"\"
    # Get unique names and their raw keys
    name_to_raws = defaultdict(list)
    for raw, name in name_map.items():
        name_to_raws[name].append(raw)
    
    unique_names = list(name_to_raws.keys())
    
    for i in range(len(unique_names)):
        for j in range(i + 1, len(unique_names)):
            n1, n2 = unique_names[i], unique_names[j]
            if overlap_ratio(n1, n2) >= 0.3:
                # Try to differentiate n2 by adding a word from its raw keys
                raws = name_to_raws[n2]
                if raws:
                    raw_words = sanitize(raws[0]).split() if sanitize(raws[0]) else []
                    lemmed = [lem(w).title() for w in raw_words]
                    w1 = words_of(n1)
                    w2 = words_of(n2)
                    
                    for lw in lemmed:
                        if lw.lower() not in w1 and lw.lower() not in w2:
                            new_name = n2 + ' | ' + lw
                            # Trim to 5 words
                            parts = new_name.split(' | ')[:5]
                            new_name = ' | '.join(parts)
                            
                            # Update all raws that had n2
                            for r in name_to_raws[n2]:
                                name_map[r] = new_name
                            
                            # Update tracking
                            name_to_raws[new_name] = name_to_raws.pop(n2)
                            unique_names[j] = new_name
                            break
    
    return name_map

# =============================================================================
# Build taxonomy
# =============================================================================

def build_taxonomy(df):
    # Map L1
    df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    unmapped = df[df['unified_level_1'].isna()]['src_l1'].unique()
    if len(unmapped) > 0:
        print(f\"  WARNING: Unmapped L1: {unmapped}\")
        for u in unmapped:
            L1_MAP[u] = 'General | Merchandise'
        df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    
    for lvl in range(2, 6):
        df[f'unified_level_{lvl}'] = None
    
    # Build L2-L5 hierarchically
    for ul1 in sorted(df['unified_level_1'].unique()):
        m1 = df['unified_level_1'] == ul1
        aw1 = words_of(ul1)
        
        l2_vals = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].dropna().unique()
        l2_counts = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].value_counts().to_dict()
        l2_map = group_and_name(l2_vals, l2_counts, aw1, fallback='Subcategory')
        
        for raw2, name2 in l2_map.items():
            m2 = m1 & (df['src_l2'] == raw2)
            df.loc[m2, 'unified_level_2'] = name2
            aw2 = aw1 | words_of(name2)
            
            l3_vals = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].dropna().unique()
            l3_counts = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].value_counts().to_dict()
            l3_map = group_and_name(l3_vals, l3_counts, aw2, fallback='Subtype')
            
            for raw3, name3 in l3_map.items():
                m3 = m2 & (df['src_l3'] == raw3)
                df.loc[m3, 'unified_level_3'] = name3
                aw3 = aw2 | words_of(name3)
                
                l4_vals = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].dropna().unique()
                l4_counts = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].value_counts().to_dict()
                l4_map = group_and_name(l4_vals, l4_counts, aw3, fallback='Variant')
                
                for raw4, name4 in l4_map.items():
                    m4 = m3 & (df['src_l4'] == raw4)
                    df.loc[m4, 'unified_level_4'] = name4
                    aw4 = aw3 | words_of(name4)
                    
                    l5_vals = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].dropna().unique()
                    l5_counts = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].value_counts().to_dict()
                    l5_map = group_and_name(l5_vals, l5_counts, aw4, fallback='Kind')
                    
                    for raw5, name5 in l5_map.items():
                        m5 = m4 & (df['src_l5'] == raw5)
                        df.loc[m5, 'unified_level_5'] = name5
    
    return df

# =============================================================================
# Final sanitization
# =============================================================================

def final_clean(df):
    for col in [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]:
        def clean(val):
            if pd.isna(val) or val is None:
                return None
            parts = str(val).split('|')
            cleaned = []
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                p = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', '', p)
                p = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', p).strip()
                if p:
                    cleaned.append(p)
            return ' | '.join(cleaned) if cleaned else None
        df[col] = df[col].apply(clean)
    return df

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================

def main():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows (depth <= 5)\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows after prefix removal\")
    
    # Verify cross-source overlap
    path_src = df.groupby('category_path')['source'].nunique()
    multi = (path_src > 1).sum()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {multi}\")
    
    print(\"Building taxonomy...\")
    df = build_taxonomy(df)
    
    print(\"Final cleanup...\")
    df = final_clean(df)
    
    # Verify no special chars
    for col in [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]:
        for val in df[col].dropna().unique():
            for word in str(val).split(' | '):
                if re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', word.strip()):
                    print(f\"  WARN special char in {col}: '{val}'\")
    
    # Output
    out_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[out_cols].copy()
    full_df['depth'] = full_df['depth'].astype(int)
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Saved full: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    hier_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hier = full_df[hier_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(hier_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    hier.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Saved hierarchy: {len(hier)} rows\")
    
    # Stats
    print(f\"\\nL1 count: {full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")
    
    # Children count check
    for lvl in range(1, 5):
        pcols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl + 1)]
        ccol = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}'
        grp = full_df.dropna(subset=[ccol]).groupby(pcols)[ccol].nunique()
        violations = grp[(grp < 3) | (grp > 20)]
        if len(violations) > 0:
            print(f\"  L{lvl}->L{lvl+1}: {len(violations)} violations (of {len(grp)} groups)\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

                                name_map[r] = new_name
                            
                            # Update tracking
                            name_to_raws[new_name] = name_to_raws.pop(n2)
                            unique_names[j] = new_name
                            break
    
    return name_map

# =============================================================================
# Build taxonomy
# =============================================================================

def build_taxonomy(df):
    # Map L1
    df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    unmapped = df[df['unified_level_1'].isna()]['src_l1'].unique()
    if len(unmapped) > 0:
        print(f\"  WARNING: Unmapped L1: {unmapped}\")
        for u in unmapped:
            L1_MAP[u] = 'General | Merchandise'
        df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    
    for lvl in range(2, 6):
        df[f'unified_level_{lvl}'] = None
    
    # Build L2-L5 hierarchically
    for ul1 in sorted(df['unified_level_1'].unique()):
        m1 = df['unified_level_1'] == ul1
        aw1 = words_of(ul1)
        
        l2_vals = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].dropna().unique()
        l2_counts = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].value_counts().to_dict()
        l2_map = group_and_name(l2_vals, l2_counts, aw1, fallback='Subcategory')
        
        for raw2, name2 in l2_map.items():
            m2 = m1 & (df['src_l2'] == raw2)
            df.loc[m2, 'unified_level_2'] = name2
            aw2 = aw1 | words_of(name2)
            
            l3_vals = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].dropna().unique()
            l3_counts = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].value_counts().to_dict()
            l3_map = group_and_name(l3_vals, l3_counts, aw2, fallback='Subtype')
            
            for raw3, name3 in l3_map.items():
                m3 = m2 & (df['src_l3'] == raw3)
                df.loc[m3, 'unified_level_3'] = name3
                aw3 = aw2 | words_of(name3)
                
                l4_vals = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].dropna().unique()
                l4_counts = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].value_counts().to_dict()
                l4_map = group_and_name(l4_vals, l4_counts, aw3, fallback='Variant')
                
                for raw4, name4 in l4_map.items():
                    m4 = m3 & (df['src_l4'] == raw4)
                    df.loc[m4, 'unified_level_4'] = name4
                    aw4 = aw3 | words_of(name4)
                    
                    l5_vals = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].dropna().unique()
                    l5_counts = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].value_counts().to_dict()
                    l5_map = group_and_name(l5_vals, l5_counts, aw4, fallback='Kind')
                    
                    for raw5, name5 in l5_map.items():
                        m5 = m4 & (df['src_l5'] == raw5)
                        df.loc[m5, 'unified_level_5'] = name5
    
    return df

# =============================================================================
# Final sanitization
# =============================================================================

def final_clean(df):
    for col in [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]:
        def clean(val):
            if pd.isna(val) or val is None:
                return None
            parts = str(val).split('|')
            cleaned = []
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                p = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', '', p)
                p = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', p).strip()
                if p:
                    cleaned.append(p)
            return ' | '.join(cleaned) if cleaned else None
        df[col] = df[col].apply(clean)
    return df

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================

def main():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows (depth <= 5)\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows after prefix removal\")
    
    # Verify cross-source overlap
    path_src = df.groupby('category_path')['source'].nunique()
    multi = (path_src > 1).sum()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {multi}\")
    
    print(\"Building taxonomy...\")
    df = build_taxonomy(df)
    
    print(\"Final cleanup...\")
    df = final_clean(df)
    
    # Verify no special chars
    for col in [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]:
        for val in df[col].dropna().unique():
            for word in str(val).split(' | '):
                if re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', word.strip()):
                    print(f\"  WARN special char in {col}: '{val}'\")
    
    # Output
    out_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[out_cols].copy()
    full_df['depth'] = full_df['depth'].astype(int)
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Saved full: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    hier_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hier = full_df[hier_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(hier_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    hier.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Saved hierarchy: {len(hier)} rows\")
    
    # Stats
    print(f\"\\nL1 count: {full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")
    
    # Children count check
    for lvl in range(1, 5):
        pcols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl + 1)]
        ccol = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}'
        grp = full_df.dropna(subset=[ccol]).groupby(pcols)[ccol].nunique()
        violations = grp[(grp < 3) | (grp > 20)]
        if len(violations) > 0:
            print(f\"  L{lvl}->L{lvl+1}: {len(violations)} violations (of {len(grp)} groups)\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

            # Check all ancestor levels

                parent_col = f'unified_level_{parent_level}'
                if pd.notna(row[parent_col]):
                    parent_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in str(row[parent_col]).split('|'))
                    if child_words & parent_words:
                        violations += 1
                        break
    
    violation_rate = violations / total if total > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.1, f\"Parent word exclusion violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total})\"

    \"\"\"Test that siblings have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total_pairs = 0
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        groups = df.dropna(subset=[col]).groupby(parent_cols)[col].apply(lambda x: list(x.unique()))
        
        for siblings in groups:
            if len(siblings) < 2:
                continue
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                w1 = set(siblings[i].lower().split(' | '))
                for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                    w2 = set(siblings[j].lower().split(' | '))
                    total_pairs += 1
                    if w1 and w2:
                        overlap = len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))
                        if overlap >= 0.3:
                            violations += 1
    
    violation_rate = violations / total_pairs if total_pairs > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.1, f\"Sibling overlap violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total_pairs})\"

    \"\"\"Test 3-20 children per parent.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0

    for level in range(1, 5):
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level + 1)]
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level + 1}'
        
        groups = df.dropna(subset=[child_col]).groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique()
        
        for count in groups:
            total += 1
            if count < 3 or count > 20:
                violations += 1
    
    violation_rate = violations / total if total > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.15, f\"Children count violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total})\"

def test_path_representativeness():
    \"\"\"Test that parent names are representative of children (70%+).\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for level in range(1, 5):
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level + 1}'
        
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level + 1)]
        
        groups = df.dropna(subset=[child_col]).groupby(parent_cols)
        
        for parent_path, group in groups:
            total += 1
            if isinstance(parent_path, str):
                parent_name = parent_path
            else:
                parent_name = parent_path[-1]
            
            parent_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in parent_name.split('|'))
            
            # Check what fraction of source category paths contain a parent word
            source_paths = group['category_path'].unique()
            matching = 0
            for sp in source_paths:
                sp_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in re.split(r'[\\s>]+', sp))
                if parent_words & sp_words:
                    matching += 1
            
            ratio = matching / len(source_paths) if source_paths.size > 0 else 0
            if ratio < 0.7:
                violations += 1
    
    violation_rate = violations / total if total > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.15, f\"Representativeness violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total})\"

    \"\"\"Test that hierarchy covers all paths in full mapping.\"\"\"
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)

    # Merge to check coverage
    merged = full_combos.merge(hier_df, how='left', indicator=True)
    missing = (merged['_merge'] == 'left_only').sum()
    
    assert missing == 0, f\"{missing} paths in full mapping not found in hierarchy\"

def test_hierarchy_depth_consistency():
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

def test_source_balance():

    # No source should be less than 10% of total

    assert min_ratio > 0.1, f\"Smallest source has only {min_ratio:.1%} of records\"

    \"\"\"No L1 category should have 0 records.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    l1_counts = df['unified_level_1'].value_counts()
    assert (l1_counts > 0).all()

    for source in expected_sources:
        count = (df['source'] == source).sum()
        assert count > 0

def test_depth_filtering():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    assert df['depth'].min() >= 1
    assert df['depth'].max() <= 5
    assert df['unified_level_1'].notna().all()

def test_prefix_removal():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    paths = set(df['category_path'].unique())
    prefix_violations = []
    for path in paths:
        parts = path.split(' > ')
        for i in range(1, len(parts)):
            prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
            if prefix in paths:
                prefix_violations.append((prefix, path))
    violation_rate = len(prefix_violations) / len(paths) if len(paths) > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.05

def test_cross_source_deduplication():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    path_counts = df['category_path'].value_counts()
    multi_source_paths = (path_counts > 1).sum()
    assert multi_source_paths > 0

# TIER 3
def test_hierarchical_structure():
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    level_1_count = df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    assert 10 <= level_1_count <= 20
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            prev_col = f'unified_level_{level-1}'
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                assert pd.notna(row[prev_col])

def test_category_naming_constraints():
    \"\"\"Test naming: pipe separator, max 5 words.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    for col in ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']:
        for val in df[col].dropna().unique():
            words = val.split(' | ')
            assert len(words) <= 5, f\"{col} '{val}' has more than 5 words\"
            # Check pipe separator format
            assert ' | ' in val or len(words) == 1, f\"{col} '{val}' doesn't use ' | ' separator\"

def test_special_characters_removed():
    \"\"\"Test that special characters are removed from unified names.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    for col in ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']:
        for val in df[col].dropna().unique():
            # Split by pipe separator, check each word
            words = val.split(' | ')
            for word in words:
                word = word.strip()
                # Only alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens allowed
                assert not re.search(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', word), f\"Special char in {col}: '{val}' word='{word}'\"

def test_lemmatization_applied():
    \"\"\"Test that lemmatization is applied.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer
    lem = WordNetLemmatizer()
    
    all_words = []
    for col in ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']:
        for val in df[col].dropna().unique():
            words = val.split(' | ')
            all_words.extend([w.strip() for w in words])
    
    lemmatized_count = 0
    for word in all_words:
        lemma = lem.lemmatize(word.lower()).title()
        if lemma == word:
            lemmatized_count += 1
    
    ratio = lemmatized_count / len(all_words) if all_words else 0
    assert ratio > 0.8, f\"Lemmatization ratio too low: {ratio}\"

def test_parent_word_exclusion():
    \"\"\"Test that child names don't contain parent words.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row[col]):
                continue
            total += 1
            child_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in str(row[col]).split('|'))
            
            # Check all ancestor levels
            for parent_level in range(1, level):
                parent_col = f'unified_level_{parent_level}'
                if pd.notna(row[parent_col]):
                    parent_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in str(row[parent_col]).split('|'))
                    if child_words & parent_words:
                        violations += 1
                        break
    
    violation_rate = violations / total if total > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.1, f\"Parent word exclusion violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total})\"

def test_sibling_distinctiveness():
    \"\"\"Test that siblings have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total_pairs = 0
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        groups = df.dropna(subset=[col]).groupby(parent_cols)[col].apply(lambda x: list(x.unique()))
        
        for siblings in groups:
            if len(siblings) < 2:
                continue
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                w1 = set(siblings[i].lower().split(' | '))
                for j in range(i+1, len(siblings)):
                    w2 = set(siblings[j].lower().split(' | '))
                    total_pairs += 1
                    if w1 and w2:
                        overlap = len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))
                        if overlap >= 0.3:
                            violations += 1
    
    violation_rate = violations / total_pairs if total_pairs > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.1, f\"Sibling overlap violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total_pairs})\"

def test_children_count_limit():
    \"\"\"Test 3-20 children per parent.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for level in range(1, 5):
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level + 1)]
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level + 1}'
        
        groups = df.dropna(subset=[child_col]).groupby(parent_cols)[child_col].nunique()
        
        for count in groups:
            total += 1
            if count < 3 or count > 20:
                violations += 1
    
    violation_rate = violations / total if total > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.15, f\"Children count violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total})\"

def test_path_representativeness():
    \"\"\"Test that parent names are representative of children (70%+).\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for level in range(1, 5):
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level + 1}'
        
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level + 1)]
        
        groups = df.dropna(subset=[child_col]).groupby(parent_cols)
        
        for parent_path, group in groups:
            total += 1
            if isinstance(parent_path, str):
                parent_name = parent_path
            else:
                parent_name = parent_path[-1]
            
            parent_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in parent_name.split('|'))
            
            # Check what fraction of source category paths contain a parent word
            source_paths = group['category_path'].unique()
            matching = 0
            for sp in source_paths:
                sp_words = set(w.strip().lower() for w in re.split(r'[\\s>]+', sp))
                if parent_words & sp_words:
                    matching += 1
            
            ratio = matching / len(source_paths) if source_paths.size > 0 else 0
            if ratio < 0.7:
                violations += 1
    
    violation_rate = violations / total if total > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.15, f\"Representativeness violation rate: {violation_rate:.2%} ({violations}/{total})\"

def test_hierarchy_coverage():
    \"\"\"Test that hierarchy covers all paths in full mapping.\"\"\"
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    hier_df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    
    # Every unique (L1..L5) combo in full should exist in hierarchy
    full_combos = full_df[['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']].drop_duplicates()
    
    # Merge to check coverage
    merged = full_combos.merge(hier_df, how='left', indicator=True)
    missing = (merged['_merge'] == 'left_only').sum()
    
    assert missing == 0, f\"{missing} paths in full mapping not found in hierarchy\"

def test_hierarchy_depth_consistency():
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{level}']):
                assert pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{level-1}'])

def test_pyramid_distribution():
    \"\"\"Test pyramid structure: more items at deeper levels.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    counts = []
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        counts.append(df[col].notna().sum())
    
    # Generally should decrease or be pyramid-like
    # At minimum, L1 count < L2 count
    assert counts[0] <= counts[1], f\"L1 ({counts[0]}) should be <= L2 ({counts[1]})\"

def test_cluster_size_balance():
    \"\"\"Test that cluster sizes are reasonably balanced.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    l1_counts = df['unified_level_1'].value_counts()
    
    # No single L1 should have more than 30% of all records
    max_ratio = l1_counts.max() / len(df)
    assert max_ratio < 0.3, f\"Largest L1 cluster has {max_ratio:.1%} of records\"

def test_mapping_completeness():
    \"\"\"All rows should have unified_level_1.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    assert df['unified_level_1'].notna().all()

def test_source_balance():
    \"\"\"Sources should be relatively balanced in the taxonomy.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    source_counts = df['source'].value_counts()
    # No source should be less than 10% of total
    min_ratio = source_counts.min() / len(df)
    assert min_ratio > 0.1, f\"Smallest source has only {min_ratio:.1%} of records\"

def test_no_empty_clusters():
    \"\"\"No L1 category should have 0 records.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    l1_counts = df['unified_level_1'].value_counts()
    assert (l1_counts > 0).all()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Build unified 5-level product category taxonomy.
Strategy:
1. Load, normalize to title case, filter depth<=5, remove prefix paths
2. Map source L1 -> unified L1 (18 categories)
3. For L2-L5: use source hierarchy, format names, strip ancestor words
4. Post-process: merge small groups (<3 children) upward
5. Post-process: enforce max 20 children
6. Post-process: fix parent word violations
7. Generate outputs
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import defaultdict

import nltk
nltk.download('"'wordnet', quiet=True)
nltk.download('omw-1.4', quiet=True)
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer

lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

def sanitize(text):
    if pd.isna(text) or text is None:
        return None
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', '', str(text))
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def lem(word):
    return lemmatizer.lemmatize(word.lower())

def make_name(raw, ancestor_words=None):
    if pd.isna(raw) or raw is None:
        return None
    s = sanitize(raw)
    if not s:
        return None
    words = [lem(w).title() for w in s.split() if w.strip()]
    if ancestor_words:
        filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
        if filtered:
            words = filtered
    return ' | '.join(words[:5]) if words else None

def words_of(name):
    if pd.isna(name) or name is None:
        return set()
    return set(w.strip().lower() for w in str(name).split('|'))

def overlap_ratio(n1, n2):
    w1, w2 = words_of(n1), words_of(n2)
    if not w1 or not w2:
        return 0.0
    return len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))

# ============================================================
# Load
# ============================================================
def load_data():
    amz = pd.read_csv('/root/data/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amz['source'] = 'amazon'
    fb = pd.read_csv('/root/data/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    fb = fb[['category_path', 'source']]
    ggl = pd.read_csv('/root/data/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    ggl['source'] = 'google'
    
    combined = pd.concat([amz, fb, ggl], ignore_index=True)
    combined['category_path'] = combined['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    # Title-case normalize for cross-source matching
    def tc(p):
        return ' > '.join(part.strip().title() for part in str(p).split(' > '))
    combined['category_path'] = combined['category_path'].apply(tc)
    
    combined['parts'] = combined['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    combined['depth'] = combined['parts'].str.len()
    combined = combined[combined['depth'] <= 5].copy()
    
    for i in range(5):
        combined[f'src_l{i+1}'] = combined['parts'].apply(lambda x: x[i].strip() if i < len(x) else None)
    
    return combined

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    all_paths = set(df['category_path'].unique())
    to_remove = set()
    for path in all_paths:
        parts = path.split(' > ')
        for i in range(1, len(parts)):
            prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
            if prefix in all_paths:
                to_remove.add(prefix)
    return df[~df['category_path'].isin(to_remove)].copy()

# ============================================================
# L1 mapping
# ============================================================
_l1_groups = {
    'Pet | Supply': ['Pet Supplies', 'Animals & Pet Supplies'],
    'Vehicle | Part': ['Automotive', 'Auto Parts & Accessories', 'Vehicles & Parts'],
    'Baby | Product': ['Baby Products', 'Baby & Toddler'],
    'Apparel | Fashion': ['Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry', 'Clothing & Accessories',
                          'Apparel & Accessories', 'Jewelry & Watches',
                          'Luggage & Bags', 'Travel & Luggage'],
    'Electronic | Device': ['Electronics', 'Cell Phones & Accessories',
                            'Cameras & Optics', 'Video Games', 'Software'],
    'Food | Beverage': ['Grocery & Gourmet Food', 'Food & Beverages',
                        'Food, Beverages & Tobacco'],
    'Health | Wellness': ['Health & Household', 'Beauty & Personal Care',
                          'Health & Beauty', 'Mature'],
    'Home | Furnishing': ['Home & Kitchen', 'Home', 'Furniture', 'Appliances'],
    'Hardware | Improvement': ['Tools & Home Improvement', 'Home Improvement', 'Hardware'],
    'Garden | Patio': ['Patio, Lawn & Garden', 'Patio & Garden', 'Home & Garden'],
    'Sporting | Recreation': ['Sports & Outdoors', 'Sporting Goods'],
    'Toy | Game': ['Toys & Games'],
    'Art | Craft': ['Arts, Crafts & Sewing', 'Arts & Crafts', 'Arts & Entertainment',
                    'Antiques & Collectibles', 'Religious & Ceremonial'],
    'Book | Media': ['Books', 'Cds & Vinyl', 'Movies & Tv', 'Media'],
    'Office | Supply': ['Office Products', 'Office Supplies'],
    'Industrial | Commercial': ['Industrial & Scientific', 'Business & Industrial'],
    'Musical | Instrument': ['Musical Instruments'],
    'General | Merchandise': ['Other'],
}

L1_MAP = {}
for unified, sources in _l1_groups.items():
    for s in sources:
        L1_MAP[s.title()] = unified

# ============================================================
# Build taxonomy
# ============================================================
def build_taxonomy(df):
    df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    unmapped = df[df['unified_level_1'].isna()]['src_l1'].unique()
    if len(unmapped) > 0:
        print(f\"  Unmapped L1: {unmapped}\")
        for u in unmapped:
            L1_MAP[u] = 'General | Merchandise'
        df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    
    for lvl in range(2, 6):
        df[f'unified_level_{lvl}'] = None
    
    # Build L2-L5
    for ul1 in sorted(df['unified_level_1'].unique()):
        m1 = df['unified_level_1'] == ul1
        aw1 = words_of(ul1)
        
        l2_vals = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].dropna().unique()
        l2_counts = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].value_counts().to_dict()
        l2_map = _build_name_map(l2_vals, l2_counts, aw1)
        
        for raw2, name2 in l2_map.items():
            m2 = m1 & (df['src_l2'] == raw2)
            df.loc[m2, 'unified_level_2'] = name2
            aw2 = aw1 | words_of(name2)
            
            l3_vals = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].dropna().unique()
            l3_counts = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].value_counts().to_dict()
            l3_map = _build_name_map(l3_vals, l3_counts, aw2)
            
            for raw3, name3 in l3_map.items():
                m3 = m2 & (df['src_l3'] == raw3)
                df.loc[m3, 'unified_level_3'] = name3
                aw3 = aw2 | words_of(name3)
                
                l4_vals = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].dropna().unique()
                l4_counts = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].value_counts().to_dict()
                l4_map = _build_name_map(l4_vals, l4_counts, aw3)
                
                for raw4, name4 in l4_map.items():
                    m4 = m3 & (df['src_l4'] == raw4)
                    df.loc[m4, 'unified_level_4'] = name4
                    aw4 = aw3 | words_of(name4)
                    
                    l5_vals = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].dropna().unique()
                    l5_counts = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].value_counts().to_dict()
                    l5_map = _build_name_map(l5_vals, l5_counts, aw4)
                    
                    for raw5, name5 in l5_map.items():
                        m5 = m4 & (df['src_l5'] == raw5)
                        df.loc[m5, 'unified_level_5'] = name5
    
    return df

def _build_name_map(raw_vals, counts, ancestor_words):
    if len(raw_vals) == 0:
        return {}
    
    name_map = {}
    for raw in raw_vals:
        name = make_name(raw, ancestor_words)
        if name:
            name_map[raw] = name
        else:
            name_map[raw] = 'Misc'
    
    # Enforce max 20 unique names
    unique_names = list(set(name_map.values()))
    if len(unique_names) > 20:
        # Keep top 19 by total count
        name_counts = defaultdict(int)
        for raw, name in name_map.items():
            name_counts[name] += counts.get(raw, 1)
        sorted_names = sorted(name_counts.keys(), key=lambda n: name_counts[n], reverse=True)
        keep = set(sorted_names[:19])
        for raw in list(name_map.keys()):
            if name_map[raw] not in keep:
                name_map[raw] = 'Other | Misc'
    
    # Fix sibling overlap
    name_map = _fix_siblings(name_map)
    
    return name_map

def _fix_siblings(name_map):
    name_to_raws = defaultdict(list)
    for raw, name in name_map.items():
        name_to_raws[name].append(raw)
    
    unique_names = list(name_to_raws.keys())
    
    for i in range(len(unique_names)):
        for j in range(i + 1, len(unique_names)):
            n1, n2 = unique_names[i], unique_names[j]
            if overlap_ratio(n1, n2) >= 0.3:
                raws = name_to_raws[n2]
                if raws:
                    raw_words = sanitize(raws[0]).split() if sanitize(raws[0]) else []
                    lemmed = [lem(w).title() for w in raw_words]
                    w1 = words_of(n1)
                    w2 = words_of(n2)
                    for lw in lemmed:
                        if lw.lower() not in w1 and lw.lower() not in w2:
                            new_name = ' | '.join((n2 + ' | ' + lw).split(' | ')[:5])
                            for r in name_to_raws[n2]:
                                name_map[r] = new_name
                            name_to_raws[new_name] = name_to_raws.pop(n2)
                            unique_names[j] = new_name
                            break
    return name_map

# ============================================================
# Post-processing: merge small groups, fix parent words
# ============================================================

def merge_small_groups(df):
    \"\"\"
    For groups with < 3 children, merge children into a single 'Combined' category
    or remove the level (shift children up).
    Strategy: if a parent at level N has < 3 children at level N+1,
    set level N+1 to None for those rows (effectively merging them into the parent).
    \"\"\"
    for lvl in range(4, 1, -1):  # Start from deepest: L4->L5, L3->L4, L2->L3
        child_col = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}' if lvl < 5 else None
        current_col = f'unified_level_{lvl}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl)]
        
        if lvl == 5:
            continue
        
        # For each parent group, count children
        child_col = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}'
        
        # Get groups with children
        has_children = df[child_col].notna()
        if not has_children.any():
            continue
        
        group_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl + 1)]
        groups = df[has_children].groupby(group_cols)[child_col].nunique()
        
        # Find groups with < 3 children
        small_groups = groups[groups < 3]
        
        for parent_path, count in small_groups.items():
            if isinstance(parent_path, str):
                parent_path = (parent_path,)
            
            mask = pd.Series(True, index=df.index)
            for i, col in enumerate(group_cols):
                mask &= (df[col] == parent_path[i])
            
            # Clear child level and all deeper levels
            for deeper in range(lvl + 1, 6):
                df.loc[mask, f'unified_level_{deeper}'] = None
    
    return df

def fix_parent_words_post(df):
    \"\"\"
    Post-process: for each row, check if child words overlap with any ancestor.
    If so, try to rename the child by removing overlapping words.
    \"\"\"
    for lvl in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{lvl}'
        
        for idx, row in df.iterrows():
            if pd.isna(row[col]):
                continue
            
            child_words = words_of(row[col])
            all_ancestor_words = set()
            
            for plvl in range(1, lvl):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{plvl}'
                if pd.notna(row[pcol]):
                    all_ancestor_words |= words_of(row[pcol])
            
            overlap = child_words & all_ancestor_words
            if overlap:
                remaining = [w for w in row[col].split(' | ') if w.strip().lower() not in all_ancestor_words]
                if remaining:
                    new_name = ' | '.join(remaining[:5])
                    # Update all rows with the same value at this level under the same parent
                    parent_mask = pd.Series(True, index=df.index)
                    for plvl in range(1, lvl):
                        pcol = f'unified_level_{plvl}'
                        parent_mask &= (df[pcol] == row[pcol])
                    parent_mask &= (df[col] == row[col])
                    df.loc[parent_mask, col] = new_name
    
    return df

def handle_general_merchandise(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure General | Merchandise has at least L2.\"\"\"
    mask = (df['unified_level_1'] == 'General | Merchandise') & df['unified_level_2'].isna()
    df.loc[mask, 'unified_level_2'] = 'Uncategorized | Item'
    return df

# ============================================================
# Final clean
# ============================================================
def final_clean(df):
    for col in [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]:
        def clean(val):
            if pd.isna(val) or val is None:
                return None
            parts = str(val).split('|')
            cleaned = []
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                p = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', '', p)
                p = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', p).strip()
                if p:
                    cleaned.append(p)
            return ' | '.join(cleaned) if cleaned else None
        df[col] = df[col].apply(clean)
    return df

# ============================================================
# Main
# ============================================================
def main():
    print(\"Loading...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefixes...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows\")
    
    xsrc = df.groupby('category_path')['source'].nunique()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {(xsrc > 1).sum()}\")
    
    print(\"Building taxonomy...\")
    df = build_taxonomy(df)
    
    print(\"Handling edge cases...\")
    df = handle_general_merchandise(df)
    
    print(\"Merging small groups...\")
    df = merge_small_groups(df)
    
    print(\"Fixing parent word violations...\")
    df = fix_parent_words_post(df)
    
    print(\"Final cleanup...\")
    df = final_clean(df)
    
    # Output
    out_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[out_cols].copy()
    full_df['depth'] = full_df['depth'].astype(int)
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Full: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    hier_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hier = full_df[hier_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(hier_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    hier.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hier)} rows\")
    
    # Stats
    print(f\"\\nL1: {full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")
    
    # Pyramid check
    for lvl in range(1, 6):
        c = hier[f'unified_level_{lvl}'].notna().sum()
        print(f\"  Hier L{lvl}: {c}\")
    
    # Children violations
    for lvl in range(1, 5):
        pcols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl + 1)]
        ccol = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}'
        grp = hier.dropna(subset=[ccol]).groupby(pcols)[ccol].nunique()
        too_few = (grp < 3).sum()
        too_many = (grp > 20).sum()
        print(f\"  L{lvl}->L{lvl+1}: {len(grp)} groups, {too_few} <3, {too_many} >20\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

Now I see the issues clearly:

1. **Parent word exclusion**: The `make_name` function removes ancestor words, but it's only checking the *immediate* parent's words during construction. The issue is that when building L3, it checks L1+L2 ancestor words, but the L2 name itself might contain words from L1 that then appear in L3. The real problem is words like "accessory", "baby", "clothing" leaking through.

2. **Children count**: Massive number of singletons at L3-L5. Need to merge small groups.

3. **Pyramid**: One row with only L1 (General | Merchandise from "other").

4. **Representativeness**: Parent names not matching source paths well enough.

Let me rewrite the script with a post-processing approach that merges small groups and properly strips ancestor words:"}}

    'Baby | Product': ['Baby Products', 'Baby & Toddler'],
    'Apparel | Fashion': ['Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry', 'Clothing & Accessories',
                          'Apparel & Accessories', 'Jewelry & Watches',
                          'Luggage & Bags', 'Travel & Luggage'],
    'Electronic | Device': ['Electronics', 'Cell Phones & Accessories',
                            'Cameras & Optics', 'Video Games', 'Software'],
    'Food | Beverage': ['Grocery & Gourmet Food', 'Food & Beverages',
                        'Food, Beverages & Tobacco'],
    'Health | Wellness': ['Health & Household', 'Beauty & Personal Care',
                          'Health & Beauty', 'Mature'],
    'Home | Furnishing': ['Home & Kitchen', 'Home', 'Furniture', 'Appliances'],
    'Hardware | Improvement': ['Tools & Home Improvement', 'Home Improvement', 'Hardware'],
    'Garden | Patio': ['Patio, Lawn & Garden', 'Patio & Garden', 'Home & Garden'],
    'Sporting | Recreation': ['Sports & Outdoors', 'Sporting Goods'],
    'Toy | Game': ['Toys & Games'],
    'Art | Craft': ['Arts, Crafts & Sewing', 'Arts & Crafts', 'Arts & Entertainment',
                    'Antiques & Collectibles', 'Religious & Ceremonial'],
    'Book | Media': ['Books', 'Cds & Vinyl', 'Movies & Tv', 'Media'],
    'Office | Supply': ['Office Products', 'Office Supplies'],
    'Industrial | Commercial': ['Industrial & Scientific', 'Business & Industrial'],
    'Musical | Instrument': ['Musical Instruments'],
    'General | Merchandise': ['Other'],
}

L1_MAP = {}
for unified, sources in _l1_groups.items():
    for s in sources:
        L1_MAP[s.title()] = unified

# ============================================================
# Build taxonomy
# ============================================================
def build_taxonomy(df):
    df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    unmapped = df[df['unified_level_1'].isna()]['src_l1'].unique()
    if len(unmapped) > 0:
        print(f\"  Unmapped L1: {unmapped}\")
        for u in unmapped:
            L1_MAP[u] = 'General | Merchandise'
        df['unified_level_1'] = df['src_l1'].map(L1_MAP)
    
    for lvl in range(2, 6):
        df[f'unified_level_{lvl}'] = None
    
    # Build L2-L5
    for ul1 in sorted(df['unified_level_1'].unique()):
        m1 = df['unified_level_1'] == ul1
        aw1 = words_of(ul1)
        
        l2_vals = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].dropna().unique()
        l2_counts = df.loc[m1, 'src_l2'].value_counts().to_dict()
        l2_map = _build_name_map(l2_vals, l2_counts, aw1)
        
        for raw2, name2 in l2_map.items():
            m2 = m1 & (df['src_l2'] == raw2)
            df.loc[m2, 'unified_level_2'] = name2
            aw2 = aw1 | words_of(name2)
            
            l3_vals = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].dropna().unique()
            l3_counts = df.loc[m2, 'src_l3'].value_counts().to_dict()
            l3_map = _build_name_map(l3_vals, l3_counts, aw2)
            
            for raw3, name3 in l3_map.items():
                m3 = m2 & (df['src_l3'] == raw3)
                df.loc[m3, 'unified_level_3'] = name3
                aw3 = aw2 | words_of(name3)
                
                l4_vals = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].dropna().unique()
                l4_counts = df.loc[m3, 'src_l4'].value_counts().to_dict()
                l4_map = _build_name_map(l4_vals, l4_counts, aw3)
                
                for raw4, name4 in l4_map.items():
                    m4 = m3 & (df['src_l4'] == raw4)
                    df.loc[m4, 'unified_level_4'] = name4
                    aw4 = aw3 | words_of(name4)
                    
                    l5_vals = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].dropna().unique()
                    l5_counts = df.loc[m4, 'src_l5'].value_counts().to_dict()
                    l5_map = _build_name_map(l5_vals, l5_counts, aw4)
                    
                    for raw5, name5 in l5_map.items():
                        m5 = m4 & (df['src_l5'] == raw5)
                        df.loc[m5, 'unified_level_5'] = name5
    
    return df

def _build_name_map(raw_vals, counts, ancestor_words):
    if len(raw_vals) == 0:
        return {}
    
    name_map = {}
    for raw in raw_vals:
        name = make_name(raw, ancestor_words)
        if name:
            name_map[raw] = name
        else:
            name_map[raw] = 'Misc'
    
    # Enforce max 20 unique names
    unique_names = list(set(name_map.values()))
    if len(unique_names) > 20:
        # Keep top 19 by total count
        name_counts = defaultdict(int)
        for raw, name in name_map.items():
            name_counts[name] += counts.get(raw, 1)
        sorted_names = sorted(name_counts.keys(), key=lambda n: name_counts[n], reverse=True)
        keep = set(sorted_names[:19])
        for raw in list(name_map.keys()):
            if name_map[raw] not in keep:
                name_map[raw] = 'Other | Misc'
    
    # Fix sibling overlap
    name_map = _fix_siblings(name_map)
    
    return name_map

def _fix_siblings(name_map):
    name_to_raws = defaultdict(list)
    for raw, name in name_map.items():
        name_to_raws[name].append(raw)
    
    unique_names = list(name_to_raws.keys())
    
    for i in range(len(unique_names)):
        for j in range(i + 1, len(unique_names)):
            n1, n2 = unique_names[i], unique_names[j]
            if overlap_ratio(n1, n2) >= 0.3:
                raws = name_to_raws[n2]
                if raws:
                    raw_words = sanitize(raws[0]).split() if sanitize(raws[0]) else []
                    lemmed = [lem(w).title() for w in raw_words]
                    w1 = words_of(n1)
                    w2 = words_of(n2)
                    for lw in lemmed:
                        if lw.lower() not in w1 and lw.lower() not in w2:
                            new_name = ' | '.join((n2 + ' | ' + lw).split(' | ')[:5])
                            for r in name_to_raws[n2]:
                                name_map[r] = new_name
                            name_to_raws[new_name] = name_to_raws.pop(n2)
                            unique_names[j] = new_name
                            break
    return name_map

# ============================================================
# Post-processing: merge small groups, fix parent words
# ============================================================

def merge_small_groups(df):
    \"\"\"
    For groups with < 3 children, merge children into a single 'Combined' category
    or remove the level (shift children up).
    Strategy: if a parent at level N has < 3 children at level N+1,
    set level N+1 to None for those rows (effectively merging them into the parent).
    \"\"\"
    for lvl in range(4, 1, -1):  # Start from deepest: L4->L5, L3->L4, L2->L3
        child_col = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}' if lvl < 5 else None
        current_col = f'unified_level_{lvl}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl)]
        
        if lvl == 5:
            continue
        
        # For each parent group, count children
        child_col = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}'
        
        # Get groups with children
        has_children = df[child_col].notna()
        if not has_children.any():
            continue
        
        group_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl + 1)]
        groups = df[has_children].groupby(group_cols)[child_col].nunique()
        
        # Find groups with < 3 children
        small_groups = groups[groups < 3]
        
        for parent_path, count in small_groups.items():
            if isinstance(parent_path, str):
                parent_path = (parent_path,)
            
            mask = pd.Series(True, index=df.index)
            for i, col in enumerate(group_cols):
                mask &= (df[col] == parent_path[i])
            
            # Clear child level and all deeper levels
            for deeper in range(lvl + 1, 6):
                df.loc[mask, f'unified_level_{deeper}'] = None
    
    return df

def fix_parent_words_post(df):
    \"\"\"
    Post-process: for each row, check if child words overlap with any ancestor.
    If so, try to rename the child by removing overlapping words.
    \"\"\"
    for lvl in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{lvl}'
        
        for idx, row in df.iterrows():
            if pd.isna(row[col]):
                continue
            
            child_words = words_of(row[col])
            all_ancestor_words = set()
            
            for plvl in range(1, lvl):
                pcol = f'unified_level_{plvl}'
                if pd.notna(row[pcol]):
                    all_ancestor_words |= words_of(row[pcol])
            
            overlap = child_words & all_ancestor_words
            if overlap:
                remaining = [w for w in row[col].split(' | ') if w.strip().lower() not in all_ancestor_words]
                if remaining:
                    new_name = ' | '.join(remaining[:5])
                    # Update all rows with the same value at this level under the same parent
                    parent_mask = pd.Series(True, index=df.index)
                    for plvl in range(1, lvl):
                        pcol = f'unified_level_{plvl}'
                        parent_mask &= (df[pcol] == row[pcol])
                    parent_mask &= (df[col] == row[col])
                    df.loc[parent_mask, col] = new_name
    
    return df

def handle_general_merchandise(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure General | Merchandise has at least L2.\"\"\"
    mask = (df['unified_level_1'] == 'General | Merchandise') & df['unified_level_2'].isna()
    df.loc[mask, 'unified_level_2'] = 'Uncategorized | Item'
    return df

# ============================================================
# Final clean
# ============================================================
def final_clean(df):
    for col in [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]:
        def clean(val):
            if pd.isna(val) or val is None:
                return None
            parts = str(val).split('|')
            cleaned = []
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                p = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s-]', '', p)
                p = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', p).strip()
                if p:
                    cleaned.append(p)
            return ' | '.join(cleaned) if cleaned else None
        df[col] = df[col].apply(clean)
    return df

# ============================================================
# Main
# ============================================================
def main():
    print(\"Loading...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefixes...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  {len(df)} rows\")
    
    xsrc = df.groupby('category_path')['source'].nunique()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {(xsrc > 1).sum()}\")
    
    print(\"Building taxonomy...\")
    df = build_taxonomy(df)
    
    print(\"Handling edge cases...\")
    df = handle_general_merchandise(df)
    
    print(\"Merging small groups...\")
    df = merge_small_groups(df)
    
    print(\"Fixing parent word violations...\")
    df = fix_parent_words_post(df)
    
    print(\"Final cleanup...\")
    df = final_clean(df)
    
    # Output
    out_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth',
                'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3',
                'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    full_df = df[out_cols].copy()
    full_df['depth'] = full_df['depth'].astype(int)
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Full: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    
    hier_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hier = full_df[hier_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(hier_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    hier.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hier)} rows\")
    
    # Stats
    print(f\"\\nL1: {full_df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    for s in ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']:
        print(f\"  {s}: {(full_df['source'] == s).sum()}\")
    
    # Pyramid check
    for lvl in range(1, 6):
        c = hier[f'unified_level_{lvl}'].notna().sum()
        print(f\"  Hier L{lvl}: {c}\")
    
    # Children violations
    for lvl in range(1, 5):
        pcols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, lvl + 1)]
        ccol = f'unified_level_{lvl + 1}'
        grp = hier.dropna(subset=[ccol]).groupby(pcols)[ccol].nunique()
        too_few = (grp < 3).sum()
        too_many = (grp > 20).sum()
        print(f\"  L{lvl}->L{lvl+1}: {len(grp)} groups, {too_few} <3, {too_many} >20\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

        if len(non_null) > 0:

def test_depth_filtering():

def test_prefix_removal():
    """Test that prefix paths are removed when longer paths exist."""
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)

    # Get unique category paths
    paths = set(df['category_path'].unique())

    # Check for prefix violations
    prefix_violations = []
    for path in paths:
        parts = path.split(' > ')
        # Check all prefixes
        for i in range(1, len(parts)):
            prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
            if prefix in paths:
                prefix_violations.append((prefix, path))

    # Allow some minimal violations (edge cases), but should be < 5%
    violation_rate = len(prefix_violations) / len(paths) if len(paths) > 0 else 0
    assert violation_rate < 0.05, f"Too many prefix violations: {len(prefix_violations)} out of {len(paths)} paths. Examples: {prefix_violations[:5]}"

def test_parent_word_exclusion():
    """Test that child categories don't repeat parent words."""
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

    violations = []

    # Sample 20 parent-child pairs across different levels
    for level in range(1, 5):  # Check levels 1-4 as parents
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level + 1}'

        # Get pairs
        pairs_df = df[[parent_col, child_col]].dropna().drop_duplicates()

        for _, row in pairs_df.head(20).iterrows():
            parent = str(row[parent_col]).lower()
            child = str(row[child_col]).lower()

            # Extract words from parent
            parent_words = set(w.strip() for w in parent.replace('|', ' ').split())
            child_words = set(w.strip() for w in child.replace('|', ' ').split())

            # Check overlap
            overlap = parent_words & child_words
            if overlap:
                violations.append((parent, child, overlap))

    # Allow up to 10% violations (edge cases)
    violation_rate = len(violations) / 20 if len(violations) <= 20 else 1.0
    assert violation_rate < 0.10, f"Too many parent-word violations: {len(violations)} cases. Examples: {violations[:3]}"


def test_lemmatization_applied():
    """Test that common plural/variant words are lemmatized."""
    hierarchy_df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

    # Collect all category words
    all_words = []
    for level in range(1, 6):
        for category in hierarchy_df[f'unified_level_{level}'].dropna().unique():
            words = [w.strip() for w in str(category).replace('|', ' ').split()]
            all_words.extend(words)

    word_set = set(all_words)

    # Check for common plural pairs (should be lemmatized to singular)
    problematic_pairs = []
    plural_patterns = [
        ('sport', 'sports'),
        ('game', 'games'),
        ('book', 'books'),
        ('shoe', 'shoes'),
        ('toy', 'toys')
    ]

    for singular, plural in plural_patterns:
        if singular in word_set and plural in word_set:
            problematic_pairs.append((singular, plural))

    # Allow up to 1 pair (edge cases), but not systematic failures
    assert len(problematic_pairs) <= 1, f"Found non-lemmatized word pairs: {problematic_pairs}"

def test_path_representativeness():
    """Test that unified_level_1 category names cover >= 70% of their child paths."""
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)

    violations = []

    # Check each unified_level_1 category
    for level_1_cat in full_df['unified_level_1'].dropna().unique()[:15]:  # Sample 15 categories
        # Get all original paths under this unified_level_1
        level_1_data = full_df[full_df['unified_level_1'] == level_1_cat]
        original_paths = level_1_data['category_path'].tolist()

        # Extract words from unified_level_1 name
        level_1_words = set(w.strip() for w in str(level_1_cat).replace('|', ' ').split())

        # Check how many paths contain at least one unified_level_1 word
        matches = sum(
            any(word in str(path).lower() for word in level_1_words)
            for path in original_paths
        )

        coverage = matches / len(original_paths) if original_paths else 0

        if coverage < 0.70:
            violations.append((level_1_cat, coverage, len(original_paths)))

    # Should have very few violations
    assert len(violations) < 3, f"Found {len(violations)} unified_level_1 categories with <70% path coverage: {violations}"


def test_sibling_distinctiveness():
    """Test that sibling categories under same parent have distinct names."""
    hierarchy_df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

    violations = []

    # Check siblings at unified_level_2 (under same unified_level_1)
    for level_1_cat in hierarchy_df['unified_level_1'].unique()[:5]:  # Sample 5 parents
        siblings = hierarchy_df[hierarchy_df['unified_level_1'] == level_1_cat]['unified_level_2'].dropna().unique()

        if len(siblings) > 1:
            # Check pairwise distinctiveness
            for i, sib1 in enumerate(siblings):
                for sib2 in siblings[i + 1:]:
                    words1 = set(w.strip() for w in str(sib1).replace('|', ' ').split())
                    words2 = set(w.strip() for w in str(sib2).replace('|', ' ').split())

                    overlap = words1 & words2
                    overlap_ratio = len(overlap) / max(len(words1), len(words2)) if words1 and words2 else 0

                    if overlap_ratio > 0.30:  # More than 30% word overlap
                        violations.append((level_1_cat, sib1, sib2, overlap_ratio))

    assert len(violations) <= 3, f"Found {len(violations)} sibling pairs with >30% word overlap: {violations[:3]}"


def test_children_count_limit():
    """Test that no category has more than 60 children."""
    hierarchy_df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

    violations = []

    # Check each level (1-4) as parent, count children at next level
    for parent_level in range(1, 5):
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{parent_level}'
        child_col = f'unified_level_{parent_level + 1}'

        # Group by parent, count unique children
        for parent_cat in hierarchy_df[parent_col].dropna().unique():
            children = hierarchy_df[hierarchy_df[parent_col] == parent_cat][child_col].dropna().unique()
            child_count = len(children)

            if child_count > 60:
                violations.append((parent_col, parent_cat, child_count))

    assert len(violations) == 0, f"Found {len(violations)} categories with >60 children: {violations[:5]}"


def test_hierarchy_depth_consistency():
    """Test that larger clusters have deeper hierarchies."""
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)

    # Calculate average depth for each unified_level_1 category
    level_1_stats = []
    for level_1_cat in full_df['unified_level_1'].dropna().unique():
        level_1_data = full_df[full_df['unified_level_1'] == level_1_cat]
        record_count = len(level_1_data)
        avg_depth = level_1_data['depth'].mean()
        max_depth = level_1_data['depth'].max()

        level_1_stats.append({
            'category': level_1_cat,
            'count': record_count,
            'avg_depth': avg_depth,
            'max_depth': max_depth
        })

    # Sort by count
    stats_df = pd.DataFrame(level_1_stats).sort_values('count', ascending=False)

    if len(stats_df) >= 4:
        # Top 25% should have higher avg depth than bottom 25%
        top_25_avg = stats_df.head(len(stats_df) // 4)['avg_depth'].mean()
        bottom_25_avg = stats_df.tail(len(stats_df) // 4)['avg_depth'].mean()

        # This is a soft requirement - larger clusters tend to be deeper
        assert top_25_avg >= bottom_25_avg * 0.90, f"Large clusters should have similar or deeper hierarchies. Top 25%: {top_25_avg:.2f}, Bottom 25%: {bottom_25_avg:.2f}"

def test_hierarchy_coverage():
    """Test that hierarchy CSV is a complete deduplicated subset of full CSV."""
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    hierarchy_df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

    # Get level columns
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']

    # Deduplicate full_df on level columns
    full_unique = full_df[level_cols].dropna(subset=['unified_level_1']).drop_duplicates()

    # Check that hierarchy is a subset
    for _, row in hierarchy_df.iterrows():
        # Check if this combination exists in full_df
        match = full_unique
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                match = match[match[col] == row[col]]
            else:
                match = match[match[col].isna()]

        assert len(match) > 0, f"Hierarchy row not found in full CSV: {row.to_dict()}"


def test_source_balance():
    """Test that sources are relatively evenly distributed across the unified taxonomy."""
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)

    # Check distribution across level 1 categories
    level1_source_dist = {}
    for level1_cat in full_df['unified_level_1'].dropna().unique():
        cat_data = full_df[full_df['unified_level_1'] == level1_cat]
        source_counts = cat_data['source'].value_counts(normalize=True)
        level1_source_dist[level1_cat] = source_counts.to_dict()

    # Calculate average source representation across all level 1 categories
    all_sources = ['amazon', 'facebook', 'google']
    source_representations = {source: [] for source in all_sources}

    for cat, dist in level1_source_dist.items():
        for source in all_sources:
            source_representations[source].append(dist.get(source, 0.0))

    # Check that no source dominates or is severely underrepresented on average
    avg_representations = {source: sum(reps) / len(reps) if reps else 0
                          for source, reps in source_representations.items()}

    if len(avg_representations) >= 2:
        max_avg = max(avg_representations.values())
        min_avg = min(avg_representations.values())

        # Allow for reasonable variation but ensure no extreme imbalance
        # If one source has 3x the representation of another, that's problematic
        if min_avg > 0:
            ratio = max_avg / min_avg
            assert ratio < 3.0, (
                f"Source representation imbalance detected (ratio: {ratio:.2f}): "
                f"{avg_representations}. Consider sampling to balance sources."
            )
