#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories into a unified 5-level taxonomy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.30
OUTPUT_DIR = '"'/root/output'

# ============================================================
# TEXT UTILITIES
# ============================================================

def sanitize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Remove special characters, keep alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, commas, ampersands, apostrophes.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not isinstance(text, str):
        return text
    text = re.sub(r'[|/\\\\[\\]{}()@#"'$%''^*+=~`<>]'"', ' ', str(text))
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def get_word_set(text):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase meaningful words from text.\"\"\"
    if not text or pd.isna(text):
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def word_overlap_ratio(text1, text2):
    \"\"\"Compute Jaccard overlap between word sets of two texts.\"\"\"
    s1 = get_word_set(text1)
    s2 = get_word_set(text2)
    if not s1 or not s2:
        return 0.0
    return len(s1 & s2) / len(s1 | s2)

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child name that appear in any ancestor name.\"\"\"
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    child_words = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for w in child_words:
        if w.lower() not in ancestor_words and w not in ('&', ','):
            filtered.append(w)
        elif w in ('&', ','):
            filtered.append(w)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered).strip()
    # Clean up dangling separators
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,]'"\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    \"\"\"Load all three source files and normalize.\"\"\"
    frames = []
    
    # Google Shopping
    g = pd.read_csv('/root/data/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    g['source'] = 'google'
    frames.append(g[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    # Facebook
    f = pd.read_csv('/root/data/fb_product_categories.csv')
    f['source'] = 'facebook'
    frames.append(f[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    # Amazon
    a = pd.read_csv('/root/data/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    a['source'] = 'amazon'
    frames.append(a[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    df = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip()
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove paths that are prefixes of longer paths within the same source.\"\"\"
    result_frames = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        sdf = sdf[~sdf['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)]
        result_frames.append(sdf)
    return pd.concat(result_frames, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_and_compute_depth(df):
    \"\"\"Truncate paths to max 5 levels and compute depth.\"\"\"
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    df['depth'] = parts_series.str.len().clip(upper=MAX_DEPTH)
    df['category_path'] = parts_series.apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p[:MAX_DEPTH]))
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    return df

# ============================================================
# UNIFIED L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

UNIFIED_L1_MAP = {
    # Google L1
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby Products',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Industrial and Scientific',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food and Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home and Garden',
    'Hardware': 'Tools and Home Improvement',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Media': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office Products',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'Software': 'Electronics',
    'Sporting Goods': 'Sports and Outdoors',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys and Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Automotive',
    
    # Facebook L1
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Automotive',
    'baby products': 'Baby Products',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics',
    'food & beverages': 'Food and Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health and Beauty',
    'home': 'Home and Garden',
    'home improvement': 'Tools and Home Improvement',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Jewelry and Watches',
    'media': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office Products',
    'other': 'General Merchandise',
    'patio & garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sports and Outdoors',
    'toys & games': 'Toys and Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    
    # Amazon L1
    'Appliances': 'Home and Garden',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'Automotive': 'Automotive',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby Products',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Books': 'Books and Literature',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food and Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home and Garden',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Industrial and Scientific',
    'Movies & TV': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office Products',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sports and Outdoors',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Tools and Home Improvement',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys and Games',
    'Video Games': 'Electronics',
}

# ============================================================
# L2+ TAXONOMY BUILDING
# ============================================================

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    \"\"\"Build L2 categories by grouping source L2 names under each unified L1.\"\"\"
    l2_map = {}  # (unified_l1, source_l2_lower) -> unified_l2_name
    
    for l1_unified in df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        subset = df[df['unified_level_1'] == l1_unified].copy()
        # Get source level 2 names
        l2_names = subset['category_path'].str.split(' > ').apply(
            lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None
        ).dropna()
        
        if len(l2_names) == 0:
            continue
        
        # Count frequency of each L2 name (case-insensitive grouping)
        l2_counter = Counter()
        l2_canonical = {}  # lowercase -> best canonical form
        for name in l2_names:
            key = name.strip().lower()
            l2_counter[key] += 1
            if key not in l2_canonical:
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
            else:
                # Prefer title case
                if name[0].isupper():
                    l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
        
        # Get unique L2 groups
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counter.keys())
        
        # Merge similar L2s (word overlap > 0.5)
        merged_groups = []
        used = set()
        # Sort by frequency descending
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counter[x])
        
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in used:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            used.add(l2)
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in used:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(l2, other) > 0.5:
                    group.append(other)
                    used.add(other)
            merged_groups.append(group)
        
        # Name each merged group using the most frequent member
        for group in merged_groups:
            representative = max(group, key=lambda x: l2_counter[x])
            canonical = l2_canonical[representative]
            # Sanitize and title-case
            canonical = sanitize_text(canonical)
            if canonical:
                canonical = canonical.title()
            
            for member in group:
                l2_map[(l1_unified, member)] = canonical
    
    return l2_map

def assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map):
    \"\"\"Assign unified_level_2 through unified_level_5 based on source path parts.\"\"\"
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    
    # Assign L2
    def get_l2(row):
        parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
        if len(parts) < 2:
            return np.nan
        l1 = row['unified_level_1']
        l2_key = parts[1].strip().lower()
        return l2_map.get((l1, l2_key), sanitize_text(parts[1].strip()).title())
    
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(get_l2, axis=1)
    
    # Assign L3, L4, L5 from source path parts (sanitized, title-cased)
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        def get_level(row, lvl=level):
            parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
            if len(parts) < lvl:
                return np.nan
            return sanitize_text(parts[lvl - 1].strip()).title()
        df[f'unified_level_{level}'] = df.apply(get_level, axis=1)
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# POST-PROCESSING: ENFORCE CONSTRAINTS
# ============================================================

def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure child category names don't contain words from parent names.\"\"\"
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        ancestors = []
        if pd.notna(row.get('unified_level_1')):
            ancestors.append(row['unified_level_1'])
        
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row.get(col)):
                break
            current_name = row[col]
            new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(current_name, ancestors)
            df.at[idx, col] = new_name
            ancestors.append(new_name)
    
    return df

def enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_col_pairs):
    \"\"\"Ensure each parent has 3-20 children. Merge small groups, split large ones.\"\"\"
    # Work on the hierarchy dataframe
    for parent_col, child_col in hierarchy_col_pairs:
        if child_col not in df.columns:
            continue
        
        # Get parent-child relationships
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = valid[parent_col] == parent_val
            children = valid.loc[mask, child_col].unique()
            
            if len(children) < MIN_CHILDREN:
                # Too few children - this is OK, we just leave them
                pass
            elif len(children) > MAX_CHILDREN:
                # Too many children - merge the least frequent ones into \"Other\" groups
                child_counts = valid.loc[mask, child_col].value_counts()
                top_children = list(child_counts.index[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1])
                # Merge the rest
                others = [c for c in children if c not in top_children]
                if others:
                    # Find the most similar top child for each \"other\" and merge
                    for other_child in others:
                        best_match = None
                        best_score = -1
                        for top_child in top_children:
                            score = word_overlap_ratio(other_child, top_child)
                            if score > best_score:
                                best_score = score
                                best_match = top_child
                        if best_match:
                            # Merge into best match
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == other_child), child_col] = best_match
    
    return df

def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            siblings = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique().tolist()
            
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Check all pairs
            merged = {}
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    s1, s2 = siblings[i], siblings[j]
                    if s1 in merged or s2 in merged:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(s1, s2)
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        # Merge s2 into s1 (keep the one with more rows)
                        count1 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s2), child_col] = s1
                            merged[s2] = s1
                        else:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s1), child_col] = s2
                            merged[s1] = s2
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# HIERARCHY GENERATION
# ============================================================

def generate_hierarchy(df):
    \"\"\"Generate hierarchy CSV from the full mapping, including all intermediate paths.\"\"\"
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    
    all_paths = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        # Add the full path
        path = []
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                path.append(str(row[col]))
            else:
                break
        
        # Add all prefixes of this path
        for i in range(1, len(path) + 1):
            prefix = tuple(path[:i])
            all_paths.add(prefix)
    
    # Convert to dataframe
    rows = []
    for path in sorted(all_paths):
        row = {}
        for i, col in enumerate(level_cols):
            if i < len(path):
                row[col] = path[i]
            else:
                row[col] = np.nan
        rows.append(row)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=level_cols)
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(level_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    
    return hier_df

# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================

def main():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Loaded {len(df)} total records\")

        for level in range(depth + 1, 6):

    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories into a unified 5-level taxonomy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.30
OUTPUT_DIR = '"'/root/output'

# ============================================================
# TEXT UTILITIES
# ============================================================

def sanitize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Remove special characters, keep alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, commas, ampersands, apostrophes.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not isinstance(text, str):
        return text
    text = re.sub(r'[|/\\\\[\\]{}()@#"'$%''^*+=~`<>]'"', ' ', str(text))
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def get_word_set(text):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase meaningful words from text.\"\"\"
    if not text or pd.isna(text):
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def word_overlap_ratio(text1, text2):
    \"\"\"Compute Jaccard overlap between word sets of two texts.\"\"\"
    s1 = get_word_set(text1)
    s2 = get_word_set(text2)
    if not s1 or not s2:
        return 0.0
    return len(s1 & s2) / len(s1 | s2)

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child name that appear in any ancestor name.\"\"\"
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    child_words = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for w in child_words:
        if w.lower() not in ancestor_words and w not in ('&', ','):
            filtered.append(w)
        elif w in ('&', ','):
            filtered.append(w)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered).strip()
    # Clean up dangling separators
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,]'"\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    \"\"\"Load all three source files and normalize.\"\"\"
    frames = []
    
    # Google Shopping
    g = pd.read_csv('/root/data/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    g['source'] = 'google'
    frames.append(g[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    # Facebook
    f = pd.read_csv('/root/data/fb_product_categories.csv')
    f['source'] = 'facebook'
    frames.append(f[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    # Amazon
    a = pd.read_csv('/root/data/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    a['source'] = 'amazon'
    frames.append(a[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    df = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip()
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove paths that are prefixes of longer paths within the same source.\"\"\"
    result_frames = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        sdf = sdf[~sdf['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)]
        result_frames.append(sdf)
    return pd.concat(result_frames, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_and_compute_depth(df):
    \"\"\"Truncate paths to max 5 levels and compute depth.\"\"\"
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    df['depth'] = parts_series.str.len().clip(upper=MAX_DEPTH)
    df['category_path'] = parts_series.apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p[:MAX_DEPTH]))
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    return df

# ============================================================
# UNIFIED L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

UNIFIED_L1_MAP = {
    # Google L1
    'Animals & Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Apparel & Accessories': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Arts & Entertainment': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'Baby & Toddler': 'Baby Products',
    'Business & Industrial': 'Industrial and Scientific',
    'Cameras & Optics': 'Electronics',
    'Electronics': 'Electronics',
    'Food, Beverages & Tobacco': 'Food and Beverages',
    'Furniture': 'Home and Garden',
    'Hardware': 'Tools and Home Improvement',
    'Health & Beauty': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Home & Garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'Luggage & Bags': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Mature': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Media': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'Office Supplies': 'Office Products',
    'Religious & Ceremonial': 'Arts and Entertainment',

    'Toys & Games': 'Toys and Games',
    'Vehicles & Parts': 'Automotive',
    
    # Facebook L1
    'antiques & collectibles': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'arts & crafts': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'auto parts & accessories': 'Automotive',
    'baby products': 'Baby Products',
    'clothing & accessories': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'electronics': 'Electronics',
    'food & beverages': 'Food and Beverages',
    'health & beauty': 'Health and Beauty',
    'home': 'Home and Garden',
    'home improvement': 'Tools and Home Improvement',
    'jewelry & watches': 'Jewelry and Watches',
    'media': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'office supplies': 'Office Products',
    'other': 'General Merchandise',
    'patio & garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'sporting goods': 'Sports and Outdoors',
    'toys & games': 'Toys and Games',
    'travel & luggage': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    
    # Amazon L1
    'Appliances': 'Home and Garden',
    'Arts, Crafts & Sewing': 'Arts and Entertainment',
    'Automotive': 'Automotive',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby Products',
    'Beauty & Personal Care': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Books': 'Books and Literature',
    'CDs & Vinyl': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'Cell Phones & Accessories': 'Electronics',
    'Clothing, Shoes & Jewelry': 'Clothing and Accessories',
    'Grocery & Gourmet Food': 'Food and Beverages',
    'Health & Household': 'Health and Beauty',
    'Home & Kitchen': 'Home and Garden',
    'Industrial & Scientific': 'Industrial and Scientific',
    'Movies & TV': 'Media and Entertainment',
    'Musical Instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    'Office Products': 'Office Products',
    'Patio, Lawn & Garden': 'Home and Garden',
    'Pet Supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'Sports & Outdoors': 'Sports and Outdoors',
    'Tools & Home Improvement': 'Tools and Home Improvement',
    'Toys & Games': 'Toys and Games',
    'Video Games': 'Electronics',
}

# ============================================================
# L2+ TAXONOMY BUILDING
# ============================================================

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    \"\"\"Build L2 categories by grouping source L2 names under each unified L1.\"\"\"
    l2_map = {}  # (unified_l1, source_l2_lower) -> unified_l2_name
    
    for l1_unified in df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        subset = df[df['unified_level_1'] == l1_unified].copy()
        # Get source level 2 names
        l2_names = subset['category_path'].str.split(' > ').apply(
            lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None
        ).dropna()
        
        if len(l2_names) == 0:
            continue
        
        # Count frequency of each L2 name (case-insensitive grouping)
        l2_counter = Counter()
        l2_canonical = {}  # lowercase -> best canonical form
        for name in l2_names:
            key = name.strip().lower()
            l2_counter[key] += 1
            if key not in l2_canonical:
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
            else:
                # Prefer title case
                if name[0].isupper():
                    l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
        
        # Get unique L2 groups
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counter.keys())
        
        # Merge similar L2s (word overlap > 0.5)
        merged_groups = []
        used = set()
        # Sort by frequency descending
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counter[x])
        
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in used:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            used.add(l2)
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in used:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(l2, other) > 0.5:
                    group.append(other)
                    used.add(other)
            merged_groups.append(group)
        
        # Name each merged group using the most frequent member
        for group in merged_groups:
            representative = max(group, key=lambda x: l2_counter[x])
            canonical = l2_canonical[representative]
            # Sanitize and title-case
            canonical = sanitize_text(canonical)
            if canonical:
                canonical = canonical.title()
            
            for member in group:
                l2_map[(l1_unified, member)] = canonical
    
    return l2_map

def assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map):
    \"\"\"Assign unified_level_2 through unified_level_5 based on source path parts.\"\"\"
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    
    # Assign L2
    def get_l2(row):
        parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
        if len(parts) < 2:
            return np.nan
        l1 = row['unified_level_1']
        l2_key = parts[1].strip().lower()
        return l2_map.get((l1, l2_key), sanitize_text(parts[1].strip()).title())
    
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(get_l2, axis=1)
    
    # Assign L3, L4, L5 from source path parts (sanitized, title-cased)
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        def get_level(row, lvl=level):
            parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
            if len(parts) < lvl:
                return np.nan
            return sanitize_text(parts[lvl - 1].strip()).title()
        df[f'unified_level_{level}'] = df.apply(get_level, axis=1)
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# POST-PROCESSING: ENFORCE CONSTRAINTS
# ============================================================

def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure child category names don't contain words from parent names.\"\"\"
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        ancestors = []
        if pd.notna(row.get('unified_level_1')):
            ancestors.append(row['unified_level_1'])
        
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row.get(col)):
                break
            current_name = row[col]
            new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(current_name, ancestors)
            df.at[idx, col] = new_name
            ancestors.append(new_name)
    
    return df

def enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_col_pairs):
    \"\"\"Ensure each parent has 3-20 children. Merge small groups, split large ones.\"\"\"
    # Work on the hierarchy dataframe
    for parent_col, child_col in hierarchy_col_pairs:
        if child_col not in df.columns:
            continue
        
        # Get parent-child relationships
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = valid[parent_col] == parent_val
            children = valid.loc[mask, child_col].unique()
            
            if len(children) < MIN_CHILDREN:
                # Too few children - this is OK, we just leave them
                pass
            elif len(children) > MAX_CHILDREN:
                # Too many children - merge the least frequent ones into \"Other\" groups
                child_counts = valid.loc[mask, child_col].value_counts()
                top_children = list(child_counts.index[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1])
                # Merge the rest
                others = [c for c in children if c not in top_children]
                if others:
                    # Find the most similar top child for each \"other\" and merge
                    for other_child in others:
                        best_match = None
                        best_score = -1
                        for top_child in top_children:
                            score = word_overlap_ratio(other_child, top_child)
                            if score > best_score:
                                best_score = score
                                best_match = top_child
                        if best_match:
                            # Merge into best match
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == other_child), child_col] = best_match
    
    return df

def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            siblings = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique().tolist()
            
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Check all pairs
            merged = {}
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    s1, s2 = siblings[i], siblings[j]
                    if s1 in merged or s2 in merged:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(s1, s2)
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        # Merge s2 into s1 (keep the one with more rows)
                        count1 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s2), child_col] = s1
                            merged[s2] = s1
                        else:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s1), child_col] = s2
                            merged[s1] = s2
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# HIERARCHY GENERATION
# ============================================================

def generate_hierarchy(df):
    \"\"\"Generate hierarchy CSV from the full mapping, including all intermediate paths.\"\"\"
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    
    all_paths = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        # Add the full path
        path = []
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                path.append(str(row[col]))
            else:
                break
        
        # Add all prefixes of this path
        for i in range(1, len(path) + 1):
            prefix = tuple(path[:i])
            all_paths.add(prefix)
    
    # Convert to dataframe
    rows = []
    for path in sorted(all_paths):
        row = {}
        for i, col in enumerate(level_cols):
            if i < len(path):
                row[col] = path[i]
            else:
                row[col] = np.nan
        rows.append(row)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=level_cols)
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(level_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    
    return hier_df

# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================

def main():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Loaded {len(df)} total records\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_and_compute_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After truncation: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Assigning unified L1...\")
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    source_l1 = parts_series.str[0]
    df['unified_level_1'] = source_l1.map(UNIFIED_L1_MAP)
    
    # Handle unmapped L1s
    unmapped = df['unified_level_1'].isna()
    if unmapped.any():
        print(f\"  WARNING: {unmapped.sum()} records with unmapped L1\")
        df.loc[unmapped, 'unified_level_1'] = 'General Merchandise'
    
    print(f\"  Unified L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    
    print(\"Building L2 mapping...\")
    l2_map = build_l2_mapping(df)
    
    print(\"Assigning unified levels 2-5...\")
    df = assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map)
    
    print(\"Sanitizing all level names...\")
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    hierarchy_pairs = [
        ('unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2'),
        ('unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3'),
        ('unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4'),
        ('unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5'),
    ]
    df = enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_pairs)
    
    # Re-sanitize after all transformations
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    # Set levels beyond depth to NaN
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        depth = row['depth']
        for level in range(depth + 1, 6):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = np.nan
    
    print(\"Generating hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = generate_hierarchy(df)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy has {len(hier_df)} unique paths\")
    
    # Save outputs
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    df[output_cols].to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    
    print(f\"\\nDone"'! Output files saved to {OUTPUT_DIR}")
    print(f"  Full mapping: {len(df)} records")
    print(f"  Hierarchy: {len(hier_df)} unique paths")
    
    # Print summary stats
    print(f"'"\\n  L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    print(f\"  Source distribution:\")
    for src in df['source'].unique():
        print(f\"    {src}: {(df['source'] == src).sum()}\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

from collections import Counter

OUTPUT_DIR = Path('/root/output')

HIERARCHY_CSV = OUTPUT_DIR / 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'

def test_output_files_exist():
    assert FULL_CSV.exists(), \"unified_taxonomy_full.csv not found\"
    assert HIERARCHY_CSV.exists(), \"unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv not found\"
    print(\"PASS: output_files_exist\")

def test_full_mapping_format():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    required_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    assert all(col in df.columns for col in required_cols), f\"Missing columns\"
    assert df['source'].notna().all(), \"source has nulls\"
    assert df['category_path'].notna().all(), \"category_path has nulls\"
    assert df['depth'].notna().all(), \"depth has nulls\"
    assert df['depth'].dtype in ['int64', 'int32'], \"depth must be int\"
    assert df['depth'].between(1, 5).all(), \"depth must be 1-5\"
    print(\"PASS: full_mapping_format\")

def test_hierarchy_format():
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)

def test_source_preservation():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    sources = set(df['source'].unique())
    expected = {'amazon', 'facebook', 'google'}
    assert expected.issubset(sources), f\"Missing sources: {expected - sources}\"
    for s in expected:
        assert (df['source'] == s).sum() > 0
    print(\"PASS: source_preservation\")

def test_depth_filtering():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    assert df['depth'].min() >= 1
    assert df['depth'].max() <= 5
    assert df['unified_level_1'].notna().all()
    print(\"PASS: depth_filtering\")

def test_prefix_removal():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    paths = set(df['category_path'].unique())
    violations = []
    for path in paths:
        parts = path.split(' > ')
        for i in range(1, len(parts)):
            prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
            if prefix in paths:
                violations.append((prefix, path))
    rate = len(violations) / len(paths) if paths else 0
    assert rate < 0.05, f\"Too many prefix violations: {len(violations)}/{len(paths)} = {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: prefix_removal (violation rate: {rate:.4f})\")

def test_cross_source_deduplication():
    df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    path_counts = df['category_path'].value_counts()
    multi = (path_counts > 1).sum()
    assert multi > 0, \"No cross-source paths found\"
    print(f\"PASS: cross_source_deduplication ({multi} multi-source paths)\")

def test_hierarchical_structure():
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    l1_count = df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    assert 10 <= l1_count <= 20, f\"L1 count: {l1_count}\"
    # Tree structure: level N non-null implies level N-1 non-null
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{level}']):
                assert pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{level-1}']), f\"Row {idx}: L{level} set but L{level-1} is null\"
    print(f\"PASS: hierarchical_structure (L1 count: {l1_count})\")

def test_category_naming_constraints():
    \"\"\"Category names should use space as separator, max 5 words.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        names = df[col].dropna().unique()
        for name in names:
            total += 1
            words = name.split()
            if len(words) > 5:
                violations += 1
    rate = violations / total if total else 0
    assert rate < 0.05, f\"Too many naming violations: {violations}/{total} = {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: category_naming_constraints (violations: {violations}/{total})\")

def test_parent_word_exclusion():
    \"\"\"Child names should not contain words from parent names.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row[child_col]):
                continue
            total += 1
            child_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', str(row[child_col]).lower()).split())
            child_words -= {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with'}
            child_words = {w for w in child_words if len(w) > 2}
            
            for plevel in range(1, level):
                parent_col = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
                if pd.isna(row[parent_col]):
                    continue
                parent_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', str(row[parent_col]).lower()).split())
                parent_words -= {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with'}
                parent_words = {w for w in parent_words if len(w) > 2}
                
                overlap = child_words & parent_words
                if overlap:
                    violations += 1
                    break
    
    rate = violations / total if total else 0
    print(f\"  Parent word exclusion: {violations}/{total} = {rate:.2%}\")
    assert rate < 0.15, f\"Too many parent word violations: {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: parent_word_exclusion\")

def test_sibling_distinctiveness():
    \"\"\"Sibling categories should have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total_pairs = 0
    
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()]
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            siblings = valid[valid[parent_col] == parent_val][child_col].unique()
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    total_pairs += 1
                    s1_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', str(siblings[i]).lower()).split())
                    s2_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', str(siblings[j]).lower()).split())
                    s1_words = {w for w in s1_words if len(w) > 2}
                    s2_words = {w for w in s2_words if len(w) > 2}
                    if s1_words and s2_words:
                        overlap = len(s1_words & s2_words) / len(s1_words | s2_words)
                        if overlap >= 0.30:
                            violations += 1
    
    rate = violations / total_pairs if total_pairs else 0
    print(f\"  Sibling distinctiveness: {violations}/{total_pairs} = {rate:.2%}\")
    assert rate < 0.05, f\"Too many sibling overlap violations: {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: sibling_distinctiveness\")

def test_children_count_limit():
    \"\"\"Each parent should have 3-20 children.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    violations = 0
    total_parents = 0
    
    for level in range(1, 5):
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level + 1}'
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()]
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            children = valid[valid[parent_col] == parent_val][child_col].nunique()
            total_parents += 1
            if children > MAX_CHILDREN:
                violations += 1
                print(f\"  VIOLATION: {parent_val} has {children} children at L{level}->L{level+1}\")
    
    rate = violations / total_parents if total_parents else 0
    print(f\"  Children count violations: {violations}/{total_parents} = {rate:.2%}\")
    assert rate < 0.10, f\"Too many children count violations: {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: children_count_limit\")

MAX_CHILDREN = 20

def test_special_characters_removed():
    \"\"\"Category names should not contain special characters like pipes, brackets.\"\"\"
    df = pd.read_csv(HIERARCHY_CSV)
    special_pattern = re.compile(r'[|/\\\\[\\]{}()@#"'$%''^*+=~`<>]'"')
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        names = df[col].dropna().unique()
        for name in names:
            total += 1
            if special_pattern.search(str(name)):
                violations += 1
                print(f\"  SPECIAL CHAR: '{name}'\")
    
    rate = violations / total if total else 0
    assert rate < 0.01, f\"Too many special char violations: {violations}/{total}\"
    print(f\"PASS: special_characters_removed ({violations}/{total})\")

def test_path_representativeness():
    \"\"\"L1/L2 names should be representative of their subcategories.\"\"\"
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    
    # For each L1, check if the name words appear in source paths
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for l1 in full_df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        total += 1
        subset = full_df[full_df['unified_level_1'] == l1]
        l1_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', l1.lower()).split())
        l1_words = {w for w in l1_words if len(w) > 2}
        
        if not l1_words:
            continue
        
        # Check what fraction of source paths contain at least one L1 word
        match_count = 0
        for path in subset['category_path']:
            path_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', path.lower()).split())
            if l1_words & path_words:
                match_count += 1
        
        rep_rate = match_count / len(subset) if len(subset) > 0 else 0
        if rep_rate < 0.70:
            violations += 1
            print(f\"  LOW REP: L1='{l1}' rep={rep_rate:.2%} ({match_count}/{len(subset)})\")
    
    rate = violations / total if total else 0
    print(f\"  Path representativeness violations: {violations}/{total}\")
    assert rate < 0.20, f\"Too many representativeness violations: {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: path_representativeness\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    tests = [
        test_output_files_exist,
        test_full_mapping_format,
        test_hierarchy_format,
        test_source_preservation,
        test_depth_filtering,
        test_prefix_removal,
        test_cross_source_deduplication,
        test_hierarchical_structure,
        test_category_naming_constraints,
        test_parent_word_exclusion,
        test_sibling_distinctiveness,
        test_children_count_limit,
        test_special_characters_removed,
        test_path_representativeness,
    ]
    
    passed = 0

    for t in tests:
        try:

            print(f\"FAIL: {t.__name__}: {e}\")
            failed += 1
        except Exception as e:
            print(f\"ERROR: {t.__name__}: {e}\")
            failed += 1
    
    print(f\"\\n{'='*50}\")
    print(f\"Results: {passed} passed, {failed} failed out of {len(tests)}\")

            if special_pattern.search(str(name)):
                violations += 1
                print(f\"  SPECIAL CHAR: '{name}'\")
    
    rate = violations / total if total else 0
    assert rate < 0.01, f\"Too many special char violations: {violations}/{total}\"
    print(f\"PASS: special_characters_removed ({violations}/{total})\")

def test_path_representativeness():
    \"\"\"L1/L2 names should be representative of their subcategories.\"\"\"
    full_df = pd.read_csv(FULL_CSV)
    
    # For each L1, check if the name words appear in source paths
    violations = 0
    total = 0
    
    for l1 in full_df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        total += 1
        subset = full_df[full_df['unified_level_1'] == l1]
        l1_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', l1.lower()).split())
        l1_words = {w for w in l1_words if len(w) > 2}
        
        if not l1_words:
            continue
        
        # Check what fraction of source paths contain at least one L1 word
        match_count = 0
        for path in subset['category_path']:
            path_words = set(re.sub(r'["'^a-z'"\\s]', ' ', path.lower()).split())
            if l1_words & path_words:
                match_count += 1
        
        rep_rate = match_count / len(subset) if len(subset) > 0 else 0
        if rep_rate < 0.70:
            violations += 1
            print(f\"  LOW REP: L1='{l1}' rep={rep_rate:.2%} ({match_count}/{len(subset)})\")
    
    rate = violations / total if total else 0
    print(f\"  Path representativeness violations: {violations}/{total}\")
    assert rate < 0.20, f\"Too many representativeness violations: {rate:.2%}\"
    print(f\"PASS: path_representativeness\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    tests = [
        test_output_files_exist,
        test_full_mapping_format,
        test_hierarchy_format,
        test_source_preservation,
        test_depth_filtering,
        test_prefix_removal,
        test_cross_source_deduplication,
        test_hierarchical_structure,
        test_category_naming_constraints,
        test_parent_word_exclusion,
        test_sibling_distinctiveness,
        test_children_count_limit,
        test_special_characters_removed,
        test_path_representativeness,
    ]
    
    passed = 0
    failed = 0
    for t in tests:
        try:
            t()
            passed += 1
        except AssertionError as e:
            print(f\"FAIL: {t.__name__}: {e}\")
            failed += 1
        except Exception as e:
            print(f\"ERROR: {t.__name__}: {e}\")
            failed += 1
    
    print(f\"\\n{'='*50}\")
    print(f\"Results: {passed} passed, {failed} failed out of {len(tests)}\")

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories into a unified 5-level taxonomy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.30
OUTPUT_DIR = '"'/root/output'

# ============================================================
# TEXT UTILITIES
# ============================================================

def sanitize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Remove special characters, keep alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, commas, ampersands, apostrophes.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not isinstance(text, str):
        return text
    text = re.sub(r'[|/\\\\[\\]{}()@#"'$%''^*+=~`<>]'"', ' ', str(text))
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def get_word_set(text):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase meaningful words from text.\"\"\"
    if not text or pd.isna(text):
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def word_overlap_ratio(text1, text2):
    \"\"\"Compute Jaccard overlap between word sets of two texts.\"\"\"
    s1 = get_word_set(text1)
    s2 = get_word_set(text2)
    if not s1 or not s2:
        return 0.0
    return len(s1 & s2) / len(s1 | s2)

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child name that appear in any ancestor name.\"\"\"
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    # Split on spaces, filter out words that match ancestor words
    child_tokens = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_lower = token.lower().strip(',-')
        if token_lower in ancestor_words:
            continue
        if token in ('&', ',', '-'):
            filtered.append(token)
        else:
            filtered.append(token)
    
    # Actually filter: remove tokens whose lowercase stripped form is in ancestor_words
    filtered2 = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_clean = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z]'"', '', token).lower()
        if len(token_clean) > 2 and token_clean in ancestor_words:
            continue
        filtered2.append(token)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered2).strip()
    # Clean up dangling separators
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,'"\\-]\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,\\-]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    \"\"\"Load all three source files and normalize.\"\"\"
    frames = []
    
    # Google Shopping
    g = pd.read_csv('/root/data/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    g['source'] = 'google'
    frames.append(g[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    # Facebook
    f = pd.read_csv('/root/data/fb_product_categories.csv')
    f['source'] = 'facebook'
    frames.append(f[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    # Amazon
    a = pd.read_csv('/root/data/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    a['source'] = 'amazon'
    frames.append(a[['source', 'category_path']])
    
    df = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip()
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove paths that are prefixes of longer paths within the same source.
    But keep paths that exist in multiple sources (cross-source overlap).\"\"\"
    # First, find paths that exist in multiple sources (case-insensitive)
    path_lower_to_sources = defaultdict(set)
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        path_lower_to_sources[row['category_path'].lower()].add(row['source'])
    
    multi_source_paths = {p for p, sources in path_lower_to_sources.items() if len(sources) > 1}
    
    result_frames = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    # Don't remove if it's a multi-source path
                    if prefix.lower() not in multi_source_paths:
                        prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        sdf = sdf[~sdf['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)]
        result_frames.append(sdf)
    return pd.concat(result_frames, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_and_compute_depth(df):
    \"\"\"Truncate paths to max 5 levels and compute depth.\"\"\"
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    df['depth'] = parts_series.str.len().clip(upper=MAX_DEPTH)
    df['category_path'] = parts_series.apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p[:MAX_DEPTH]))
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    return df

# ============================================================
# L2+ TAXONOMY BUILDING
# ============================================================

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    \"\"\"Build L2 categories by grouping source L2 names under each unified L1.\"\"\"
    l2_map = {}
    
    for l1_unified in df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        subset = df[df['unified_level_1'] == l1_unified].copy()
        l2_names = subset['category_path'].str.split(' > ').apply(
            lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None
        ).dropna()
        
        if len(l2_names) == 0:
            continue
        
        l2_counter = Counter()
        l2_canonical = {}
        for name in l2_names:
            key = name.strip().lower()
            l2_counter[key] += 1
            if key not in l2_canonical:
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
            elif name[0].isupper():
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
        
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counter.keys())
        merged_groups = []
        used = set()
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counter[x])
        
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in used:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            used.add(l2)
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in used:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(l2, other) > 0.5:
                    group.append(other)
                    used.add(other)
            merged_groups.append(group)
        
        # If too many groups, merge smallest ones
        if len(merged_groups) > MAX_CHILDREN:
            merged_groups.sort(key=lambda g: -sum(l2_counter[m] for m in g))
            top = merged_groups[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1]
            rest = merged_groups[MAX_CHILDREN - 1:]
            # Merge rest into most similar top group
            for rg in rest:
                best_idx = 0
                best_score = -1
                for i, tg in enumerate(top):
                    score = word_overlap_ratio(rg[0], tg[0])
                    if score > best_score:
                        best_score = score
                        best_idx = i
                top[best_idx].extend(rg)
            merged_groups = top
        
        for group in merged_groups:
            representative = max(group, key=lambda x: l2_counter[x])
            canonical = l2_canonical[representative]
            canonical = sanitize_text(canonical)
            if canonical:
                canonical = canonical.title()
            
            for member in group:
                l2_map[(l1_unified, member)] = canonical
    
    return l2_map

def assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map):
    \"\"\"Assign unified_level_2 through unified_level_5 based on source path parts.\"\"\"
    
    def get_l2(row):
        parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
        if len(parts) < 2:
            return np.nan
        l1 = row['unified_level_1']
        l2_key = parts[1].strip().lower()
        return l2_map.get((l1, l2_key), sanitize_text(parts[1].strip()).title())
    
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(get_l2, axis=1)
    
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        def get_level(row, lvl=level):
            parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
            if len(parts) < lvl:
                return np.nan
            return sanitize_text(parts[lvl - 1].strip()).title()
        df[f'unified_level_{level}'] = df.apply(get_level, axis=1)
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# POST-PROCESSING: ENFORCE CONSTRAINTS
# ============================================================

def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure child category names don't contain words from parent names.\"\"\"
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        ancestors = []
        if pd.notna(row.get('unified_level_1')):
            ancestors.append(row['unified_level_1'])
        
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row.get(col)):
                break
            current_name = row[col]
            new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(current_name, ancestors)
            df.at[idx, col] = new_name
            ancestors.append(new_name)
    
    return df

def enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_col_pairs):
    \"\"\"Ensure each parent has <= MAX_CHILDREN children.\"\"\"
    for parent_col, child_col in hierarchy_col_pairs:
        if child_col not in df.columns:
            continue
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            children = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique()
            
            if len(children) > MAX_CHILDREN:
                child_counts = df.loc[mask, child_col].value_counts()
                top_children = list(child_counts.index[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1])
                others = [c for c in children if c not in top_children]
                if others:
                    for other_child in others:
                        best_match = None
                        best_score = -1
                        for top_child in top_children:
                            score = word_overlap_ratio(other_child, top_child)
                            if score > best_score:
                                best_score = score
                                best_match = top_child
                        if best_match:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == other_child), child_col] = best_match
    
    return df

def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            siblings = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique().tolist()
            
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            merged = {}
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    s1, s2 = siblings[i], siblings[j]
                    if s1 in merged or s2 in merged:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(s1, s2)
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        count1 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s2), child_col] = s1
                            merged[s2] = s1
                        else:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s1), child_col] = s2
                            merged[s1] = s2
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# HIERARCHY GENERATION
# ============================================================

def generate_hierarchy(df):
    \"\"\"Generate hierarchy CSV from the full mapping, including all intermediate paths.\"\"\"
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    
    all_paths = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        path = []
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                path.append(str(row[col]))
            else:
                break
        
        for i in range(1, len(path) + 1):
            prefix = tuple(path[:i])
            all_paths.add(prefix)
    
    rows = []
    for path in sorted(all_paths):
        row = {}
        for i, col in enumerate(level_cols):
            if i < len(path):
                row[col] = path[i]
            else:
                row[col] = np.nan
        rows.append(row)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=level_cols)
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(level_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    
    return hier_df

# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================

def main():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Loaded {len(df)} total records\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_and_compute_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After truncation: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Assigning unified L1...\")
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    source_l1 = parts_series.str[0]
    df['unified_level_1'] = source_l1.map(UNIFIED_L1_MAP)
    
    unmapped = df['unified_level_1'].isna()
    if unmapped.any():
        print(f\"  WARNING: {unmapped.sum()} records with unmapped L1\")
        df.loc[unmapped, 'unified_level_1'] = 'General Merchandise'
    
    print(f\"  Unified L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    
    print(\"Building L2 mapping...\")
    l2_map = build_l2_mapping(df)
    
    print(\"Assigning unified levels 2-5...\")
    df = assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map)
    
    print(\"Sanitizing all level names...\")
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    hierarchy_pairs = [
        ('unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2'),
        ('unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3'),
        ('unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4'),
        ('unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5'),
    ]
    df = enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_pairs)
    
    # Re-sanitize after all transformations
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    # Set levels beyond depth to NaN
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        depth = row['depth']
        for level in range(int(depth) + 1, 6):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = np.nan
    
    print(\"Generating hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = generate_hierarchy(df)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy has {len(hier_df)} unique paths\")
    
    # Save outputs
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    df[output_cols].to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    
    print(f\"\\nDone"'! Output files saved to {OUTPUT_DIR}")
    print(f"  Full mapping: {len(df)} records")
    print(f"  Hierarchy: {len(hier_df)} unique paths")
    print(f"  L1 categories: {df['"'unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    print(f\"  Source distribution:\")
    for src in df['source'].unique():
        print(f\"    {src}: {(df['source'] == src).sum()}\")
    
    # Check cross-source
    path_counts = df['category_path'].value_counts()
    multi = (path_counts > 1).sum()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {multi}\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories into a unified 5-level taxonomy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.30
OUTPUT_DIR = '"'/root/output'

# ============================================================
# TEXT UTILITIES
# ============================================================

def sanitize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Remove special characters, keep alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, commas, ampersands, apostrophes.\"\"\"
    if pd.isna(text) or not isinstance(text, str):
        return text
    text = re.sub(r'[|/\\\\[\\]{}()@#"'$%''^*+=~`<>]'"', ' ', str(text))
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text

def get_word_set(text):
    \"\"\"Get set of lowercase meaningful words from text.\"\"\"
    if not text or pd.isna(text):
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def word_overlap_ratio(text1, text2):
    \"\"\"Compute Jaccard overlap between word sets of two texts.\"\"\"
    s1 = get_word_set(text1)
    s2 = get_word_set(text2)
    if not s1 or not s2:
        return 0.0
    return len(s1 & s2) / len(s1 | s2)

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Remove words from child name that appear in any ancestor name.\"\"\"
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    # Split on spaces, filter out words that match ancestor words
    child_tokens = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_lower = token.lower().strip(',-')
        if token_lower in ancestor_words:
            continue
        if token in ('&', ',', '-'):
            filtered.append(token)
        else:
            filtered.append(token)
    
    # Actually filter: remove tokens whose lowercase stripped form is in ancestor_words
    filtered2 = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_clean = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z]'"', '', token).lower()
        if len(token_clean) > 2 and token_clean in ancestor_words:
            continue
        filtered2.append(token)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered2).strip()
    # Clean up dangling separators
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,'"\\-]\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,\\-]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    \"\"\"Load all three source files and normalize.\"\"\"
    frames = []

    df = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)

    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove paths that are prefixes of longer paths within the same source.
    But keep paths that exist in multiple sources (cross-source overlap).\"\"\"
    # First, find paths that exist in multiple sources (case-insensitive)
    path_lower_to_sources = defaultdict(set)
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        path_lower_to_sources[row['category_path'].lower()].add(row['source'])
    
    multi_source_paths = {p for p, sources in path_lower_to_sources.items() if len(sources) > 1}
    
    result_frames = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        sdf = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(sdf['category_path'])
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    # Don't remove if it's a multi-source path
                    if prefix.lower() not in multi_source_paths:
                        prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        sdf = sdf[~sdf['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)]
        result_frames.append(sdf)
    return pd.concat(result_frames, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_and_compute_depth(df):
    \"\"\"Truncate paths to max 5 levels and compute depth.\"\"\"
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    df['depth'] = parts_series.str.len().clip(upper=MAX_DEPTH)
    df['category_path'] = parts_series.apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p[:MAX_DEPTH]))
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    return df

    # Amazon L1
    'Appliances': 'Home Garden Furniture',

    'Automotive': 'Automotive Vehicles Parts',
    'Baby Products': 'Baby Products',
