# ============================================================
# L2+ TAXONOMY BUILDING
# ============================================================

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    \"\"\"Build L2 categories by grouping source L2 names under each unified L1.\"\"\"
    l2_map = {}
    
    for l1_unified in df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        subset = df[df['unified_level_1'] == l1_unified].copy()
        l2_names = subset['category_path'].str.split(' > ').apply(
            lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None
        ).dropna()
        
        if len(l2_names) == 0:
            continue
        
        l2_counter = Counter()
        l2_canonical = {}
        for name in l2_names:
            key = name.strip().lower()
            l2_counter[key] += 1
            if key not in l2_canonical:
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
            elif name[0].isupper():
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
        
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counter.keys())
        merged_groups = []
        used = set()
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counter[x])
        
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in used:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            used.add(l2)
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in used:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(l2, other) > 0.5:
                    group.append(other)
                    used.add(other)
            merged_groups.append(group)
        
        # If too many groups, merge smallest ones
        if len(merged_groups) > MAX_CHILDREN:
            merged_groups.sort(key=lambda g: -sum(l2_counter[m] for m in g))
            top = merged_groups[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1]
            rest = merged_groups[MAX_CHILDREN - 1:]
            # Merge rest into most similar top group
            for rg in rest:
                best_idx = 0
                best_score = -1
                for i, tg in enumerate(top):
                    score = word_overlap_ratio(rg[0], tg[0])
                    if score > best_score:
                        best_score = score
                        best_idx = i
                top[best_idx].extend(rg)
            merged_groups = top
        
        for group in merged_groups:
            representative = max(group, key=lambda x: l2_counter[x])
            canonical = l2_canonical[representative]
            canonical = sanitize_text(canonical)
            if canonical:
                canonical = canonical.title()
            
            for member in group:
                l2_map[(l1_unified, member)] = canonical
    
    return l2_map

def assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map):
    \"\"\"Assign unified_level_2 through unified_level_5 based on source path parts.\"\"\"
    
    def get_l2(row):
        parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
        if len(parts) < 2:
            return np.nan
        l1 = row['unified_level_1']
        l2_key = parts[1].strip().lower()
        return l2_map.get((l1, l2_key), sanitize_text(parts[1].strip()).title())
    
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(get_l2, axis=1)
    
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        def get_level(row, lvl=level):
            parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
            if len(parts) < lvl:
                return np.nan
            return sanitize_text(parts[lvl - 1].strip()).title()
        df[f'unified_level_{level}'] = df.apply(get_level, axis=1)
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# POST-PROCESSING: ENFORCE CONSTRAINTS
# ============================================================

def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure child category names don't contain words from parent names.\"\"\"
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        ancestors = []
        if pd.notna(row.get('unified_level_1')):
            ancestors.append(row['unified_level_1'])
        
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row.get(col)):
                break
            current_name = row[col]
            new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(current_name, ancestors)
            df.at[idx, col] = new_name
            ancestors.append(new_name)
    
    return df

def enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_col_pairs):
    \"\"\"Ensure each parent has <= MAX_CHILDREN children.\"\"\"
    for parent_col, child_col in hierarchy_col_pairs:
        if child_col not in df.columns:
            continue
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            children = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique()
            
            if len(children) > MAX_CHILDREN:
                child_counts = df.loc[mask, child_col].value_counts()
                top_children = list(child_counts.index[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1])
                others = [c for c in children if c not in top_children]
                if others:
                    for other_child in others:
                        best_match = None
                        best_score = -1
                        for top_child in top_children:
                            score = word_overlap_ratio(other_child, top_child)
                            if score > best_score:
                                best_score = score
                                best_match = top_child
                        if best_match:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == other_child), child_col] = best_match
    
    return df

def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            siblings = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique().tolist()
            
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            merged = {}
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    s1, s2 = siblings[i], siblings[j]
                    if s1 in merged or s2 in merged:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(s1, s2)
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        count1 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s2), child_col] = s1
                            merged[s2] = s1
                        else:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s1), child_col] = s2
                            merged[s1] = s2
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# HIERARCHY GENERATION
# ============================================================

def generate_hierarchy(df):
    \"\"\"Generate hierarchy CSV from the full mapping, including all intermediate paths.\"\"\"
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    
    all_paths = set()
    
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        path = []
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                path.append(str(row[col]))
            else:
                break
        
        for i in range(1, len(path) + 1):
            prefix = tuple(path[:i])
            all_paths.add(prefix)
    
    rows = []
    for path in sorted(all_paths):
        row = {}
        for i, col in enumerate(level_cols):
            if i < len(path):
                row[col] = path[i]
            else:
                row[col] = np.nan
        rows.append(row)
    
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=level_cols)
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(level_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    
    return hier_df

# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================

def main():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Loaded {len(df)} total records\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_and_compute_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After truncation: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Assigning unified L1...\")
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    source_l1 = parts_series.str[0]
    df['unified_level_1'] = source_l1.map(UNIFIED_L1_MAP)
    
    unmapped = df['unified_level_1'].isna()
    if unmapped.any():
        print(f\"  WARNING: {unmapped.sum()} records with unmapped L1\")
        df.loc[unmapped, 'unified_level_1'] = 'General Merchandise'
    
    print(f\"  Unified L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    
    print(\"Building L2 mapping...\")
    l2_map = build_l2_mapping(df)
    
    print(\"Assigning unified levels 2-5...\")
    df = assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map)
    
    print(\"Sanitizing all level names...\")
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    hierarchy_pairs = [
        ('unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2'),
        ('unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3'),
        ('unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4'),
        ('unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5'),
    ]
    df = enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_pairs)
    
    # Re-sanitize after all transformations
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    # Set levels beyond depth to NaN
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        depth = row['depth']
        for level in range(int(depth) + 1, 6):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = np.nan
    
    print(\"Generating hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = generate_hierarchy(df)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy has {len(hier_df)} unique paths\")
    
    # Save outputs
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    df[output_cols].to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    
    print(f\"\\nDone"'! Output files saved to {OUTPUT_DIR}")
    print(f"  Full mapping: {len(df)} records")
    print(f"  Hierarchy: {len(hier_df)} unique paths")
    print(f"  L1 categories: {df['"'unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    print(f\"  Source distribution:\")
    for src in df['source'].unique():
        print(f\"    {src}: {(df['source'] == src).sum()}\")
    
    # Check cross-source
    path_counts = df['category_path'].value_counts()
    multi = (path_counts > 1).sum()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {multi}\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping categories into a unified 5-level taxonomy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.30

def sanitize_text(text):

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text or pd.isna(text):
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:

    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:

    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    l2_map = {}
    for l1_unified in df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        subset = df[df['unified_level_1'] == l1_unified].copy()
        l2_names = subset['category_path'].str.split(' > ').apply(
            lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None
        ).dropna()
        if len(l2_names) == 0:
            continue
        l2_counter = Counter()
        l2_canonical = {}
        for name in l2_names:
            key = name.strip().lower()
            l2_counter[key] += 1
            if key not in l2_canonical:
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
            elif name[0].isupper():
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counter.keys())
        merged_groups = []
        used = set()
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counter[x])
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in used:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            used.add(l2)
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in used:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(l2, other) > 0.5:
                    group.append(other)
                    used.add(other)
            merged_groups.append(group)
        if len(merged_groups) > MAX_CHILDREN:
            merged_groups.sort(key=lambda g: -sum(l2_counter[m] for m in g))
            top = merged_groups[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1]
            rest = merged_groups[MAX_CHILDREN - 1:]
            for rg in rest:
                best_idx = 0
                best_score = -1
                for i, tg in enumerate(top):
                    score = word_overlap_ratio(rg[0], tg[0])
                    if score > best_score:
                        best_score = score
                        best_idx = i
                top[best_idx].extend(rg)
            merged_groups = top
        for group in merged_groups:
            representative = max(group, key=lambda x: l2_counter[x])
            canonical = l2_canonical[representative]
            canonical = sanitize_text(canonical)
            if canonical:
                canonical = canonical.title()
            for member in group:
                l2_map[(l1_unified, member)] = canonical
    return l2_map

def assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map):
    def get_l2(row):
        parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
        if len(parts) < 2:
            return np.nan
        l1 = row['unified_level_1']
        l2_key = parts[1].strip().lower()
        return l2_map.get((l1, l2_key), sanitize_text(parts[1].strip()).title())
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(get_l2, axis=1)
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        def get_level(row, lvl=level):
            parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
            if len(parts) < lvl:
                return np.nan
            return sanitize_text(parts[lvl - 1].strip()).title()
        df[f'unified_level_{level}'] = df.apply(get_level, axis=1)
    return df

def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        ancestors = []
        if pd.notna(row.get('unified_level_1')):
            ancestors.append(row['unified_level_1'])
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row.get(col)):
                break
            current_name = row[col]
            new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(current_name, ancestors)
            df.at[idx, col] = new_name
            ancestors.append(new_name)
    return df

def enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_col_pairs):
    for parent_col, child_col in hierarchy_col_pairs:
        if child_col not in df.columns:
            continue
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            children = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique()
            if len(children) > MAX_CHILDREN:
                child_counts = df.loc[mask, child_col].value_counts()
                top_children = list(child_counts.index[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1])
                others = [c for c in children if c not in top_children]
                for other_child in others:
                    best_match = None
                    best_score = -1
                    for top_child in top_children:
                        score = word_overlap_ratio(other_child, top_child)
                        if score > best_score:
                            best_score = score
                            best_match = top_child
                    if best_match:
                        df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == other_child), child_col] = best_match
    return df

def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            siblings = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique().tolist()
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            merged = {}
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    s1, s2 = siblings[i], siblings[j]
                    if s1 in merged or s2 in merged:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(s1, s2)
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        count1 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s2), child_col] = s1
                            merged[s2] = s1
                        else:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s1), child_col] = s2
                            merged[s1] = s2
    return df

def generate_hierarchy(df):
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    all_paths = set()
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        path = []
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                path.append(str(row[col]))
            else:
                break
        for i in range(1, len(path) + 1):
            all_paths.add(tuple(path[:i]))
    rows = []
    for path in sorted(all_paths):
        row = {}
        for i, col in enumerate(level_cols):
            row[col] = path[i] if i < len(path) else np.nan
        rows.append(row)
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=level_cols)
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(level_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    return hier_df

def main():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Loaded {len(df)} total records\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_and_compute_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After truncation: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Assigning unified L1...\")
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    source_l1 = parts_series.str[0]
    df['unified_level_1'] = source_l1.map(UNIFIED_L1_MAP)
    unmapped = df['unified_level_1'].isna()
    if unmapped.any():
        print(f\"  WARNING: {unmapped.sum()} records with unmapped L1\")
        df.loc[unmapped, 'unified_level_1'] = 'General Merchandise'
    print(f\"  Unified L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    
    print(\"Building L2 mapping...\")
    l2_map = build_l2_mapping(df)
    
    print(\"Assigning unified levels 2-5...\")
    df = assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map)
    
    print(\"Sanitizing all level names...\")
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    hierarchy_pairs = [
        ('unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2'),
        ('unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3'),
        ('unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4'),
        ('unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5'),
    ]
    df = enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_pairs)
    
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        depth = row['depth']
        for level in range(int(depth) + 1, 6):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = np.nan
    
    print(\"Generating hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = generate_hierarchy(df)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy has {len(hier_df)} unique paths\")
    
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    df[output_cols].to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    
    print(f\"\\nDone"'!")
    print(f"  Full: {len(df)} records, Hierarchy: {len(hier_df)} paths")
    print(f"  L1 categories: {df['"'unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    for src in df['source'].unique():
        print(f\"    {src}: {(df['source'] == src).sum()}\")
    path_counts = df['category_path'].value_counts()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {(path_counts > 1).sum()}\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

    'Toys & Games': 'Toys Games',
    'Video Games': 'Electronics',
}

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    l2_map = {}
    for l1_unified in df['unified_level_1'].unique():
        subset = df[df['unified_level_1'] == l1_unified].copy()
        l2_names = subset['category_path'].str.split(' > ').apply(
            lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None
        ).dropna()
        if len(l2_names) == 0:
            continue
        l2_counter = Counter()
        l2_canonical = {}
        for name in l2_names:
            key = name.strip().lower()
            l2_counter[key] += 1
            if key not in l2_canonical:
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
            elif name[0].isupper():
                l2_canonical[key] = name.strip()
        unique_l2s = list(l2_counter.keys())
        merged_groups = []
        used = set()
        sorted_l2s = sorted(unique_l2s, key=lambda x: -l2_counter[x])
        for l2 in sorted_l2s:
            if l2 in used:
                continue
            group = [l2]
            used.add(l2)
            for other in sorted_l2s:
                if other in used:
                    continue
                if word_overlap_ratio(l2, other) > 0.5:
                    group.append(other)
                    used.add(other)
            merged_groups.append(group)
        if len(merged_groups) > MAX_CHILDREN:
            merged_groups.sort(key=lambda g: -sum(l2_counter[m] for m in g))
            top = merged_groups[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1]
            rest = merged_groups[MAX_CHILDREN - 1:]
            for rg in rest:
                best_idx = 0
                best_score = -1
                for i, tg in enumerate(top):
                    score = word_overlap_ratio(rg[0], tg[0])
                    if score > best_score:
                        best_score = score
                        best_idx = i
                top[best_idx].extend(rg)
            merged_groups = top
        for group in merged_groups:
            representative = max(group, key=lambda x: l2_counter[x])
            canonical = l2_canonical[representative]
            canonical = sanitize_text(canonical)
            if canonical:
                canonical = canonical.title()
            for member in group:
                l2_map[(l1_unified, member)] = canonical
    return l2_map

def assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map):
    def get_l2(row):
        parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
        if len(parts) < 2:
            return np.nan
        l1 = row['unified_level_1']
        l2_key = parts[1].strip().lower()
        return l2_map.get((l1, l2_key), sanitize_text(parts[1].strip()).title())
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(get_l2, axis=1)
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        def get_level(row, lvl=level):
            parts = row['category_path'].split(' > ')
            if len(parts) < lvl:
                return np.nan
            return sanitize_text(parts[lvl - 1].strip()).title()
        df[f'unified_level_{level}'] = df.apply(get_level, axis=1)
    return df

def enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        ancestors = []
        if pd.notna(row.get('unified_level_1')):
            ancestors.append(row['unified_level_1'])
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            if pd.isna(row.get(col)):
                break
            current_name = row[col]
            new_name = remove_parent_words_from_name(current_name, ancestors)
            df.at[idx, col] = new_name
            ancestors.append(new_name)
    return df

def enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_col_pairs):
    for parent_col, child_col in hierarchy_col_pairs:
        if child_col not in df.columns:
            continue
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            children = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique()
            if len(children) > MAX_CHILDREN:
                child_counts = df.loc[mask, child_col].value_counts()
                top_children = list(child_counts.index[:MAX_CHILDREN - 1])
                others = [c for c in children if c not in top_children]
                for other_child in others:
                    best_match = None
                    best_score = -1
                    for top_child in top_children:
                        score = word_overlap_ratio(other_child, top_child)
                        if score > best_score:
                            best_score = score
                            best_match = top_child
                    if best_match:
                        df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == other_child), child_col] = best_match
    return df

def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_col = f'unified_level_{level - 1}'
        valid = df[df[child_col].notna() & df[parent_col].notna()].copy()
        for parent_val in valid[parent_col].unique():
            mask = (df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col].notna())
            siblings = df.loc[mask, child_col].unique().tolist()
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            merged = {}
            for i in range(len(siblings)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(siblings)):
                    s1, s2 = siblings[i], siblings[j]
                    if s1 in merged or s2 in merged:
                        continue
                    overlap = word_overlap_ratio(s1, s2)
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        count1 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (df.loc[mask, child_col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 >= count2:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s2), child_col] = s1
                            merged[s2] = s1
                        else:
                            df.loc[(df[parent_col] == parent_val) & (df[child_col] == s1), child_col] = s2
                            merged[s1] = s2
    return df

def generate_hierarchy(df):
    level_cols = ['unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    all_paths = set()
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        path = []
        for col in level_cols:
            if pd.notna(row[col]):
                path.append(str(row[col]))
            else:
                break
        for i in range(1, len(path) + 1):
            all_paths.add(tuple(path[:i]))
    rows = []
    for path in sorted(all_paths):
        row = {}
        for i, col in enumerate(level_cols):
            row[col] = path[i] if i < len(path) else np.nan
        rows.append(row)
    hier_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=level_cols)
    hier_df = hier_df.drop_duplicates()
    hier_df = hier_df.sort_values(level_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    return hier_df

def main():
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Loaded {len(df)} total records\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_and_compute_depth(df)
    print(f\"  After truncation: {len(df)} records\")
    
    print(\"Assigning unified L1...\")
    parts_series = df['category_path'].str.split(' > ')
    source_l1 = parts_series.str[0]
    df['unified_level_1'] = source_l1.map(UNIFIED_L1_MAP)
    unmapped = df['unified_level_1'].isna()
    if unmapped.any():
        print(f\"  WARNING: {unmapped.sum()} records with unmapped L1\")
        df.loc[unmapped, 'unified_level_1'] = 'General Merchandise'
    print(f\"  Unified L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    
    print(\"Building L2 mapping...\")
    l2_map = build_l2_mapping(df)
    
    print(\"Assigning unified levels 2-5...\")
    df = assign_unified_levels(df, l2_map)
    
    print(\"Sanitizing all level names...\")
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    print(\"Enforcing parent word exclusion...\")
    df = enforce_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    print(\"Enforcing children count limits...\")
    hierarchy_pairs = [
        ('unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2'),
        ('unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3'),
        ('unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4'),
        ('unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5'),
    ]
    df = enforce_children_count(df, hierarchy_pairs)
    
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_text(x) if pd.notna(x) else x)
    
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        depth = row['depth']
        for level in range(int(depth) + 1, 6):
            df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = np.nan
    
    print(\"Generating hierarchy...\")
    hier_df = generate_hierarchy(df)
    print(f\"  Hierarchy has {len(hier_df)} unique paths\")
    
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth', 'unified_level_1', 'unified_level_2', 'unified_level_3', 'unified_level_4', 'unified_level_5']
    df[output_cols].to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_full.csv'), index=False)
    hier_df.to_csv(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv'), index=False)
    
    print(f\"\\nDone"'!")
    print(f"  Full: {len(df)} records, Hierarchy: {len(hier_df)} paths")
    print(f"  L1 categories: {df['"'unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    for src in df['source'].unique():
        print(f\"    {src}: {(df['source'] == src).sum()}\")
    path_counts = df['category_path'].value_counts()
    print(f\"  Cross-source paths: {(path_counts > 1).sum()}\")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

import re

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    child_tokens = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_clean = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z]'"', '', token).lower()
        if len(token_clean) > 2 and token_clean in ancestor_words:
            continue
        filtered.append(token)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered).strip()
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,'"\\-]\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,\\-]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# Test
result = remove_parent_words_from_name('Arts & Crafts', ['Arts Crafts Entertainment'])
print(f'Result: \\\"{result}\\\"')
# Both 'Arts' and 'Crafts' are removed, leaving only '&', which has no word_set -> returns original
\"

import pandas as pd
import re
from collections import Counter

hier = pd.read_csv('/root/output/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv')

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 2}

# Count violations by parent
parent_violations = Counter()
for idx, row in hier.iterrows():
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        if pd.isna(row[child_col]):
            continue
        child_words = get_word_set(str(row[child_col]))
        for plevel in range(1, level):
            parent_col = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
            if pd.isna(row[parent_col]):
                continue
            parent_words = get_word_set(str(row[parent_col]))
            overlap = child_words & parent_words
            if overlap:
                key = f'L{plevel}:{row[parent_col]} -> L{level}:{row[child_col]} [{overlap}]'
                parent_violations[key] += 1
                break

print(f'Total unique violation patterns: {len(parent_violations)}')
for pattern, count in parent_violations.most_common(30):
    print(f'  {count}x {pattern}')

import pandas as pd
import re
from collections import Counter

hier = pd.read_csv('/root/output/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv')

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 2}

# Count violations by parent

    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        if pd.isna(row[child_col]):
            continue
        child_words = get_word_set(str(row[child_col]))
        for plevel in range(1, level):
            parent_col = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
            if pd.isna(row[parent_col]):
                continue
            parent_words = get_word_set(str(row[parent_col]))
            overlap = child_words & parent_words
            if overlap:
                key = f'L{plevel}:{row[parent_col]} -> L{level}:{row[child_col]} [{overlap}]'
                parent_violations[key] += 1
                break

print(f'Total unique violation patterns: {len(parent_violations)}')
for pattern, count in parent_violations.most_common(30):
    print(f'  {count}x {pattern}')

/bin/bash -lc "python3 -c \"
import re

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    child_tokens = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_clean = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z]'"', '', token).lower()
        if len(token_clean) > 2 and token_clean in ancestor_words:
            continue
        filtered.append(token)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered).strip()
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,'"\\-]\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,\\-]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# Test
result = remove_parent_words_from_name('Arts & Crafts', ['Arts Crafts Entertainment'])

import re
from collections import Counter

hier = pd.read_csv('/root/output/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv')

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 2}

# Count violations by parent
parent_violations = Counter()
for idx, row in hier.iterrows():
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        if pd.isna(row[child_col]):
            continue
        child_words = get_word_set(str(row[child_col]))
        for plevel in range(1, level):

            if pd.isna(row[parent_col]):
                continue

            overlap = child_words & parent_words
            if overlap:
                key = f'L{plevel}:{row[parent_col]} -> L{level}:{row[child_col]} [{overlap}]'
                parent_violations[key] += 1
                break

print(f'Total unique violation patterns: {len(parent_violations)}')
for pattern, count in parent_violations.most_common(30):
    print(f'  {count}x {pattern}')

import re

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 1}

def remove_parent_words_from_name(child_name, ancestor_names):
    if not child_name or not ancestor_names:
        return child_name
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        if aname:
            ancestor_words.update(get_word_set(aname))
    
    child_tokens = child_name.split()
    filtered = []
    for token in child_tokens:
        token_clean = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z]'"', '', token).lower()
        if len(token_clean) > 2 and token_clean in ancestor_words:
            continue
        filtered.append(token)
    
    result = ' '.join(filtered).strip()
    result = re.sub(r'"'^[&,'"\\-]\\s*', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s*[&,\\-]"'$'"', '', result)
    result = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', result).strip()
    
    if not result or len(get_word_set(result)) == 0:
        return child_name
    return result

# Test
result = remove_parent_words_from_name('Arts & Crafts', ['Arts Crafts Entertainment'])
print(f'Result: \\\"{result}\\\"')
# Both 'Arts' and 'Crafts' are removed, leaving only '&', which has no word_set -> returns original
\"
"

import pandas as pd
import re
from collections import Counter

hier = pd.read_csv('/root/output/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv')

def get_word_set(text):
    if not text:
        return set()
    stop_words = {'&', 'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'a', 'an', 'to', 'with', 'by', 'or', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its'}
    words = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z'"\\s]', ' ', str(text).lower()).split()
    return {w for w in words if w not in stop_words and len(w) > 2}

# Count violations by parent
parent_violations = Counter()
for idx, row in hier.iterrows():
    for level in range(2, 6):
        child_col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        if pd.isna(row[child_col]):
            continue
        child_words = get_word_set(str(row[child_col]))
        for plevel in range(1, level):
            parent_col = f'unified_level_{plevel}'
            if pd.isna(row[parent_col]):
                continue
            parent_words = get_word_set(str(row[parent_col]))
            overlap = child_words & parent_words
            if overlap:
                key = f'L{plevel}:{row[parent_col]} -> L{level}:{row[child_col]} [{overlap}]'
                parent_violations[key] += 1
                break

print(f'Total unique violation patterns: {len(parent_violations)}')
for pattern, count in parent_violations.most_common(30):
    print(f'  {count}x {pattern}')

## Exploration Memo (9 failed attempts)

### Attempts Log
- #1: Built full taxonomy merge pipeline with L1 mapping, L2 grouping, hierarchy generation. 17/22 passed. Failed: path_representativeness, sibling_distinctiveness, children_count_limit, hierarchy_coverage, special_characters_removed.
- #2: Added sibling distinctiveness (word overlap dedup), children count validation, special char normalization. 18/22 passed (PREVIOUS HIGH WATER MARK). Fixed sibling_distinctiveness and children_count_limit. Still failing: parent_word_exclusion, path_representativeness, hierarchy_coverage, special_characters_removed.
- #3: Rewrote script with parent word exclusion, special char stripping, hierarchy coverage from full_df. Regressed to 16/22. Lost category_naming_constraints and children_count_limit.
- #4: Full rewrite with final_fix_parent_words, enforce_children_count, enforce_sibling_distinctiveness, sanitize. 15/22 passed. MAJOR REGRESSION.
- #5: Diagnostic-heavy attempt. 16/22 passed.
- #6: Diagnostic attempt. 16/22 passed.
- #7: Read test file (partially). Recovered cross_source_deduplication and hierarchy_coverage. 17/22 passed.
- #8: Started rewriting build_taxonomy.py but SCRIPT WAS NEVER COMPLETED OR RUN. 0/22 passed.
- #9: Diagnostic-only attempt analyzing violations. Recovered children_count_limit. 17/22 passed. Same 5 tests still failing. Identified that parent_word_exclusion has a fallback-to-original problem when all child words overlap with parent (e.g., "Arts & Crafts" under "Arts Crafts Entertainment" → all meaningful words removed → falls back to original violating name). hierarchy_coverage regressed again.

### Commands From Last Attempt
```
# Diagnostic: checked hierarchy_coverage — missing paths between full and hierarchy files
# Diagnostic: checked Facebook "other" paths
# Diagnostic: checked "General Merchandise" L1 entries
# Diagnostic: checked naming violations (>5 words)
# Diagnostic: parent_word_exclusion analysis — found violation rate and top 30 violation patterns
# Diagnostic: tested remove_parent_words_from_name with "Arts & Crafts" under "Arts Crafts Entertainment"
#   Result: both "arts" and "crafts" removed, leaving only "&", which has no word_set → returns ORIGINAL name (violation!)
# NO code changes were made to build_taxonomy.py
# NO script execution
```

### Verified Facts
- **14 tests are ROCK SOLID** (never failed when output exists): output_files_exist, full_mapping_format, hierarchy_format, source_preservation, depth_filtering, prefix_removal, hierarchical_structure, pyramid_distribution, cluster_size_balance, lemmatization_applied, hierarchy_depth_consistency, mapping_completeness, source_balance, no_empty_clusters
- **category_naming_constraints**: stable (failed only in attempt 3)
- **cross_source_deduplication**: stable since attempt 7 (passed in 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9)
- **children_count_limit**: passed in attempts 2, 6, 9; fragile — merge logic needs care
- **hierarchy_coverage**: passed ONLY in attempt 7; regressed in attempt 9. Root cause: hierarchy must be regenerated AFTER all renaming/sanitization so names match between full and hierarchy files exactly.
- **sibling_distinctiveness**: passed in attempts 2, 3 only. Attempt 2's word-overlap dedup logic worked. Currently failing again — the dedup logic was likely lost or weakened in later rewrites.
- **parent_word_exclusion**: NEVER passed (0/9). ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED: when a child name's meaningful words are ALL present in ancestor names, the function falls back to the original name (to avoid empty names), which still violates. Example: "Arts & Crafts" under "Arts Crafts Entertainment" — "arts" and "crafts" both in parent → all removed → fallback to original → violation. Need a DIFFERENT naming strategy: use alternative words from source paths or generate a synonym/abbreviation instead of falling back.
- **path_representativeness**: NEVER passed (0/9). L1/L2 names don't match source path words well enough. Need to derive names from actual frequent words in source category paths.
- **special_characters_removed**: NEVER passed (0/9). Likely still using pipe `|` or other special chars (`&`, `/`, etc.) in category names. Must strip ALL non-alphanumeric-space characters.

### Current Error Pattern
5 tests failing, 17 passing. The 5 failures are the same persistent ones:
1. **special_characters_removed** — names likely contain `&`, `/`, or other special chars. Need aggressive sanitization removing ALL non-alpha-space characters.
2. **parent_word_exclusion** — fallback-to-original when all words overlap with parent. Need to generate ALTERNATIVE names (from source paths, synonyms, or sub-category words) instead of falling back.
3. **path_representativeness** — L1/L2 names not derived from actual source path words. Need TF-IDF or frequency-based naming from source paths.
4. **sibling_distinctiveness** — word-overlap dedup logic from attempt 2 is missing or broken. Need to re-add it.
5. **hierarchy_coverage** — hierarchy file doesn't match full file after renaming. Must regenerate hierarchy FROM the final full mapping.

### Next Strategy
**READ THE FULL TEST FILE FIRST** — we still haven't read the complete test implementations for all 5 failing tests. This is critical to understand exact thresholds and logic.

Then apply TARGETED fixes to the current working script (attempt 9 base, 17/22):

1. **special_characters_removed**: After ALL naming, run `re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9\s]', '', name).strip()` on every category name at every level. This removes `&`, `/`, `|`, `(`, `)`, etc. Do this BEFORE hierarchy generation.

2. **parent_word_exclusion**: When all meaningful child words overlap with parent, instead of falling back to original, use the MOST FREQUENT non-overlapping word from the source paths in that cluster. If no such word exists, use a generic differentiator (e.g., "Supplies", "Items", "Products") that isn't in the parent.

3. **path_representativeness**: Name L1/L2 categories using the top-N most frequent meaningful words extracted from the source category_path strings in that cluster. This ensures names naturally match source paths.

4. **sibling_distinctiveness**: Re-implement attempt 2's approach — after naming all siblings at a level, compute pairwise word overlap ratio. If overlap > 0.3, merge the smaller group into the larger one or rename to differentiate.

5. **hierarchy_coverage**: Generate the hierarchy file as the LAST step, directly from the final unified_taxonomy_full.csv data, extracting unique paths. This guarantees consistency.

**EXECUTION RULE**: Write the COMPLETE modified script in ONE file-write operation. Run it immediately. Then run pytest. Do NOT split writes or do diagnostics without running the script.

# Skill: E-Commerce Taxonomy Data Processing

## Purpose
Process and normalize product category data from multiple e-commerce platforms (Amazon, Facebook, Google Shopping) into a unified format.

## Key Techniques

### 1. Loading Multi-Format CSVs
"'```python
import pandas as pd

# Amazon: single column '"'category_path', some quoted with commas
amazon = pd.read_csv('amazon.csv')
# Facebook: has 'category_id' and 'category_path' columns
fb = pd.read_csv('fb.csv')
# Google: single column 'category_path'
google = pd.read_csv('google.csv')
"'```

### 2. Text Normalization Pipeline
```python
import re

def normalize_category(text):
    """Normalize category text: lowercase, remove special chars, strip."""
    text = text.lower().strip().strip('"'\"')
    # Remove ALL special characters except spaces and alphanumeric
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', text)
    # Collapse multiple spaces
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    return text
"'```

### 3. Path Parsing and Depth Calculation
```python
def parse_path(raw_path):
    """Split category path by '"' > ' delimiter, return list of levels.\"\"\"
    parts = raw_path.split(' > ')
    return [p.strip().strip('\"') for p in parts]

def get_depth(path):
    return len(parse_path(path))
"'```

### 4. Prefix Removal (Leaf-Only Retention)
```python
def remove_prefix_paths(paths):
    """Remove paths that are prefixes of longer paths."""
    path_set = set(paths)
    to_remove = set()
    for path in path_set:
        parts = path.split('"' > ')
        for i in range(1, len(parts)):
            prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
            if prefix in path_set:
                to_remove.add(prefix)
    return path_set - to_remove
"'```

### 5. Depth Truncation to 5 Levels
```python
def truncate_to_depth(path, max_depth=5):
    parts = path.split('"' > ')
    return ' > '.join(parts[:max_depth])
"'```

## Important Notes
- Always strip quotes from Amazon paths (some have embedded commas)
- Facebook paths are lowercase; Amazon/Google are title case
- Normalize all to title case for consistency
- Remove special characters BEFORE any clustering/naming

# Skill: Hierarchical Taxonomy Clustering

## Purpose
Build a unified 5-level hierarchical taxonomy from multiple category sources using word-frequency-based clustering and naming.

## Key Techniques

### 1. L1 Category Mapping
Map source-specific top-level categories to unified L1 categories using a manually curated mapping based on semantic similarity.

"'```python
L1_MAPPING = {
    # Amazon L1 -> Unified L1
    '"'electronics': 'Electronics',
    'clothing shoes jewelry': 'Clothing Accessories',
    # ... etc
}
"'```

### 2. Frequency-Based Naming (Path Representativeness)
Names should be derived from the most frequent words in the source category paths within each cluster.

```python
from collections import Counter

def get_representative_name(paths, max_words=4):
    """Extract most frequent meaningful words from paths."""
    word_counts = Counter()
    stopwords = {'"'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'or', 'with'}
    for path in paths:
        words = set(normalize(path).split())
        words -= stopwords
        word_counts.update(words)
    top_words = [w for w, _ in word_counts.most_common(max_words)]
    return ' '.join(sorted(top_words[:3]))  # Use top 3 words
"'```

### 3. Parent Word Exclusion
Child category names must NOT contain words from their parent/ancestor names.

```python
def remove_parent_words(child_name, ancestor_names):
    """Remove words that appear in any ancestor name."""
    ancestor_words = set()
    for name in ancestor_names:
        ancestor_words.update(name.lower().split())
    child_words = child_name.split()
    filtered = [w for w in child_words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
    if not filtered:
        # CRITICAL: Don'"'t fall back to original"'! Use alternative strategy
        # Use the most distinctive word from source paths NOT in ancestors
        return generate_alternative_name(child_name, ancestor_words)
    return '"' '.join(filtered)
"'```

### 4. Sibling Distinctiveness (< 30% Word Overlap)
```python
def check_sibling_overlap(names):
    """Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap."""
    for i, name1 in enumerate(names):
        words1 = set(name1.lower().split())
        for j, name2 in enumerate(names):
            if i >= j:
                continue
            words2 = set(name2.lower().split())
            if not words1 or not words2:
                continue
            overlap = len(words1 & words2) / min(len(words1), len(words2))
            if overlap >= 0.3:
                # Merge or rename the smaller group
                pass
```

### 5. Children Count Limits (3-20 per parent)
Each parent should have between 3 and 20 children. If too many, merge small ones. If too few, absorb into parent.

### 6. Pyramid Structure
L1: 10-20 categories
L2: 3-20 per L1 parent
L3: 3-20 per L2 parent
L4-L5: 3-20 per parent

## Critical Rules
- Remove ALL special characters from names (no &, /, |, etc.)
- Use only alphanumeric and spaces in category names
- Max 5 words per category name
- Names must be representative (70%+) of subcategories

# Skill: Taxonomy Naming and Sanitization

## Purpose
Ensure all category names in the unified taxonomy meet strict naming constraints:
no special characters, no parent word overlap, sibling distinctiveness, and representativeness.

## Key Techniques

### 1. Special Character Removal (CRITICAL)
"'```python
import re

def sanitize_name(name):
    """Remove ALL non-alphanumeric characters except spaces."""
    if not name or pd.isna(name):
        return name
    # Remove everything that'"'s not a letter, digit, or space
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', str(name))
    # Collapse whitespace
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    # Title case
    name = name.title()
    return name
"'```

### 2. Word Limit Enforcement (max 5 words)
```python
def enforce_word_limit(name, max_words=5):
    words = name.split()
