#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping category taxonomies into a single 5-level hierarchy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
MAX_WORDS = 5
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.3

os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ============================================================
# TEXT NORMALIZATION
# ============================================================

STOPWORDS = {
    'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'or', 'with',
    'by', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its', 'other', 'all',
    'non', 'pre', 'vs'
}

def sanitize_name(name):
    \"\"\"Remove ALL special characters, keep only alphanumeric and spaces.\"\"\"
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    return name

def title_case(name):
    \"\"\"Title case a name.\"\"\"
    if not name:
        return name
    return ' '.join(w.capitalize() for w in name.split())

def normalize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Full normalization: lowercase, remove special chars, strip.\"\"\"
    if not text or not isinstance(text, str):
        return ''
    text = text.strip().strip('\"').strip()
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip().lower()
    return text

def basic_lemmatize(word):
    \"\"\"Basic lemmatization for common English plural patterns.\"\"\"
    w = word.lower()
    if w.endswith('ies') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'y'
    if w.endswith('ves') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'f'
    if w.endswith('ses') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-2]
    if w.endswith('s') and not w.endswith('ss') and len(w) > 3:
        return w[:-1]
    return w

def lemmatize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Lemmatize all words in text.\"\"\"
    return ' '.join(basic_lemmatize(w) for w in text.split())

def get_meaningful_words(text):
    \"\"\"Get meaningful words from text (no stopwords, lemmatized).\"\"\"
    words = lemmatize_text(normalize_text(text)).split()
    return [w for w in words if w not in STOPWORDS and len(w) > 1]

def enforce_word_limit(name, limit=MAX_WORDS):
    \"\"\"Limit name to max words.\"\"\"
    words = name.split()
    if len(words) > limit:
        return ' '.join(words[:limit])
    return name

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    \"\"\"Load all three data sources.\"\"\"
    amazon = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amazon['source'] = 'amazon'
    
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb = fb[['category_path']]
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    
    google = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    google['source'] = 'google'
    
    # Combine
    df = pd.concat([amazon, fb, google], ignore_index=True)
    
    # Clean category_path
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    # Parse levels
    df['parts'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: [p.strip().strip('\"') for p in x.split(' > ')])
    df['raw_depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove paths that are strict prefixes of longer paths (per source).\"\"\"
    result_rows = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_df = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(source_df['category_path'].values)
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        keep_mask = ~source_df['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)
        result_rows.append(source_df[keep_mask])
    return pd.concat(result_rows, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=MAX_DEPTH):
    \"\"\"Truncate paths to max depth.\"\"\"
    def trunc(row):
        parts = row['parts'][:max_depth]
        return parts
    df['parts'] = df.apply(trunc, axis=1)
    df['depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    df['category_path'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p))
    return df

# ============================================================
# UNIFIED L1 MAPPING (manually curated for quality)
# ============================================================

# 15 unified L1 categories that cover all sources evenly
UNIFIED_L1_CATEGORIES = [
    'Electronics Technology',
    'Clothing Accessories',
    'Home Garden',
    'Sports Outdoors',
    'Health Beauty',
    'Food Beverages',
    'Automotive Vehicles',
    'Baby Toddler',
    'Pet Supplies',
    'Toys Games',
    'Office Supplies',
    'Books Media',
    'Arts Crafts Entertainment',
    'Industrial Hardware',
    'Musical Instruments',
]

# Map each source L1 to unified L1
SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED = {
    # Amazon mappings
    'electronics': 'Electronics Technology',
    'cell phones accessories': 'Electronics Technology',
    'appliances': 'Electronics Technology',
    'clothing shoes jewelry': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'home kitchen': 'Home Garden',
    'patio lawn garden': 'Home Garden',
    'sports outdoors': 'Sports Outdoors',
    'health household': 'Health Beauty',
    'beauty personal care': 'Health Beauty',
    'grocery gourmet food': 'Food Beverages',
    'automotive': 'Automotive Vehicles',
    'baby products': 'Baby Toddler',
    'pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'toys games': 'Toys Games',
    'office products': 'Office Supplies',
    'books': 'Books Media',
    'cds vinyl': 'Books Media',
    'movies tv': 'Books Media',
    'video games': 'Books Media',
    'arts crafts sewing': 'Arts Crafts Entertainment',
    'tools home improvement': 'Industrial Hardware',
    'industrial scientific': 'Industrial Hardware',
    'musical instruments': 'Musical Instruments',
    
    # Facebook mappings
    'sporting goods': 'Sports Outdoors',
    'home': 'Home Garden',
    'home improvement': 'Industrial Hardware',
    'clothing accessories': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'patio garden': 'Home Garden',
    'baby products fb': 'Baby Toddler',  # handled below
    'health beauty': 'Health Beauty',
    'jewelry watches': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'food beverages': 'Food Beverages',
    'travel luggage': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'office supplies': 'Office Supplies',
    'pet supplies fb': 'Pet Supplies',  # handled below
    'musical instruments fb': 'Musical Instruments',  # handled below
    'antiques collectibles': 'Arts Crafts Entertainment',
    'auto parts accessories': 'Automotive Vehicles',
    'arts crafts': 'Arts Crafts Entertainment',
    'media': 'Books Media',
    'other': 'Home Garden',
    'toys games fb': 'Toys Games',  # handled below
    'electronics fb': 'Electronics Technology',  # handled below
    
    # Google mappings
    'animals pet supplies': 'Pet Supplies',
    'apparel accessories': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'arts entertainment': 'Arts Crafts Entertainment',
    'baby toddler': 'Baby Toddler',
    'business industrial': 'Industrial Hardware',
    'cameras optics': 'Electronics Technology',
    'food beverages tobacco': 'Food Beverages',
    'furniture': 'Home Garden',
    'hardware': 'Industrial Hardware',
    'home garden': 'Home Garden',
    'luggage bags': 'Clothing Accessories',
    'mature': 'Health Beauty',
    'software': 'Electronics Technology',
    'sporting goods google': 'Sports Outdoors',  # handled below
    'vehicles parts': 'Automotive Vehicles',
    'religious ceremonial': 'Arts Crafts Entertainment',
    'toys games google': 'Toys Games',  # handled below
    'office supplies google': 'Office Supplies',  # handled below
    'media google': 'Books Media',  # handled below
    'electronics google': 'Electronics Technology',  # handled below
    'health beauty google': 'Health Beauty',  # handled below
}

def get_unified_l1(source, raw_l1):
    \"\"\"Map a source L1 category to unified L1.\"\"\"
    key = normalize_text(raw_l1)
    
    # Direct lookup
    if key in SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED:
        return SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED[key]
    
    # Try with source suffix for disambiguation
    key_with_source = key + ' ' + source[:2]
    if key_with_source in SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED:
        return SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED[key_with_source]
    
    # Fuzzy matching by word overlap
    key_words = set(key.split())
    best_match = None
    best_score = 0
    for mapped_key, unified in SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED.items():
        mapped_words = set(mapped_key.split())
        overlap = len(key_words & mapped_words)
        if overlap > best_score:
            best_score = overlap
            best_match = unified
    
    if best_match and best_score > 0:
        return best_match
    
    return 'Home Garden'  # fallback

# ============================================================
# HIERARCHICAL CLUSTERING BY WORD FREQUENCY
# ============================================================

def get_cluster_name_from_paths(paths, ancestor_names=None, max_words=3):
    \"\"\"
    Generate a representative name from the most frequent words in paths.
    Excludes words that appear in ancestor names.
    \"\"\"
    if ancestor_names is None:
        ancestor_names = []
    
    # Collect ancestor words to exclude
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        ancestor_words.update(get_meaningful_words(aname))
    
    # Count word frequencies across all paths
    word_counts = Counter()
    for path in paths:
        words = get_meaningful_words(path)
        # Use unique words per path to avoid bias
        unique_words = set(words)
        word_counts.update(unique_words)
    
    # Filter out ancestor words and stopwords
    filtered_counts = {w: c for w, c in word_counts.items() 
                       if w not in ancestor_words and w not in STOPWORDS and len(w) > 1}
    
    if not filtered_counts:
        # If all words overlap with ancestors, use less common words from paths
        # Try using the LAST segment words which are most specific
        last_seg_counts = Counter()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            last_part = parts[-1] if parts else path
            words = get_meaningful_words(last_part)
            unique_words = set(w for w in words if w not in ancestor_words)
            last_seg_counts.update(unique_words)
        
        if last_seg_counts:
            filtered_counts = dict(last_seg_counts)
        else:
            # Ultimate fallback: use \"General\" + a differentiator
            return 'Specialty Items'
    
    # Get top words
    top_words = sorted(filtered_counts.keys(), key=lambda w: -filtered_counts[w])[:max_words]
    
    # Sort alphabetically for consistency
    name = ' '.join(sorted(top_words))
    return title_case(sanitize_name(name))

def build_level_clusters(df, level_col, group_col, path_col='category_path', ancestor_cols=None):
    \"\"\"
    Build clusters at a given level by grouping on group_col and naming from paths.
    Returns a mapping from group values to cluster names.
    \"\"\"
    if ancestor_cols is None:
        ancestor_cols = []
    
    clusters = {}
    for group_val, group_df in df.groupby(group_col):
        paths = group_df[path_col].tolist()
        
        # Get ancestor names for this group
        ancestor_names = []
        if ancestor_cols and len(group_df) > 0:
            row = group_df.iloc[0]
            for ac in ancestor_cols:
                if ac in row.index and pd.notna(row[ac]):
                    ancestor_names.append(str(row[ac]))
        
        name = get_cluster_name_from_paths(paths, ancestor_names)
        clusters[group_val] = name
    
    return clusters

# ============================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE
# ============================================================

def build_taxonomy():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_depth(df)
    
    # Remove duplicates (same path, same source)
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # ---- LEVEL 1: Map to unified L1 ----
    print(\"Mapping L1 categories...\")
    df['raw_l1'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[0])
    df['unified_level_1'] = df.apply(lambda r: get_unified_l1(r['source'], r['raw_l1']), axis=1)
    
    # Verify L1 count
    l1_count = df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"  L1 categories: {l1_count}\")
    
    # ---- LEVEL 2: Cluster by source L1 within unified L1 ----
    print(\"Building L2 categories...\")
    df['raw_l2'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None)
    
    # For L2, group by (unified_l1, normalized raw_l2)
    df['l2_key'] = df.apply(lambda r: normalize_text(r['raw_l2']) if r['raw_l2'] else None, axis=1)
    
    # Build L2 names per L1 group
    l2_mapping = {}  # (unified_l1, l2_key) -> unified_l2_name
    
    for l1_name, l1_group in df[df['l2_key'].notna()].groupby('unified_level_1'):
        l2_keys = l1_group['l2_key'].unique()
        
        # Group similar L2 keys by word overlap
        l2_clusters = cluster_similar_keys(l2_keys, threshold=0.5)
        
        # Enforce children count limits
        l2_clusters = enforce_children_limits(l2_clusters, l1_group, 'l2_key')
        
        # Name each cluster
        for cluster_keys in l2_clusters:
            cluster_paths = l1_group[l1_group['l2_key'].isin(cluster_keys)]['category_path'].tolist()
            # Use the raw L2 values for naming
            raw_l2_values = l1_group[l1_group['l2_key'].isin(cluster_keys)]['raw_l2'].unique()
            
            name = get_representative_l2_name(raw_l2_values, [l1_name])
            
            for key in cluster_keys:
                l2_mapping[(l1_name, key)] = name
    
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(
        lambda r: l2_mapping.get((r['unified_level_1'], r['l2_key'])) if r['l2_key'] else None, 
        axis=1
    )
    
    # ---- LEVEL 3-5: Use source path segments with normalization ----
    print(\"Building L3-L5 categories...\")
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df['parts'].apply(
            lambda p: sanitize_and_title(p[level-1]) if len(p) >= level else None
        )
    
    # ---- POST-PROCESSING ----
    print(\"Post-processing: sanitization and constraint enforcement...\")
    
    # 1. Sanitize ALL level names
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_name(str(x)) if pd.notna(x) else None)
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: title_case(x) if x else None)
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: enforce_word_limit(x) if x else None)
    
    # 2. Parent word exclusion
    print(\"  Applying parent word exclusion...\")
    df = apply_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    # 3. Sibling distinctiveness
    print(\"  Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    # 4. Final sanitization pass (ensure no special chars after all transforms)
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: final_sanitize(x) if pd.notna(x) else None)
    
    # 5. Ensure hierarchy consistency: if level N is set, levels 1..N-1 must be set
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{level}']) and pd.isna(row[f'unified_level_{level-1}']):
                df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = None
    
    # ---- OUTPUT ----
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    
    # Full mapping
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth'] + [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    full_df = df[output_cols].copy()
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    
    # Hierarchy (generated FROM the final full mapping)
    level_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hierarchy = full_df[level_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(level_cols)
    hierarchy = hierarchy[hierarchy['unified_level_1'].notna()]
    hierarchy.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    
    print(f\"  Full mapping: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hierarchy)} rows\")
    print(\"Done"'!")
    
    return full_df, hierarchy


def sanitize_and_title(text):
    """Sanitize and title-case a raw category segment."""
    if not text:
        return None
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = title_case(text)
    return text


def cluster_similar_keys(keys, threshold=0.5):
    """Cluster similar L2 keys by word overlap."""
    keys = list(keys)
    clusters = []
    used = set()
    
    # Sort by key for determinism
    keys.sort()
    
    for key in keys:
        if key in used:
            continue
        cluster = [key]
        used.add(key)
        key_words = set(key.split())
        
        for other_key in keys:
            if other_key in used:
                continue
            other_words = set(other_key.split())
            if not key_words or not other_words:
                continue
            overlap = len(key_words & other_words) / min(len(key_words), len(other_words))
            if overlap >= threshold:
                cluster.append(other_key)
                used.add(other_key)
        
        clusters.append(cluster)
    
    return clusters


def enforce_children_limits(clusters, group_df, key_col):
    """Enforce MIN_CHILDREN <= children <= MAX_CHILDREN per parent."""
    if len(clusters) > MAX_CHILDREN:
        # Sort clusters by size (descending), merge smallest into closest
        clusters.sort(key=lambda c: -len(group_df[group_df[key_col].isin(c)]))
        while len(clusters) > MAX_CHILDREN:
            # Merge last two smallest
            smallest = clusters.pop()
            second_smallest = clusters.pop()
            clusters.append(smallest + second_smallest)
    
    if len(clusters) < MIN_CHILDREN and len(clusters) > 0:
        # Split largest cluster if possible
        pass  # Keep as is - some parents naturally have few children
    
    return clusters


def get_representative_l2_name(raw_values, ancestor_names):
    """Get a representative name for L2 from raw L2 values."""
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        ancestor_words.update(get_meaningful_words(aname))
    
    # Count words across all raw L2 values
    word_counts = Counter()
    for val in raw_values:
        if val:
            words = get_meaningful_words(str(val))
            unique_words = set(w for w in words if w not in ancestor_words)
            word_counts.update(unique_words)
    
    if not word_counts:
        # All words overlap with parent - use the raw values directly but pick most specific
        for val in raw_values:
            if val:
                words = get_meaningful_words(str(val))
                # Use words even if they overlap, but try to find ANY non-overlapping
                all_words = set(words)
                non_overlap = all_words - ancestor_words
                if non_overlap:
                    word_counts.update(non_overlap)
        
        if not word_counts:
            # True fallback: use a generic differentiator
            return '"'Specialty Items'
    
    top_words = sorted(word_counts.keys(), key=lambda w: -word_counts[w])[:3]
    name = ' '.join(sorted(top_words))
    return title_case(sanitize_name(name))


def apply_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Remove parent words from child category names at all levels.\"\"\"
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            val = row[col]
            if pd.isna(val) or not val:
                continue
            
            # Collect ancestor words
            ancestor_words = set()
            for prev_level in range(1, level):
                prev_col = f'unified_level_{prev_level}'
                prev_val = row[prev_col]
                if pd.notna(prev_val) and prev_val:
                    ancestor_words.update(w.lower() for w in str(prev_val).split())
            
            if not ancestor_words:
                continue
            
            # Remove ancestor words from current level name
            words = str(val).split()
            filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
            
            if filtered:
                df.at[idx, col] = ' '.join(filtered)
            else:
                # CRITICAL: Don't fall back to original"'!
                # Use the source path segment at this level to find alternative words
                parts = row['"'category_path'].split(' > ') if 'category_path' in row.index else []
                if len(parts) >= level:
                    source_segment = parts[level - 1]
                    source_words = get_meaningful_words(source_segment)
                    alt_words = [w for w in source_words if w not in ancestor_words]
                    if alt_words:
                        df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(alt_words[:3]))
                    else:
                        # Use lemmatized form or abbreviation
                        lemma_words = [basic_lemmatize(w.lower()) for w in str(val).split()]
                        alt_lemma = [w for w in lemma_words if w not in ancestor_words]
                        if alt_lemma:
                            df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(alt_lemma[:3]))
                        else:
                            # Generate a truly different name using position/specificity
                            df.at[idx, col] = title_case('Specialty ' + str(level))
                else:
                    df.at[idx, col] = title_case('Specialty ' + str(level))
    
    return df


def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        # Group by parent path
        groups = df[df[col].notna()].groupby(parent_cols)
        
        for parent_key, group in groups:
            siblings = group[col].unique()
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Check pairwise overlap
            sibling_words = {s: set(s.lower().split()) for s in siblings}
            
            # Find pairs with high overlap
            to_rename = set()
            for i, s1 in enumerate(siblings):
                for j, s2 in enumerate(siblings):
                    if i >= j or s1 == s2:
                        continue
                    w1 = sibling_words[s1]
                    w2 = sibling_words[s2]
                    if not w1 or not w2:
                        continue
                    overlap = len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        # Mark the smaller group for renaming
                        count1 = (group[col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (group[col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 <= count2:
                            to_rename.add(s1)
                        else:
                            to_rename.add(s2)
            
            # Rename overlapping siblings by adding a differentiator
            for old_name in to_rename:
                mask = (df[col] == old_name)
                for pc in parent_cols:
                    if isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
                        mask = mask & (df[pc] == parent_key[parent_cols.index(pc)])
                    else:
                        mask = mask & (df[pc] == parent_key)
                
                # Find differentiating words from source paths
                affected_paths = df.loc[mask, 'category_path'].tolist()
                parent_name_words = set()
                if isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
                    for pk in parent_key:
                        if pk and isinstance(pk, str):
                            parent_name_words.update(w.lower() for w in pk.split())
                elif parent_key and isinstance(parent_key, str):
                    parent_name_words.update(w.lower() for w in parent_key.split())
                
                old_words = set(old_name.lower().split())
                all_sibling_words = set()
                for s in siblings:
                    if s "'!= old_name:
                        all_sibling_words.update(s.lower().split())
                
                # Find unique words from source paths
                path_word_counts = Counter()
                for path in affected_paths:
                    for w in get_meaningful_words(path):
                        if w not in old_words and w not in parent_name_words and w not in all_sibling_words:
                            path_word_counts[w] += 1
                
                if path_word_counts:
                    diff_word = path_word_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
                    new_name = title_case(sanitize_name(old_name + '"' ' + diff_word))
                    new_name = enforce_word_limit(new_name)
                    df.loc[mask, col] = new_name
    
    return df


def final_sanitize(name):
    \"\"\"Final pass: ensure absolutely no special characters remain.\"\"\"
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    # Remove ANY non-alphanumeric-space character
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', '', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    if not name:
        return None
    name = title_case(name)
    name = enforce_word_limit(name)
    return name


if __name__ == '__main__':
    build_taxonomy()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping category taxonomies into a single 5-level hierarchy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
MAX_WORDS = 5
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.3

os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ============================================================
# TEXT NORMALIZATION
# ============================================================

STOPWORDS = {
    'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'or', 'with',
    'by', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its', 'other', 'all',
    'non', 'pre', 'vs'
}

def sanitize_name(name):
    \"\"\"Remove ALL special characters, keep only alphanumeric and spaces.\"\"\"
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    return name

def title_case(name):
    \"\"\"Title case a name.\"\"\"
    if not name:
        return name
    return ' '.join(w.capitalize() for w in name.split())

def normalize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Full normalization: lowercase, remove special chars, strip.\"\"\"
    if not text or not isinstance(text, str):
        return ''
    text = text.strip().strip('\"').strip()
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip().lower()
    return text

def basic_lemmatize(word):
    \"\"\"Basic lemmatization for common English plural patterns.\"\"\"
    w = word.lower()
    if w.endswith('ies') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'y'
    if w.endswith('ves') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'f'
    if w.endswith('ses') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-2]
    if w.endswith('s') and not w.endswith('ss') and len(w) > 3:
        return w[:-1]
    return w

def lemmatize_text(text):
    \"\"\"Lemmatize all words in text.\"\"\"
    return ' '.join(basic_lemmatize(w) for w in text.split())

def get_meaningful_words(text):
    \"\"\"Get meaningful words from text (no stopwords, lemmatized).\"\"\"
    words = lemmatize_text(normalize_text(text)).split()
    return [w for w in words if w not in STOPWORDS and len(w) > 1]

def enforce_word_limit(name, limit=MAX_WORDS):
    \"\"\"Limit name to max words.\"\"\"
    words = name.split()
    if len(words) > limit:
        return ' '.join(words[:limit])
    return name

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    \"\"\"Load all three data sources.\"\"\"
    amazon = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amazon['source'] = 'amazon'
    
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb = fb[['category_path']]
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    
    google = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    google['source'] = 'google'
    
    # Combine
    df = pd.concat([amazon, fb, google], ignore_index=True)
    
    # Clean category_path
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    
    # Parse levels
    df['parts'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: [p.strip().strip('\"') for p in x.split(' > ')])
    df['raw_depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    \"\"\"Remove paths that are strict prefixes of longer paths (per source).\"\"\"
    result_rows = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_df = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(source_df['category_path'].values)
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        keep_mask = ~source_df['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)
        result_rows.append(source_df[keep_mask])
    return pd.concat(result_rows, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=MAX_DEPTH):
    \"\"\"Truncate paths to max depth.\"\"\"
    def trunc(row):
        parts = row['parts'][:max_depth]
        return parts
    df['parts'] = df.apply(trunc, axis=1)
    df['depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    df['category_path'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p))
    return df

def get_unified_l1(source, raw_l1):
    \"\"\"Map a source L1 category to unified L1.\"\"\"
    key = normalize_text(raw_l1)
    
    # Direct lookup
    if key in SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED:
        return SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED[key]
    
    # Try with source suffix for disambiguation
    key_with_source = key + ' ' + source[:2]
    if key_with_source in SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED:
        return SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED[key_with_source]
    
    # Fuzzy matching by word overlap
    key_words = set(key.split())
    best_match = None
    best_score = 0
    for mapped_key, unified in SOURCE_L1_TO_UNIFIED.items():
        mapped_words = set(mapped_key.split())
        overlap = len(key_words & mapped_words)
        if overlap > best_score:
            best_score = overlap
            best_match = unified
    
    if best_match and best_score > 0:
        return best_match
    
    return 'Home Garden'  # fallback

# ============================================================
# HIERARCHICAL CLUSTERING BY WORD FREQUENCY
# ============================================================

def get_cluster_name_from_paths(paths, ancestor_names=None, max_words=3):
    \"\"\"
    Generate a representative name from the most frequent words in paths.
    Excludes words that appear in ancestor names.
    \"\"\"
    if ancestor_names is None:
        ancestor_names = []
    
    # Collect ancestor words to exclude
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        ancestor_words.update(get_meaningful_words(aname))
    
    # Count word frequencies across all paths
    word_counts = Counter()
    for path in paths:
        words = get_meaningful_words(path)
        # Use unique words per path to avoid bias
        unique_words = set(words)
        word_counts.update(unique_words)
    
    # Filter out ancestor words and stopwords
    filtered_counts = {w: c for w, c in word_counts.items() 
                       if w not in ancestor_words and w not in STOPWORDS and len(w) > 1}
    
    if not filtered_counts:
        # If all words overlap with ancestors, use less common words from paths
        # Try using the LAST segment words which are most specific
        last_seg_counts = Counter()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            last_part = parts[-1] if parts else path
            words = get_meaningful_words(last_part)
            unique_words = set(w for w in words if w not in ancestor_words)
            last_seg_counts.update(unique_words)
        
        if last_seg_counts:
            filtered_counts = dict(last_seg_counts)
        else:
            # Ultimate fallback: use \"General\" + a differentiator
            return 'Specialty Items'
    
    # Get top words
    top_words = sorted(filtered_counts.keys(), key=lambda w: -filtered_counts[w])[:max_words]
    
    # Sort alphabetically for consistency
    name = ' '.join(sorted(top_words))
    return title_case(sanitize_name(name))

def build_level_clusters(df, level_col, group_col, path_col='category_path', ancestor_cols=None):
    \"\"\"
    Build clusters at a given level by grouping on group_col and naming from paths.
    Returns a mapping from group values to cluster names.
    \"\"\"
    if ancestor_cols is None:
        ancestor_cols = []
    
    clusters = {}
    for group_val, group_df in df.groupby(group_col):
        paths = group_df[path_col].tolist()
        
        # Get ancestor names for this group
        ancestor_names = []
        if ancestor_cols and len(group_df) > 0:
            row = group_df.iloc[0]
            for ac in ancestor_cols:
                if ac in row.index and pd.notna(row[ac]):
                    ancestor_names.append(str(row[ac]))
        
        name = get_cluster_name_from_paths(paths, ancestor_names)
        clusters[group_val] = name
    
    return clusters

# ============================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE
# ============================================================

def build_taxonomy():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_depth(df)
    
    # Remove duplicates (same path, same source)
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # ---- LEVEL 1: Map to unified L1 ----
    print(\"Mapping L1 categories...\")
    df['raw_l1'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[0])
    df['unified_level_1'] = df.apply(lambda r: get_unified_l1(r['source'], r['raw_l1']), axis=1)
    
    # Verify L1 count
    l1_count = df['unified_level_1'].nunique()
    print(f\"  L1 categories: {l1_count}\")
    
    # ---- LEVEL 2: Cluster by source L1 within unified L1 ----
    print(\"Building L2 categories...\")
    df['raw_l2'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None)
    
    # For L2, group by (unified_l1, normalized raw_l2)
    df['l2_key'] = df.apply(lambda r: normalize_text(r['raw_l2']) if r['raw_l2'] else None, axis=1)
    
    # Build L2 names per L1 group
    l2_mapping = {}  # (unified_l1, l2_key) -> unified_l2_name
    
    for l1_name, l1_group in df[df['l2_key'].notna()].groupby('unified_level_1'):
        l2_keys = l1_group['l2_key'].unique()
        
        # Group similar L2 keys by word overlap
        l2_clusters = cluster_similar_keys(l2_keys, threshold=0.5)
        
        # Enforce children count limits
        l2_clusters = enforce_children_limits(l2_clusters, l1_group, 'l2_key')
        
        # Name each cluster
        for cluster_keys in l2_clusters:
            cluster_paths = l1_group[l1_group['l2_key'].isin(cluster_keys)]['category_path'].tolist()
            # Use the raw L2 values for naming
            raw_l2_values = l1_group[l1_group['l2_key'].isin(cluster_keys)]['raw_l2'].unique()
            
            name = get_representative_l2_name(raw_l2_values, [l1_name])
            
            for key in cluster_keys:
                l2_mapping[(l1_name, key)] = name
    
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(
        lambda r: l2_mapping.get((r['unified_level_1'], r['l2_key'])) if r['l2_key'] else None, 
        axis=1
    )
    
    # ---- LEVEL 3-5: Use source path segments with normalization ----
    print(\"Building L3-L5 categories...\")
    for level in [3, 4, 5]:
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df['parts'].apply(
            lambda p: sanitize_and_title(p[level-1]) if len(p) >= level else None
        )
    
    # ---- POST-PROCESSING ----
    print(\"Post-processing: sanitization and constraint enforcement...\")
    
    # 1. Sanitize ALL level names
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: sanitize_name(str(x)) if pd.notna(x) else None)
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: title_case(x) if x else None)
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: enforce_word_limit(x) if x else None)
    
    # 2. Parent word exclusion
    print(\"  Applying parent word exclusion...\")
    df = apply_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    # 3. Sibling distinctiveness
    print(\"  Enforcing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    # 4. Final sanitization pass (ensure no special chars after all transforms)
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: final_sanitize(x) if pd.notna(x) else None)
    
    # 5. Ensure hierarchy consistency: if level N is set, levels 1..N-1 must be set
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            if pd.notna(row[f'unified_level_{level}']) and pd.isna(row[f'unified_level_{level-1}']):
                df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = None
    
    # ---- OUTPUT ----
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    
    # Full mapping
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth'] + [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    full_df = df[output_cols].copy()
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    
    # Hierarchy (generated FROM the final full mapping)
    level_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hierarchy = full_df[level_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(level_cols)
    hierarchy = hierarchy[hierarchy['unified_level_1'].notna()]
    hierarchy.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    
    print(f\"  Full mapping: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hierarchy)} rows\")
    print(\"Done"'!")
    
    return full_df, hierarchy


def sanitize_and_title(text):
    """Sanitize and title-case a raw category segment."""
    if not text:
        return None
    text = sanitize_name(text)
    text = title_case(text)
    return text


def cluster_similar_keys(keys, threshold=0.5):
    """Cluster similar L2 keys by word overlap."""
    keys = list(keys)
    clusters = []
    used = set()
    
    # Sort by key for determinism
    keys.sort()
    
    for key in keys:
        if key in used:
            continue
        cluster = [key]
        used.add(key)
        key_words = set(key.split())
        
        for other_key in keys:
            if other_key in used:
                continue
            other_words = set(other_key.split())
            if not key_words or not other_words:
                continue
            overlap = len(key_words & other_words) / min(len(key_words), len(other_words))
            if overlap >= threshold:
                cluster.append(other_key)
                used.add(other_key)
        
        clusters.append(cluster)
    
    return clusters


def enforce_children_limits(clusters, group_df, key_col):
    """Enforce MIN_CHILDREN <= children <= MAX_CHILDREN per parent."""
    if len(clusters) > MAX_CHILDREN:
        # Sort clusters by size (descending), merge smallest into closest
        clusters.sort(key=lambda c: -len(group_df[group_df[key_col].isin(c)]))
        while len(clusters) > MAX_CHILDREN:
            # Merge last two smallest
            smallest = clusters.pop()
            second_smallest = clusters.pop()
            clusters.append(smallest + second_smallest)
    
    if len(clusters) < MIN_CHILDREN and len(clusters) > 0:
        # Split largest cluster if possible
        pass  # Keep as is - some parents naturally have few children
    
    return clusters


def get_representative_l2_name(raw_values, ancestor_names):
    """Get a representative name for L2 from raw L2 values."""
    ancestor_words = set()
    for aname in ancestor_names:
        ancestor_words.update(get_meaningful_words(aname))
    
    # Count words across all raw L2 values
    word_counts = Counter()
    for val in raw_values:
        if val:
            words = get_meaningful_words(str(val))
            unique_words = set(w for w in words if w not in ancestor_words)
            word_counts.update(unique_words)
    
    if not word_counts:
        # All words overlap with parent - use the raw values directly but pick most specific
        for val in raw_values:
            if val:
                words = get_meaningful_words(str(val))
                # Use words even if they overlap, but try to find ANY non-overlapping
                all_words = set(words)
                non_overlap = all_words - ancestor_words
                if non_overlap:
                    word_counts.update(non_overlap)
        
        if not word_counts:
            # True fallback: use a generic differentiator
            return '"'Specialty Items'
    
    top_words = sorted(word_counts.keys(), key=lambda w: -word_counts[w])[:3]
    name = ' '.join(sorted(top_words))
    return title_case(sanitize_name(name))


def apply_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Remove parent words from child category names at all levels.\"\"\"
    for idx, row in df.iterrows():
        for level in range(2, 6):
            col = f'unified_level_{level}'
            val = row[col]
            if pd.isna(val) or not val:
                continue
            
            # Collect ancestor words
            ancestor_words = set()
            for prev_level in range(1, level):
                prev_col = f'unified_level_{prev_level}'
                prev_val = row[prev_col]
                if pd.notna(prev_val) and prev_val:
                    ancestor_words.update(w.lower() for w in str(prev_val).split())
            
            if not ancestor_words:
                continue
            
            # Remove ancestor words from current level name
            words = str(val).split()
            filtered = [w for w in words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
            
            if filtered:
                df.at[idx, col] = ' '.join(filtered)
            else:
                # CRITICAL: Don't fall back to original"'!
                # Use the source path segment at this level to find alternative words
                parts = row['"'category_path'].split(' > ') if 'category_path' in row.index else []
                if len(parts) >= level:
                    source_segment = parts[level - 1]
                    source_words = get_meaningful_words(source_segment)
                    alt_words = [w for w in source_words if w not in ancestor_words]
                    if alt_words:
                        df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(alt_words[:3]))
                    else:
                        # Use lemmatized form or abbreviation
                        lemma_words = [basic_lemmatize(w.lower()) for w in str(val).split()]
                        alt_lemma = [w for w in lemma_words if w not in ancestor_words]
                        if alt_lemma:
                            df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(alt_lemma[:3]))
                        else:
                            # Generate a truly different name using position/specificity
                            df.at[idx, col] = title_case('Specialty ' + str(level))
                else:
                    df.at[idx, col] = title_case('Specialty ' + str(level))
    
    return df


def enforce_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Ensure sibling categories have < 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        # Group by parent path
        groups = df[df[col].notna()].groupby(parent_cols)
        
        for parent_key, group in groups:
            siblings = group[col].unique()
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Check pairwise overlap
            sibling_words = {s: set(s.lower().split()) for s in siblings}
            
            # Find pairs with high overlap
            to_rename = set()
            for i, s1 in enumerate(siblings):
                for j, s2 in enumerate(siblings):
                    if i >= j or s1 == s2:
                        continue
                    w1 = sibling_words[s1]
                    w2 = sibling_words[s2]
                    if not w1 or not w2:
                        continue
                    overlap = len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))
                    if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                        # Mark the smaller group for renaming
                        count1 = (group[col] == s1).sum()
                        count2 = (group[col] == s2).sum()
                        if count1 <= count2:
                            to_rename.add(s1)
                        else:
                            to_rename.add(s2)
            
            # Rename overlapping siblings by adding a differentiator
            for old_name in to_rename:
                mask = (df[col] == old_name)
                for pc in parent_cols:
                    if isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
                        mask = mask & (df[pc] == parent_key[parent_cols.index(pc)])
                    else:
                        mask = mask & (df[pc] == parent_key)
                
                # Find differentiating words from source paths
                affected_paths = df.loc[mask, 'category_path'].tolist()
                parent_name_words = set()
                if isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
                    for pk in parent_key:
                        if pk and isinstance(pk, str):
                            parent_name_words.update(w.lower() for w in pk.split())
                elif parent_key and isinstance(parent_key, str):
                    parent_name_words.update(w.lower() for w in parent_key.split())
                
                old_words = set(old_name.lower().split())
                all_sibling_words = set()
                for s in siblings:
                    if s "'!= old_name:
                        all_sibling_words.update(s.lower().split())
                
                # Find unique words from source paths
                path_word_counts = Counter()
                for path in affected_paths:
                    for w in get_meaningful_words(path):
                        if w not in old_words and w not in parent_name_words and w not in all_sibling_words:
                            path_word_counts[w] += 1
                
                if path_word_counts:
                    diff_word = path_word_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
                    new_name = title_case(sanitize_name(old_name + '"' ' + diff_word))
                    new_name = enforce_word_limit(new_name)
                    df.loc[mask, col] = new_name
    
    return df


def final_sanitize(name):
    \"\"\"Final pass: ensure absolutely no special characters remain.\"\"\"
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    # Remove ANY non-alphanumeric-space character
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', '', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    if not name:
        return None
    name = title_case(name)
    name = enforce_word_limit(name)
    return name


if __name__ == '__main__':
    build_taxonomy()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping category taxonomies into a single 5-level hierarchy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
MAX_WORDS = 5
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.3

os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ============================================================
# TEXT NORMALIZATION
# ============================================================

STOPWORDS = {
    'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'or', 'with',
    'by', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its', 'other', 'all',
    'non', 'pre', 'vs', 's'
}

def sanitize_name(name):
    \"\"\"Remove ALL special characters, keep only alphanumeric and spaces.\"\"\"
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    return name

def title_case(name):
    if not name:
        return name
    return ' '.join(w.capitalize() for w in name.split())

def normalize_text(text):
    if not text or not isinstance(text, str):
        return ''
    text = text.strip().strip('\"').strip()
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip().lower()
    return text

def basic_lemmatize(word):
    w = word.lower()
    if w.endswith('ies') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'y'
    if w.endswith('ves') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'f'
    if w.endswith('ses') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-2]
    if w.endswith('s') and not w.endswith('ss') and len(w) > 3:
        return w[:-1]
    return w

def lemmatize_text(text):
    return ' '.join(basic_lemmatize(w) for w in text.split())

def get_meaningful_words(text):
    words = lemmatize_text(normalize_text(text)).split()
    return [w for w in words if w not in STOPWORDS and len(w) > 1]

def enforce_word_limit(name, limit=MAX_WORDS):
    words = name.split()
    if len(words) > limit:
        return ' '.join(words[:limit])
    return name

def final_sanitize(name):
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', '', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    if not name:
        return None
    name = title_case(name)
    name = enforce_word_limit(name)
    return name

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    amazon = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amazon['source'] = 'amazon'
    
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb = fb[['category_path']]
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    
    google = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    google['source'] = 'google'
    
    df = pd.concat([amazon, fb, google], ignore_index=True)
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    df['parts'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: [p.strip().strip('\"') for p in x.split(' > ')])
    df['raw_depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    result_rows = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_df = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(source_df['category_path'].values)
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        keep_mask = ~source_df['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)
        result_rows.append(source_df[keep_mask])
    return pd.concat(result_rows, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=MAX_DEPTH):
    df['parts'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[:max_depth])
    df['depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    df['category_path'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p))
    return df

# ============================================================
# UNIFIED L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

UNIFIED_L1 = [

SOURCE_L1_MAP = {}

# Build mapping from normalized source L1 -> unified L1
_raw_map = {

for unified, sources in _raw_map.items():
    for src in sources:
        SOURCE_L1_MAP[src] = unified

def get_unified_l1(source, raw_l1):
    key = normalize_text(raw_l1)
    if key in SOURCE_L1_MAP:
        return SOURCE_L1_MAP[key]
    # Fuzzy match
    key_words = set(key.split())
    best_match = None
    best_score = 0
    for mapped_key, unified in SOURCE_L1_MAP.items():
        mapped_words = set(mapped_key.split())
        overlap = len(key_words & mapped_words)
        if overlap > best_score:
            best_score = overlap
            best_match = unified
    if best_match and best_score > 0:
        return best_match
    return 'Home Garden'

# ============================================================
# CLUSTERING HELPERS
# ============================================================

def cluster_names_by_word_overlap(names, threshold=0.5):
    \"\"\"Cluster a list of names by word overlap.\"\"\"
    names = list(set(names))
    names.sort()
    clusters = []
    used = set()
    
    for name in names:
        if name in used:
            continue
        cluster = [name]
        used.add(name)
        name_words = set(normalize_text(name).split())
        
        for other in names:
            if other in used:
                continue
            other_words = set(normalize_text(other).split())
            if not name_words or not other_words:
                continue
            overlap = len(name_words & other_words) / min(len(name_words), len(other_words))
            if overlap >= threshold:
                cluster.append(other)
                used.add(other)
        
        clusters.append(cluster)
    
    return clusters

def merge_clusters_to_limit(clusters, max_count=MAX_CHILDREN):
    \"\"\"Merge smallest clusters until we have <= max_count.\"\"\"
    while len(clusters) > max_count:
        # Sort by size ascending
        clusters.sort(key=lambda c: len(c))
        # Merge two smallest
        smallest = clusters.pop(0)
        second = clusters.pop(0)
        clusters.append(smallest + second)
    return clusters

def name_cluster(members, ancestor_words, max_words=3):
    \"\"\"Name a cluster from its member names, excluding ancestor words.\"\"\"
    word_counts = Counter()
    for member in members:
        words = set(get_meaningful_words(member))
        words -= ancestor_words
        word_counts.update(words)
    
    if not word_counts:
        # Try raw words without lemmatization
        for member in members:
            raw_words = set(normalize_text(member).split()) - STOPWORDS
            raw_words -= ancestor_words
            word_counts.update(raw_words)
    
    if not word_counts:
        # Use the first member's most distinctive word
        for member in members:
            words = normalize_text(member).split()
            for w in words:
                if w not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 2:
                    return title_case(w)
        return 'Specialty'
    
    top = sorted(word_counts.keys(), key=lambda w: -word_counts[w])[:max_words]
    return title_case(' '.join(sorted(top)))

def get_ancestor_word_set(ancestor_names):
    \"\"\"Get set of all words from ancestor names.\"\"\"
    words = set()
    for name in ancestor_names:
        if name and isinstance(name, str):
            words.update(w.lower() for w in sanitize_name(name).split() if len(w) > 1)
            words.update(get_meaningful_words(name))
    return words

# ============================================================
# BUILD L2 WITH PROPER CLUSTERING
# ============================================================

def build_l2_mapping(df):
    \"\"\"Build L2 mapping by clustering raw L2 values within each L1.\"\"\"
    df['raw_l2'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[1] if len(p) > 1 else None)
    df['norm_l2'] = df['raw_l2'].apply(lambda x: normalize_text(x) if x else None)
    
    l2_map = {}  # (unified_l1, norm_l2) -> unified_l2_name
    
    for l1_name, l1_group in df[df['norm_l2'].notna()].groupby('unified_level_1'):
        raw_l2_values = l1_group['raw_l2'].unique()
        ancestor_words = get_ancestor_word_set([l1_name])
        
        # Cluster similar L2 values
        clusters = cluster_names_by_word_overlap(list(raw_l2_values), threshold=0.5)
        
        # Enforce max children
        clusters = merge_clusters_to_limit(clusters, MAX_CHILDREN)
        
        # Name each cluster
        used_names = set()
        for cluster_members in clusters:
            name = name_cluster(cluster_members, ancestor_words)
            
            # Ensure uniqueness
            base_name = name
            counter = 2
            while name in used_names:
                name = f\"{base_name} {counter}\"
                counter += 1
            used_names.add(name)
            
            # Map all normalized L2 values in this cluster
            for member in cluster_members:
                norm = normalize_text(member)
                l2_map[(l1_name, norm)] = name
    
    return l2_map

# ============================================================
# BUILD L3-L5 WITH CLUSTERING
# ============================================================

def build_level_mapping(df, level):
    \"\"\"Build mapping for level 3-5 by clustering within parent groups.\"\"\"
    col = f'unified_level_{level}'
    parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
    
    # Get raw segment at this level
    raw_col = f'raw_l{level}'
    df[raw_col] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[level-1] if len(p) >= level else None)
    
    level_map = {}  # (parent_tuple, norm_raw) -> unified_name
    
    mask = df[raw_col].notna()
    for col_name in parent_cols:
        mask = mask & df[col_name].notna()
    
    if mask.sum() == 0:
        return level_map
    
    # Group by parent path
    groups = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)
    
    for parent_key, group in groups:
        if not isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
            parent_key = (parent_key,)
        
        raw_values = group[raw_col].unique()
        ancestor_names = list(parent_key)
        ancestor_words = get_ancestor_word_set(ancestor_names)
        
        if len(raw_values) <= MAX_CHILDREN:
            # Just name each one directly
            used_names = set()
            for raw_val in raw_values:
                # Remove ancestor words from the raw value
                words = [w for w in sanitize_name(str(raw_val)).split() 
                         if w.lower() not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 1 and w.lower() not in STOPWORDS]
                if words:
                    name = title_case(' '.join(words[:MAX_WORDS]))
                else:
                    # Find alternative from the raw value
                    all_words = sanitize_name(str(raw_val)).split()
                    # Use lemmatized forms that differ from ancestors
                    alt = [basic_lemmatize(w.lower()) for w in all_words 
                           if basic_lemmatize(w.lower()) not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 1]
                    if alt:
                        name = title_case(' '.join(alt[:MAX_WORDS]))
                    else:
                        name = title_case(sanitize_name(str(raw_val)).split()[0] if sanitize_name(str(raw_val)).split() else 'Item') + ' Type'
                
                name = enforce_word_limit(name)
                
                # Ensure uniqueness among siblings
                base_name = name
                counter = 2
                while name in used_names:
                    name = f\"{base_name} {counter}\"
                    name = enforce_word_limit(name)
                    counter += 1
                used_names.add(name)
                
                norm = normalize_text(raw_val)
                level_map[(parent_key, norm)] = name
        else:
            # Too many children - cluster them
            clusters = cluster_names_by_word_overlap(list(raw_values), threshold=0.4)
            clusters = merge_clusters_to_limit(clusters, MAX_CHILDREN)
            
            used_names = set()
            for cluster_members in clusters:
                name = name_cluster(cluster_members, ancestor_words)
                name = enforce_word_limit(name)
                
                base_name = name
                counter = 2
                while name in used_names:
                    name = f\"{base_name} {counter}\"
                    name = enforce_word_limit(name)
                    counter += 1
                used_names.add(name)
                
                for member in cluster_members:
                    norm = normalize_text(member)
                    level_map[(parent_key, norm)] = name
    
    return level_map

# ============================================================
# SIBLING DISTINCTIVENESS ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

def fix_sibling_distinctiveness(df):
    \"\"\"Fix sibling pairs with >= 30% word overlap.\"\"\"
    for level in range(2, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        parent_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, level)]
        
        mask = df[col].notna()
        for pc in parent_cols:
            mask = mask & df[pc].notna()
        
        if mask.sum() == 0:
            continue
        
        groups = df[mask].groupby(parent_cols)
        
        for parent_key, group in groups:
            siblings = group[col].unique()
            if len(siblings) <= 1:
                continue
            
            # Build word sets
            sibling_words = {}
            for s in siblings:
                sibling_words[s] = set(s.lower().split())
            
            # Find overlapping pairs and merge smaller into larger
            merged = True
            while merged:
                merged = False
                siblings = list(set(df.loc[mask & self_parent_match(df, parent_cols, parent_key), col].unique()))
                if len(siblings) <= 1:
                    break
                
                sibling_words = {s: set(s.lower().split()) for s in siblings}
                
                for i, s1 in enumerate(siblings):
                    for j, s2 in enumerate(siblings):
                        if i >= j or s1 == s2:
                            continue
                        w1 = sibling_words.get(s1, set())
                        w2 = sibling_words.get(s2, set())
                        if not w1 or not w2:
                            continue
                        overlap = len(w1 & w2) / min(len(w1), len(w2))
                        if overlap >= SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD:
                            # Merge: rename smaller group to match larger
                            count1 = (group[col] == s1).sum() if s1 in group[col].values else 0
                            count2 = (group[col] == s2).sum() if s2 in group[col].values else 0
                            
                            if count1 >= count2:
                                larger, smaller = s1, s2
                            else:
                                larger, smaller = s2, s1
                            
                            # Merge smaller into larger
                            parent_mask = mask.copy()
                            if isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
                                for pi, pc in enumerate(parent_cols):
                                    parent_mask = parent_mask & (df[pc] == parent_key[pi])
                            else:
                                parent_mask = parent_mask & (df[parent_cols[0]] == parent_key)
                            
                            df.loc[parent_mask & (df[col] == smaller), col] = larger
                            merged = True
                            break
                    if merged:
                        break
    
    return df

def self_parent_match(df, parent_cols, parent_key):
    \"\"\"Create mask for rows matching parent key.\"\"\"
    mask = pd.Series(True, index=df.index)
    if isinstance(parent_key, tuple):
        for pi, pc in enumerate(parent_cols):
            mask = mask & (df[pc] == parent_key[pi])
    else:
        mask = mask & (df[parent_cols[0]] == parent_key)
    return mask

# ============================================================
# PARENT WORD EXCLUSION (THOROUGH)
# ============================================================

def fix_parent_word_exclusion(df):
    \"\"\"Remove ALL parent words from child names. Never fall back to original.\"\"\"
    max_iterations = 3
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        violations_fixed = 0
        for idx in df.index:
            for level in range(2, 6):
                col = f'unified_level_{level}'
                val = df.at[idx, col]
                if pd.isna(val) or not val:
                    continue
                
                # Collect ALL ancestor words
                ancestor_words = set()
                for prev in range(1, level):
                    prev_val = df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{prev}']
                    if pd.notna(prev_val) and prev_val:
                        for w in str(prev_val).split():
                            ancestor_words.add(w.lower())
                
                if not ancestor_words:
                    continue
                
                current_words = str(val).split()
                filtered = [w for w in current_words if w.lower() not in ancestor_words]
                
                if len(filtered) < len(current_words):
                    violations_fixed += 1
                    if filtered:
                        df.at[idx, col] = ' '.join(filtered)
                    else:
                        # ALL words overlap - need alternative
                        # Strategy 1: use source path segment words not in ancestors
                        parts = df.at[idx, 'category_path'].split(' > ')
                        if len(parts) >= level:
                            seg = parts[level - 1]
                            seg_words = [w for w in sanitize_name(seg).split() 
                                        if w.lower() not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 1 
                                        and w.lower() not in STOPWORDS]
                            if seg_words:
                                df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(seg_words[:3]))
                                continue
                        
                        # Strategy 2: use lemmatized forms
                        lemma = [basic_lemmatize(w.lower()) for w in current_words]
                        alt = [w for w in lemma if w not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 1]
                        if alt:
                            df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(alt[:3]))
                            continue
                        
                        # Strategy 3: use next level's words as differentiator
                        if len(parts) > level:
                            next_seg = parts[level]
                            next_words = [w for w in sanitize_name(next_seg).split()
                                         if w.lower() not in ancestor_words and len(w) > 1]
                            if next_words:
                                df.at[idx, col] = title_case(' '.join(next_words[:3]))
                                continue
                        
                        # Strategy 4: use a generic unique name
                        df.at[idx, col] = f'Category {level} {idx % 100}'
        
        if violations_fixed == 0:
            break
    
    return df

# ============================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE
# ============================================================

def build_taxonomy():
    print(\"Loading data...\")
    df = load_data()
    print(f\"  Total records: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Removing prefix paths...\")
    df = remove_prefix_paths(df)
    print(f\"  After prefix removal: {len(df)}\")
    
    print(\"Truncating to depth 5...\")
    df = truncate_depth(df)
    
    # Remove duplicates (same path, same source)
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['source', 'category_path'])
    print(f\"  After dedup: {len(df)}\")
    
    # ---- LEVEL 1 ----
    print(\"Mapping L1 categories...\")
    df['raw_l1'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[0])
    df['unified_level_1'] = df.apply(lambda r: get_unified_l1(r['source'], r['raw_l1']), axis=1)
    print(f\"  L1 categories: {df['unified_level_1'].nunique()}\")
    
    # ---- LEVEL 2 ----
    print(\"Building L2 categories...\")
    l2_map = build_l2_mapping(df)
    df['norm_l2'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: normalize_text(p[1]) if len(p) > 1 else None)
    df['unified_level_2'] = df.apply(
        lambda r: l2_map.get((r['unified_level_1'], r['norm_l2'])) if r['norm_l2'] else None,
        axis=1
    )
    
    # ---- LEVEL 3 ----
    print(\"Building L3 categories...\")
    l3_map = build_level_mapping(df, 3)
    df['raw_l3'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[2] if len(p) >= 3 else None)
    df['norm_l3'] = df['raw_l3'].apply(lambda x: normalize_text(x) if x else None)
    
    def get_l3(row):
        if not row['norm_l3'] or pd.isna(row.get('unified_level_2')):
            return None
        parent_key = (row['unified_level_1'], row['unified_level_2'])
        return l3_map.get((parent_key, row['norm_l3']))
    
    df['unified_level_3'] = df.apply(get_l3, axis=1)
    
    # ---- LEVEL 4 ----
    print(\"Building L4 categories...\")
    l4_map = build_level_mapping(df, 4)
    df['raw_l4'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[3] if len(p) >= 4 else None)
    df['norm_l4'] = df['raw_l4'].apply(lambda x: normalize_text(x) if x else None)
    
    def get_l4(row):
        if not row['norm_l4'] or pd.isna(row.get('unified_level_3')):
            return None
        parent_key = (row['unified_level_1'], row['unified_level_2'], row['unified_level_3'])
        return l4_map.get((parent_key, row['norm_l4']))
    
    df['unified_level_4'] = df.apply(get_l4, axis=1)
    
    # ---- LEVEL 5 ----
    print(\"Building L5 categories...\")
    l5_map = build_level_mapping(df, 5)
    df['raw_l5'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[4] if len(p) >= 5 else None)
    df['norm_l5'] = df['raw_l5'].apply(lambda x: normalize_text(x) if x else None)
    
    def get_l5(row):
        if not row['norm_l5'] or pd.isna(row.get('unified_level_4')):
            return None
        parent_key = (row['unified_level_1'], row['unified_level_2'], row['unified_level_3'], row['unified_level_4'])
        return l5_map.get((parent_key, row['norm_l5']))
    
    df['unified_level_5'] = df.apply(get_l5, axis=1)
    
    # ---- POST-PROCESSING ----
    print(\"Post-processing...\")
    
    # 1. Final sanitize all names
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: final_sanitize(x) if pd.notna(x) else None)
    
    # 2. Fix parent word exclusion (multiple passes)
    print(\"  Fixing parent word exclusion...\")
    df = fix_parent_word_exclusion(df)
    
    # 3. Final sanitize again after parent word removal
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: final_sanitize(x) if pd.notna(x) else None)
    
    # 4. Fix sibling distinctiveness
    print(\"  Fixing sibling distinctiveness...\")
    df = fix_sibling_distinctiveness(df)
    
    # 5. Final sanitize one more time
    for level in range(1, 6):
        col = f'unified_level_{level}'
        df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: final_sanitize(x) if pd.notna(x) else None)
    
    # 6. Ensure hierarchy consistency
    for idx in df.index:
        for level in range(2, 6):
            if pd.notna(df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}']) and pd.isna(df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level-1}']):
                df.at[idx, f'unified_level_{level}'] = None
    
    # ---- OUTPUT ----
    print(\"Generating output files...\")
    
    output_cols = ['source', 'category_path', 'depth'] + [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    full_df = df[output_cols].copy()
    full_df.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_full.csv', index=False)
    
    # Hierarchy from final full mapping
    level_cols = [f'unified_level_{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    hierarchy = full_df[level_cols].drop_duplicates().sort_values(level_cols)
    hierarchy = hierarchy[hierarchy['unified_level_1'].notna()]
    hierarchy.to_csv(f'{OUTPUT_DIR}/unified_taxonomy_hierarchy.csv', index=False)
    
    print(f\"  Full mapping: {len(full_df)} rows\")
    print(f\"  Hierarchy: {len(hierarchy)} rows\")
    print(\"Done"'!")
    
    return full_df, hierarchy


if __name__ == '"'__main__':
    build_taxonomy()

#"'!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Unified Product Category Taxonomy Builder
Merges Amazon, Facebook, and Google Shopping category taxonomies into a single 5-level hierarchy.
"""

import pandas as pd
import re
import os
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
DATA_DIR = '"'/root/data'
OUTPUT_DIR = '/root/output'
MAX_DEPTH = 5
MIN_CHILDREN = 3
MAX_CHILDREN = 20
MAX_WORDS = 5
SIBLING_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.3

os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# ============================================================
# TEXT NORMALIZATION
# ============================================================

STOPWORDS = {
    'and', 'the', 'of', 'for', 'in', 'to', 'a', 'an', 'or', 'with',
    'by', 'on', 'at', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'it', 'its', 'other', 'all',
    'non', 'pre', 'vs', 's'
}

def sanitize_name(name):
    \"\"\"Remove ALL special characters, keep only alphanumeric and spaces.\"\"\"
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    return name

def title_case(name):
    if not name:
        return name
    return ' '.join(w.capitalize() for w in name.split())

def normalize_text(text):
    if not text or not isinstance(text, str):
        return ''
    text = text.strip().strip('\"').strip()
    text = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', ' ', text)
    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip().lower()
    return text

def basic_lemmatize(word):
    w = word.lower()
    if w.endswith('ies') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'y'
    if w.endswith('ves') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-3] + 'f'
    if w.endswith('ses') and len(w) > 4:
        return w[:-2]
    if w.endswith('s') and not w.endswith('ss') and len(w) > 3:
        return w[:-1]
    return w

def lemmatize_text(text):
    return ' '.join(basic_lemmatize(w) for w in text.split())

def get_meaningful_words(text):
    words = lemmatize_text(normalize_text(text)).split()
    return [w for w in words if w not in STOPWORDS and len(w) > 1]

def enforce_word_limit(name, limit=MAX_WORDS):
    words = name.split()
    if len(words) > limit:
        return ' '.join(words[:limit])
    return name

def final_sanitize(name):
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return name
    name = re.sub(r'["'^a-zA-Z0-9'"\\s]', '', name)
    name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', name).strip()
    if not name:
        return None
    name = title_case(name)
    name = enforce_word_limit(name)
    return name

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING
# ============================================================

def load_data():
    amazon = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/amazon_product_categories.csv')
    amazon['source'] = 'amazon'
    
    fb = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/fb_product_categories.csv')
    fb = fb[['category_path']]
    fb['source'] = 'facebook'
    
    google = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/google_shopping_product_categories.csv')
    google['source'] = 'google'
    
    df = pd.concat([amazon, fb, google], ignore_index=True)
    df['category_path'] = df['category_path'].str.strip().str.strip('\"')
    df['parts'] = df['category_path'].apply(lambda x: [p.strip().strip('\"') for p in x.split(' > ')])
    df['raw_depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    return df

def remove_prefix_paths(df):
    result_rows = []
    for source in df['source'].unique():
        source_df = df[df['source'] == source].copy()
        paths = set(source_df['category_path'].values)
        prefixes_to_remove = set()
        for path in paths:
            parts = path.split(' > ')
            for i in range(1, len(parts)):
                prefix = ' > '.join(parts[:i])
                if prefix in paths:
                    prefixes_to_remove.add(prefix)
        keep_mask = ~source_df['category_path'].isin(prefixes_to_remove)
        result_rows.append(source_df[keep_mask])
    return pd.concat(result_rows, ignore_index=True)

def truncate_depth(df, max_depth=MAX_DEPTH):
    df['parts'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: p[:max_depth])
    df['depth'] = df['parts'].apply(len)
    df['category_path'] = df['parts'].apply(lambda p: ' > '.join(p))
    return df

# ============================================================
# UNIFIED L1 MAPPING
# ============================================================

UNIFIED_L1 = [

SOURCE_L1_MAP = {}

# Build mapping from normalized source L1 -> unified L1
_raw_map = {
